🎓 Continue Learning

This comprehensive guide covers 95% of EC2 use cases. For advanced topics like API/CLI references, integration patterns, and compliance details, visit the AWS documentation.

⌨️ API & CLI Quick Reference

🚀 Most Used EC2 Commands
# ========================================
# INSTANCE MANAGEMENT
# ========================================

# List all instances
aws ec2 describe-instances

# List running instances only
aws ec2 describe-instances \
    --filters "Name=instance-state-name,Values=running"

# List instances by tag
aws ec2 describe-instances \
    --filters "Name=tag:Project,Values=anihpj"

# Launch new instance
aws ec2 run-instances \
    --image-id ami-0c7217cdde317cfec \
    --instance-type t3.medium \
    --key-name anihpj-keypair \
    --security-group-ids sg-0abc123 \
    --subnet-id subnet-0abc123 \
    --tag-specifications 'ResourceType=instance,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=anihpj-web}]'

# Stop instance
aws ec2 stop-instances --instance-ids i-0abc123

# Start instance
aws ec2 start-instances --instance-ids i-0abc123

# Reboot instance
aws ec2 reboot-instances --instance-ids i-0abc123

# Terminate instance
aws ec2 terminate-instances --instance-ids i-0abc123

# Change instance type (must be stopped first)
aws ec2 modify-instance-attribute \
    --instance-id i-0abc123 \
    --instance-type m5.large

# ========================================
# EBS VOLUME MANAGEMENT
# ========================================

# Create volume
aws ec2 create-volume \
    --availability-zone us-east-1a \
    --size 50 \
    --volume-type gp3 \
    --encrypted

# Attach volume to instance
aws ec2 attach-volume \
    --volume-id vol-0abc123 \
    --instance-id i-0abc123 \
    --device /dev/sdf

# Detach volume
aws ec2 detach-volume --volume-id vol-0abc123

# Delete volume
aws ec2 delete-volume --volume-id vol-0abc123

# Create snapshot
aws ec2 create-snapshot \
    --volume-id vol-0abc123 \
    --description "anihpj backup 2024-01-15"

# Copy snapshot to another region
aws ec2 copy-snapshot \
    --source-region us-east-1 \
    --source-snapshot-id snap-0abc123 \
    --destination-region us-west-2

# ========================================
# SECURITY GROUPS
# ========================================

# Create security group
aws ec2 create-security-group \
    --group-name anihpj-web-sg \
    --description "Security group for anihpj web servers" \
    --vpc-id vpc-0abc123

# Add inbound rule (HTTP)
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id sg-0abc123 \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port 80 \
    --cidr 0.0.0.0/0

# Add inbound rule (HTTPS)
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id sg-0abc123 \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port 443 \
    --cidr 0.0.0.0/0

# Add inbound rule (SSH from specific IP)
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id sg-0abc123 \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port 22 \
    --cidr 203.0.113.0/24

# Remove rule
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id sg-0abc123 \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port 22 \
    --cidr 0.0.0.0/0

# ========================================
# ELASTIC IP
# ========================================

# Allocate Elastic IP
aws ec2 allocate-address --domain vpc

# Associate with instance
aws ec2 associate-address \
    --instance-id i-0abc123 \
    --allocation-id eipalloc-0abc123

# Disassociate from instance
aws ec2 disassociate-address \
    --association-id eipassoc-0abc123

# Release Elastic IP
aws ec2 release-address --allocation-id eipalloc-0abc123

# ========================================
# KEY PAIRS
# ========================================

# Create key pair (saves private key to file)
aws ec2 create-key-pair \
    --key-name anihpj-keypair-2024 \
    --query 'KeyMaterial' \
    --output text > anihpj-keypair-2024.pem
chmod 400 anihpj-keypair-2024.pem

# List key pairs
aws ec2 describe-key-pairs

# Delete key pair
aws ec2 delete-key-pair --key-name anihpj-old-keypair

# ========================================
# AMI MANAGEMENT
# ========================================

# Create AMI from instance
aws ec2 create-image \
    --instance-id i-0abc123 \
    --name "anihpj-golden-image-2024-01-15" \
    --description "Configured anihpj Django application" \
    --no-reboot

# Copy AMI to another region
aws ec2 copy-image \
    --source-region us-east-1 \
    --source-image-id ami-0abc123 \
    --region us-west-2 \
    --name "anihpj-golden-image-west"

# Delete AMI
aws ec2 deregister-image --image-id ami-0abc123

# ========================================
# MONITORING & LOGS
# ========================================

# Get instance console output
aws ec2 get-console-output --instance-id i-0abc123

# Get instance screenshot
aws ec2 get-console-screenshot --instance-id i-0abc123

# Describe instance status
aws ec2 describe-instance-status --instance-ids i-0abc123

# Enable detailed monitoring
aws ec2 monitor-instances --instance-ids i-0abc123

# Disable detailed monitoring
aws ec2 unmonitor-instances --instance-ids i-0abc123
🐍 Python Boto3 Examples
# anihpj/management/commands/manage_ec2.py
import boto3
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Manage EC2 instances for anihpj'
    
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.ec2 = boto3.client('ec2', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.ec2_resource = boto3.resource('ec2', region_name='us-east-1')
    
    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument('action', type=str, 
                          help='Action: list|start|stop|launch')
        parser.add_argument('--instance-id', type=str, 
                          help='Instance ID for start/stop actions')
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        action = options['action']
        
        if action == 'list':
            self.list_instances()
        elif action == 'start':
            self.start_instance(options['instance_id'])
        elif action == 'stop':
            self.stop_instance(options['instance_id'])
        elif action == 'launch':
            self.launch_instance()
    
    def list_instances(self):
        """List all anihpj instances"""
        response = self.ec2.describe_instances(
            Filters=[
                {'Name': 'tag:Project', 'Values': ['anihpj']},
                {'Name': 'instance-state-name', 
                 'Values': ['running', 'stopped', 'stopping', 'pending']}
            ]
        )
        
        for reservation in response['Reservations']:
            for instance in reservation['Instances']:
                name = next((tag['Value'] for tag in instance.get('Tags', []) 
                           if tag['Key'] == 'Name'), 'N/A')
                
                self.stdout.write(
                    f"ID: {instance['InstanceId']}\n"
                    f"Name: {name}\n"
                    f"Type: {instance['InstanceType']}\n"
                    f"State: {instance['State']['Name']}\n"
                    f"IP: {instance.get('PublicIpAddress', 'N/A')}\n"
                    f"Launch Time: {instance['LaunchTime']}\n"
                    f"---"
                )
    
    def start_instance(self, instance_id):
        """Start stopped instance"""
        if not instance_id:
            self.stderr.write("Error: --instance-id required")
            return
        
        try:
            self.ec2.start_instances(InstanceIds=[instance_id])
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.SUCCESS(f"Started instance {instance_id}")
            )
        except Exception as e:
            self.stderr.write(f"Error starting instance: {str(e)}")
    
    def stop_instance(self, instance_id):
        """Stop running instance"""
        if not instance_id:
            self.stderr.write("Error: --instance-id required")
            return
        
        try:
            self.ec2.stop_instances(InstanceIds=[instance_id])
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.SUCCESS(f"Stopped instance {instance_id}")
            )
        except Exception as e:
            self.stderr.write(f"Error stopping instance: {str(e)}")
    
    def launch_instance(self):
        """Launch new anihpj web server"""
        try:
            instances = self.ec2_resource.create_instances(
                ImageId='ami-0c7217cdde317cfec',  # Amazon Linux 2023
                InstanceType='t3.medium',
                KeyName='anihpj-keypair',
                SecurityGroupIds=['sg-0abc123'],
                SubnetId='subnet-0abc123',
                MinCount=1,
                MaxCount=1,
                TagSpecifications=[
                    {
                        'ResourceType': 'instance',
                        'Tags': [
                            {'Key': 'Name', 'Value': 'anihpj-web-auto'},
                            {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'anihpj'},
                            {'Key': 'Environment', 'Value': 'production'},
                            {'Key': 'ManagedBy', 'Value': 'django-command'}
                        ]
                    }
                ],
                IamInstanceProfile={'Name': 'anihpj-ec2-profile'},
                UserData=self.get_user_data()
            )
            
            instance_id = instances[0].id
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.SUCCESS(f"Launched instance {instance_id}")
            )
            
            # Wait for instance to be running
            self.stdout.write("Waiting for instance to start...")
            instances[0].wait_until_running()
            instances[0].reload()
            
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.SUCCESS(
                    f"Instance running at {instances[0].public_ip_address}"
                )
            )
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.stderr.write(f"Error launching instance: {str(e)}")
    
    def get_user_data(self):
        """Get user data script for instance initialization"""
        return '''#!/bin/bash
        yum update -y
        yum install -y python3 python3-pip git
        pip3 install django gunicorn psycopg2-binary boto3
        
        # Clone anihpj repository
        cd /opt
        git clone https://github.com/your-org/anihpj.git
        cd anihpj
        
        # Install Python dependencies
        pip3 install -r requirements.txt
        
        # Set environment variables
        export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://..."
        export SECRET_KEY="..."
        
        # Run migrations
        python3 manage.py migrate
        
        # Collect static files
        python3 manage.py collectstatic --noinput
        
        # Start Gunicorn
        gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:8000 --workers 4 --daemon
        '''

# Usage:
# python manage.py manage_ec2 list
# python manage.py manage_ec2 start --instance-id i-0abc123
# python manage.py manage_ec2 stop --instance-id i-0abc123
# python manage.py manage_ec2 launch

🔗 Integration with AWS Services

📦 S3 Integration for Static Files
# settings.py - Configure Django to use S3 for static/media files
import os

# S3 Configuration
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID = os.environ.get('AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID')
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = os.environ.get('AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY')
AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME = 'anihpj-static'
AWS_S3_REGION_NAME = 'us-east-1'
AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN = f'{AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME}.s3.amazonaws.com'

# Static files (CSS, JavaScript, Images)
STATIC_URL = f'https://{AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN}/static/'
STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'storages.backends.s3boto3.S3Boto3Storage'

# Media files (User uploads)
MEDIA_URL = f'https://{AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN}/media/'
DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = 'storages.backends.s3boto3.S3Boto3Storage'

# S3 object parameters
AWS_S3_OBJECT_PARAMETERS = {
    'CacheControl': 'max-age=86400',  # 1 day cache
}

# Security
AWS_DEFAULT_ACL = 'public-read'
AWS_S3_FILE_OVERWRITE = False
AWS_QUERYSTRING_AUTH = False  # Don't add auth to URLs
🗄️ RDS Database Integration
# settings.py - Configure Django to use RDS PostgreSQL
import os
import dj_database_url

# Parse DATABASE_URL environment variable
DATABASES = {
    'default': dj_database_url.config(
        default=os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL'),
        conn_max_age=600,
        conn_health_checks=True,
        ssl_require=True
    )
}

# Alternative explicit configuration
DATABASES = {
    'default': {
        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql',
        'NAME': 'anihpj',
        'USER': 'anihpj_admin',
        'PASSWORD': os.environ.get('DB_PASSWORD'),
        'HOST': 'anihpj-db.abc123.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com',
        'PORT': '5432',
        'OPTIONS': {
            'sslmode': 'require',
            'connect_timeout': 10,
        },
        'CONN_MAX_AGE': 600,  # Connection pooling
    }
}

# Read replica for reporting queries
DATABASES['read_replica'] = {
    'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql',
    'NAME': 'anihpj',
    'USER': 'anihpj_readonly',
    'PASSWORD': os.environ.get('DB_PASSWORD_READONLY'),
    'HOST': 'anihpj-db-replica.abc123.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com',
    'PORT': '5432',
    'OPTIONS': {'sslmode': 'require'},
}

# Database router for read/write splitting
class DatabaseRouter:
    def db_for_read(self, model, **hints):
        """Send read queries to replica"""
        return 'read_replica'
    
    def db_for_write(self, model, **hints):
        """Send write queries to primary"""
        return 'default'

DATABASE_ROUTERS = ['anihpj.settings.DatabaseRouter']
📧 SES Email Integration
# settings.py - Configure Django to use SES for emails
import os

EMAIL_BACKEND = 'django_ses.SESBackend'
AWS_SES_REGION_NAME = 'us-east-1'
AWS_SES_REGION_ENDPOINT = 'email.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'

DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL = 'noreply@anihpj.com'
SERVER_EMAIL = 'admin@anihpj.com'

# jobpost/views.py - Send job application emails
from django.core.mail import send_mail

def apply_job(request, job_id):
    job = JobPost.objects.get(id=job_id)
    applicant_email = request.POST.get('email')
    resume = request.FILES.get('resume')
    
    # Send confirmation to applicant
    send_mail(
        subject=f'Application Received: {job.title}',
        message=f'Thank you for applying to {job.title} at {job.author.company}.',
        from_email='noreply@anihpj.com',
        recipient_list=[applicant_email],
        fail_silently=False,
    )
    
    # Notify employer
    send_mail(
        subject=f'New Application: {job.title}',
        message=f'New application from {applicant_email}',
        from_email='noreply@anihpj.com',
        recipient_list=[job.author.user.email],
        fail_silently=False,
    )
🔍 CloudWatch Logs Integration
# Install CloudWatch logs agent
# settings.py - Configure Django logging to CloudWatch
import boto3
from botocore.config import Config

LOGGING = {
    'version': 1,
    'disable_existing_loggers': False,
    'handlers': {
        'cloudwatch': {
            'level': 'INFO',
            'class': 'watchtower.CloudWatchLogHandler',
            'boto3_client': boto3.client(
                'logs',
                region_name='us-east-1',
                config=Config(retries={'max_attempts': 10, 'mode': 'standard'})
            ),
            'log_group_name': '/aws/ec2/anihpj',
            'log_stream_name': '{instance_id}-django',
        },
        'console': {
            'class': 'logging.StreamHandler',
        },
    },
    'root': {
        'handlers': ['console', 'cloudwatch'],
        'level': 'INFO',
    },
    'loggers': {
        'django': {
            'handlers': ['console', 'cloudwatch'],
            'level': 'INFO',
            'propagate': False,
        },
    },
}

🚚 Migration to EC2

🏠 On-Premises to AWS EC2
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         MIGRATION STRATEGY: ON-PREMISES → AWS EC2                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│  Phase 1: Assessment & Planning (2-4 weeks)                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ☐ Inventory applications and dependencies                  │ │
│  │  ☐ Assess performance requirements                          │ │
│  │  ☐ Calculate costs (TCO comparison)                         │ │
│  │  ☐ Identify dependencies and integration points             │ │
│  │  ☐ Plan network architecture (VPC, subnets)                 │ │
│  │  ☐ Define security requirements                             │ │
│  │  ☐ Create migration timeline                                │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                  │
│  Phase 2: Proof of Concept (2-3 weeks)                          │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ☐ Set up AWS account and IAM                               │ │
│  │  ☐ Create VPC and networking                                │ │
│  │  ☐ Deploy test anihpj instance                              │ │
│  │  ☐ Migrate sample database                                  │ │
│  │  ☐ Test application functionality                           │ │
│  │  ☐ Performance testing                                      │ │
│  │  ☐ Validate backup/recovery procedures                      │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                  │
│  Phase 3: Migration Execution (1-2 weeks)                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ☐ Set up production environment                            │ │
│  │  ☐ Configure Auto Scaling and Load Balancing                │ │
│  │  ☐ Migrate database (use AWS DMS if needed)                 │ │
│  │  ☐ Sync static files to S3                                  │ │
│  │  ☐ Deploy application code                                  │ │
│  │  ☐ Configure monitoring and alerts                          │ │
│  │  ☐ Update DNS records                                       │ │
│  │  ☐ Run parallel for cutover period                          │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                  │
│  Phase 4: Post-Migration (Ongoing)                              │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  ☐ Monitor performance and costs                            │ │
│  │  ☐ Optimize resource utilization                            │ │
│  │  ☐ Implement cost optimization strategies                   │ │
│  │  ☐ Train team on AWS operations                             │ │
│  │  ☐ Document architecture and procedures                     │ │
│  │  ☐ Decommission on-premises infrastructure                  │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                  │
│  Migration Tools:                                                │
│  • AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) - Lift-and-shift     │
│  • AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) - Database migration    │
│  • AWS DataSync - Large data transfers                          │
│  • AWS Snow Family - Petabyte-scale data transfer               │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🛡️ Compliance & Governance

📜 Compliance Certifications
AWS EC2 Compliance & Certifications:

✅ SOC 1/2/3 - Service Organization Control
✅ PCI DSS Level 1 - Payment Card Industry
✅ ISO 27001/27017/27018 - Information Security
✅ HIPAA - Healthcare Insurance Portability
✅ GDPR - General Data Protection Regulation
✅ FedRAMP - Federal Risk Authorization Program
✅ FISMA - Federal Information Security Management
✅ IRAP - Australian Government (Certified)
✅ MTCS - Multi-Tier Cloud Security (Singapore)
✅ C5 - Cloud Computing Compliance Controls (Germany)

For anihpj Job Portal:
• GDPR compliance required if serving EU users
• Data encryption at rest (EBS) and in transit (HTTPS)
• Regular security audits and penetration testing
• User data access controls and logging (CloudTrail)
• Right to erasure (delete user data on request)
🔐 Security Best Practices Summary
AWS Well-Architected Framework - Security Pillar:

1. Identity & Access Management
   ✓ Use IAM roles (never store credentials on EC2)
   ✓ Enable MFA for root and admin accounts
   ✓ Apply least privilege principle
   ✓ Rotate credentials regularly

2. Detective Controls
   ✓ Enable CloudTrail for audit logging
   ✓ Enable VPC Flow Logs
   ✓ Use GuardDuty for threat detection
   ✓ Set up CloudWatch alarms

3. Infrastructure Protection
   ✓ Use Security Groups and NACLs
   ✓ Deploy in private subnets where possible
   ✓ Use AWS WAF for web application firewall
   ✓ Enable DDoS protection (Shield)

4. Data Protection
   ✓ Encrypt data at rest (EBS, RDS, S3)
   ✓ Encrypt data in transit (HTTPS, TLS)
   ✓ Use AWS KMS for key management
   ✓ Regular backups and snapshots

5. Incident Response
   ✓ Documented incident response plan
   ✓ Automated remediation where possible
   ✓ Regular security drills
   ✓ Post-incident analysis and improvements

📖 Glossary

Key EC2 Terms:

AMI (Amazon Machine Image)
    Pre-configured template for EC2 instances containing OS and software.

Auto Scaling
    Automatically adjust the number of EC2 instances based on demand.

Availability Zone (AZ)
    Isolated data center within an AWS Region. Each Region has multiple AZs.

EBS (Elastic Block Store)
    Persistent block storage for EC2 instances (like a hard drive).

EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud)
    Virtual servers in the AWS cloud.

Elastic IP
    Static IPv4 address designed for dynamic cloud computing.

IAM (Identity and Access Management)
    Service for managing access to AWS resources.

Instance
    A virtual server in Amazon EC2.

Instance Store
    Temporary block storage physically attached to host computer.

Launch Template
    Configuration template for launching EC2 instances.

Reserved Instance
    Purchase option with discounted hourly rate for 1 or 3-year commitment.

Security Group
    Virtual firewall controlling inbound and outbound traffic to instances.

Snapshot
    Point-in-time backup of an EBS volume.

Spot Instance
    Spare EC2 capacity available at up to 90% discount.

User Data
    Scripts or commands that run when an instance starts.

VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)
    Isolated virtual network where you launch AWS resources.

📝 Changelog & Version History

Amazon EC2 Major Updates (Recent):

2024
─────
• January: M7i instances (Intel Sapphire Rapids)
• February: Graviton4-based R8g instances
• March: Improved EBS performance (gp3 up to 16,000 IOPS)
• April: EC2 capacity reservations in Wavelength Zones

2023
─────
• Q4: C7gn instances (network-optimized Graviton3)
• Q3: Hpc7g instances (HPC workloads with Graviton3)
• Q2: M7g instances (general-purpose Graviton3)
• Q1: Trn1n instances (100GB networking for ML training)

2022
─────
• Q4: C7g instances (compute-optimized Graviton3)
• Q3: G5g instances (GPU with Graviton2)
• Q2: I4i instances (storage-optimized with NVMe SSD)
• Q1: X2idn, X2iedn instances (memory-optimized)

Instance Type Evolution:
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ T1 (2010) → T2 (2014) → T3 (2018) → T3a (2019)          │
│ M1 (2006) → M3 (2012) → M4 (2015) → M5 (2017) → M7g (2023) │
│ C1 (2008) → C3 (2013) → C4 (2015) → C5 (2017) → C7g (2022) │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Key Feature Additions:
• 2023: EC2 Instance Connect Endpoint (secure access without public IPs)
• 2022: Nitro Enclaves (isolated compute environments)
• 2021: Mac instances (macOS on EC2)
• 2020: Graviton2 processors (ARM-based, better price/performance)
• 2019: Inf1 instances (ML inference with AWS Inferentia chips)
• 2018: Elastic Fabric Adapter (EFA) for HPC workloads

For anihpj Job Portal:
• Recommended: Use latest generation (M7g, T3, C7g)
• Benefits: Better performance, lower cost, modern features
• Migration: Easy to change instance types (stop → modify → start)
🎉 Congratulations! You've completed the comprehensive Amazon EC2 guide for the anihpj Django Job Portal. This documentation covers everything from basic concepts to advanced deployment strategies, security best practices, and real-world integration patterns. Keep this guide handy as your go-to reference for EC2 operations!

Elastic Load Balancing (ELB)

Category: Networking & Content Delivery  |  Use Case: Traffic Distribution & High Availability  |  Pricing: Hourly + Data Processing  |  Last Updated: November 2025  |  Documentation: AWS ELB Docs

Automatically distribute incoming application traffic across multiple targets such as EC2 instances, containers, and IP addresses ensuring high availability, fault tolerance, and scalability for your anihpj Django job portal.

📋 Overview & Introduction

Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) automatically distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets, such as EC2 instances, containers, IP addresses, and Lambda functions, in one or more Availability Zones. It increases the availability and fault tolerance of your applications.

🎯 Key Benefits
  • High Availability: Distribute traffic across multiple AZs automatically
  • Auto Scaling: Seamlessly integrate with EC2 Auto Scaling
  • Health Checks: Automatic health monitoring and routing only to healthy targets
  • Security: Integrated with VPC, SSL/TLS termination, and WAF
  • Flexible: Multiple load balancer types for different use cases
  • Managed Service: AWS handles scaling, patching, and maintenance
🚀 Quick Start: Application Load Balancer for anihpj in 5 Minutes
1 Create Target Group: Register your EC2 instances running Django
2 Create ALB: aws elbv2 create-load-balancer --name anihpj-alb
3 Configure Listener: HTTP (80) → HTTPS (443) redirect
4 Add SSL Certificate: Attach ACM certificate for HTTPS
5 Update Django: Set ALLOWED_HOSTS to ALB DNS name
💡 When to Use ELB for anihpj: Essential when you have multiple Django instances running across different AZs. The ALB distributes job search traffic, handles SSL termination, performs health checks, and automatically removes unhealthy instances from rotation. Perfect for high-traffic job portals requiring zero downtime deployments.
⚖️ ELB vs Other Load Balancers
Component Purpose anihpj Usage
🌐 ELB Traffic distribution Distribute job portal traffic
🖥️ EC2 Compute instances Django application servers
📊 Auto Scaling Dynamic capacity Scale based on traffic
🌍 Route 53 DNS routing anihpj.com → ALB
🔒 ACM SSL certificates HTTPS encryption
🛡️ WAF Web firewall Protect from attacks
🏗️ Architecture Diagram
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        ELASTIC LOAD BALANCING ARCHITECTURE                               │
│                         (anihpj Django Job Portal with ALB)                              │
│                                                                                          │
│  Internet Users (Job Seekers & Employers)                                               │
│  │                                                                                       │
│  ▼                                                                                       │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  🌍 Route 53 DNS                                                                    │ │
│  │  anihpj.com → ALB DNS (anihpj-alb-123456789.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com)          │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                        │                                                 │
│                                        ▼                                                 │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  🌐 APPLICATION LOAD BALANCER (anihpj-alb)                                         │ │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │  │  Listeners:                                                                   │ │ │
│  │  │  • Port 80 (HTTP)  → Redirect to HTTPS                                       │ │ │
│  │  │  • Port 443 (HTTPS) → Forward to Target Group                                │ │ │
│  │  │                                                                               │ │ │
│  │  │  Features:                                                                    │ │ │
│  │  │  ✓ SSL/TLS Termination (ACM Certificate)                                     │ │ │
│  │  │  ✓ Path-based routing (/api/* → API servers)                                │ │ │
│  │  │  ✓ Health checks every 30 seconds                                            │ │ │
│  │  │  ✓ Sticky sessions (for user sessions)                                       │ │ │
│  │  │  ✓ WAF integration (DDoS protection)                                         │ │ │
│  │  │  ✓ Access logging to S3                                                      │ │ │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                        │                                                 │
│                                        ▼                                                 │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  🎯 TARGET GROUP: anihpj-web-tg                                                    │ │
│  │  Protocol: HTTP | Port: 8000 | Health Check: /health/                             │ │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                  │                              │                                        │
│     ┌────────────┴────────────┐    ┌───────────┴─────────────┐                         │
│     ▼                         ▼    ▼                         ▼                         │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                    AMAZON VPC (10.0.0.0/16)                                      │   │
│  │                                                                                  │   │
│  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────┐    ┌───────────────────────────────────┐ │   │
│  │  │  Availability Zone A              │    │  Availability Zone B              │ │   │
│  │  │  (us-east-1a)                     │    │  (us-east-1b)                     │ │   │
│  │  │                                   │    │                                   │ │   │
│  │  │  Private Subnet: 10.0.11.0/24     │    │  Private Subnet: 10.0.12.0/24     │ │   │
│  │  │                                   │    │                                   │ │   │
│  │  │  ┌─────────────────────────────┐  │    │  ┌─────────────────────────────┐  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ EC2: anihpj-web-1           │  │    │  │ EC2: anihpj-web-2           │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ ┌─────────────────────────┐ │  │    │  │ ┌─────────────────────────┐ │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ │ Django App (Gunicorn)   │ │  │    │  │ │ Django App (Gunicorn)   │ │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ │ Port: 8000              │ │  │    │  │ │ Port: 8000              │ │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ │ • JobPost views         │ │  │    │  │ │ • JobPost views         │ │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ │ • Search API            │ │  │    │  │ │ • Search API            │ │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ │ • Application forms     │ │  │    │  │ │ • Application forms     │ │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ └─────────────────────────┘ │  │    │  │ └─────────────────────────┘ │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ Status: ✅ Healthy          │  │    │  │ Status: ✅ Healthy          │  │ │   │
│  │  │  └─────────────────────────────┘  │    │  └─────────────────────────────┘  │ │   │
│  │  │                                   │    │                                   │ │   │
│  │  │  ┌─────────────────────────────┐  │    │  ┌─────────────────────────────┐  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ EC2: anihpj-web-3           │  │    │  │ EC2: anihpj-web-4           │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ Status: ❌ Unhealthy         │  │    │  │ Status: ✅ Healthy          │  │ │   │
│  │  │  │ (Removed from rotation)     │  │    │  │                             │  │ │   │
│  │  │  └─────────────────────────────┘  │    │  └─────────────────────────────┘  │ │   │
│  │  └───────────────────────────────────┘    └───────────────────────────────────┘ │   │
│  │                                                                                  │   │
│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │   │
│  │  │  Data Tier (Shared Across AZs)                                             │ │   │
│  │  │  • RDS PostgreSQL (Multi-AZ)                                               │ │   │
│  │  │  • ElastiCache Redis (Cluster mode)                                        │ │   │
│  │  │  • S3 (Resume storage)                                                     │ │   │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                                          │
│  📊 Traffic Flow:                                                                        │
│  1. User requests anihpj.com → Route 53 resolves to ALB                                │
│  2. ALB terminates SSL, checks health of targets                                       │
│  3. Routes request to healthy EC2 instance (round-robin or least connections)          │
│  4. Django processes request, queries RDS/ElastiCache                                  │
│  5. Response → ALB → User                                                              │
│                                                                                          │
│  💰 Monthly Cost Estimate:                                                              │
│  • ALB: ~$22 (730 hours × $0.0225/hr + data processing)                                │
│  • 4 EC2 t3.medium: ~$120                                                               │
│  • RDS: ~$50                                                                            │
│  • Total: ~$192/month                                                                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

� 2. Core Concepts & Load Balancer Types ▼

Understanding the different types of load balancers and choosing the right one for your anihpj application.

🔍 Which Load Balancer Should anihpj Use?
Type OSI Layer Best For anihpj Use Case Price
🌐 ALB Layer 7 (HTTP/HTTPS) Web apps, microservices ✅ RECOMMENDED for Django $$
⚡ NLB Layer 4 (TCP/UDP) High performance, low latency WebSocket connections, real-time $$
🛡️ GWLB Layer 3 (IP) Firewall, IDS/IPS appliances Security appliances (advanced) $$$
⚠️ CLB Layer 4 & 7 Legacy (deprecated) ❌ Not recommended $

💡 For anihpj: Use Application Load Balancer (ALB) for HTTP/HTTPS traffic with path-based routing, SSL termination, and sticky sessions for Django applications.

2.1 Application Load Balancer (ALB)

Best choice for HTTP/HTTPS web applications. Operates at Layer 7 (Application Layer) with advanced routing capabilities.

🎯 ALB Key Features for anihpj
  • Path-Based Routing: Route /api/* to API servers, /admin/* to admin instances
  • Host-Based Routing: Route api.anihpj.com vs www.anihpj.com to different targets
  • HTTP/2 & WebSocket: Modern protocol support for real-time job notifications
  • SSL/TLS Termination: Offload SSL processing from Django servers
  • Sticky Sessions: Route returning users to same instance (important for Django sessions)
  • Lambda Targets: Mix EC2 instances with serverless functions
🏗️ ALB Architecture with Path-Based Routing
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    APPLICATION LOAD BALANCER (ALB)                            │
│                         anihpj.com (Django Job Portal)                        │
│                                                                               │
│  Listener: HTTPS (443)                                                       │
│  SSL Certificate: *.anihpj.com (from ACM)                                    │
│                                                                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  ROUTING RULES (evaluated in priority order)                           │  │
│  │                                                                         │  │
│  │  Priority 1: /api/*                                                    │  │
│  │  ├─→ Condition: Path pattern = /api/*                                  │  │
│  │  └─→ Action: Forward to → anihpj-api-tg                               │  │
│  │      ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐                  │  │
│  │      │  🎯 API Target Group                         │                  │  │
│  │      │  • EC2: anihpj-api-1 (DRF API endpoints)    │                  │  │
│  │      │  • EC2: anihpj-api-2                         │                  │  │
│  │      │  Health: GET /api/health/                    │                  │  │
│  │      └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘                  │  │
│  │                                                                         │  │
│  │  Priority 2: /admin/*                                                  │  │
│  │  ├─→ Condition: Path pattern = /admin/*                                │  │
│  │  └─→ Action: Forward to → anihpj-admin-tg                             │  │
│  │      ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐                  │  │
│  │      │  🎯 Admin Target Group                       │                  │  │
│  │      │  • EC2: anihpj-admin-1 (Django admin)       │                  │  │
│  │      │  Health: GET /admin/login/                   │                  │  │
│  │      └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘                  │  │
│  │                                                                         │  │
│  │  Priority 3: /static/*, /media/*                                       │  │
│  │  ├─→ Condition: Path pattern = /static/* OR /media/*                   │  │
│  │  └─→ Action: Return fixed response (CloudFront handles static files)  │  │
│  │                                                                         │  │
│  │  Priority 4: Default (/)                                               │  │
│  │  ├─→ Condition: Default (matches all)                                  │  │
│  │  └─→ Action: Forward to → anihpj-web-tg                               │  │
│  │      ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐                  │  │
│  │      │  🎯 Web Target Group                         │                  │  │
│  │      │  • EC2: anihpj-web-1 (Main Django app)      │                  │  │
│  │      │  • EC2: anihpj-web-2                         │                  │  │
│  │      │  • EC2: anihpj-web-3                         │                  │  │
│  │      │  • EC2: anihpj-web-4                         │                  │  │
│  │      │  Health: GET /health/                        │                  │  │
│  │      └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘                  │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                               │
│  Examples:                                                                    │
│  • https://anihpj.com/jobs/search/      → anihpj-web-tg (Priority 4)        │
│  • https://anihpj.com/api/jobs/         → anihpj-api-tg (Priority 1)        │
│  • https://anihpj.com/admin/jobpost/    → anihpj-admin-tg (Priority 2)      │
│  • https://anihpj.com/static/css/app.css → Fixed response (Priority 3)      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
📝 Create ALB with AWS CLI
# 1. Create Application Load Balancer
aws elbv2 create-load-balancer \
    --name anihpj-alb \
    --subnets subnet-0abc123 subnet-0def456 \
    --security-groups sg-0web123 \
    --scheme internet-facing \
    --type application \
    --ip-address-type ipv4 \
    --tags Key=Application,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production

# 2. Create Target Group for Web Servers
aws elbv2 create-target-group \
    --name anihpj-web-tg \
    --protocol HTTP \
    --port 8000 \
    --vpc-id vpc-0xyz789 \
    --health-check-enabled \
    --health-check-protocol HTTP \
    --health-check-path /health/ \
    --health-check-interval-seconds 30 \
    --health-check-timeout-seconds 5 \
    --healthy-threshold-count 2 \
    --unhealthy-threshold-count 3 \
    --matcher HttpCode=200

# 3. Register EC2 Instances to Target Group
aws elbv2 register-targets \
    --target-group-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/abc123 \
    --targets Id=i-0abc123,Port=8000 Id=i-0def456,Port=8000

# 4. Create HTTPS Listener
aws elbv2 create-listener \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc123 \
    --protocol HTTPS \
    --port 443 \
    --certificates CertificateArn=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789:certificate/abc-123 \
    --default-actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/abc123

# 5. Create HTTP to HTTPS Redirect Listener
aws elbv2 create-listener \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc123 \
    --protocol HTTP \
    --port 80 \
    --default-actions Type=redirect,RedirectConfig='{Protocol=HTTPS,Port=443,StatusCode=HTTP_301}'

2.2 Network Load Balancer (NLB)

Ultra-high performance load balancer operating at Layer 4 (Transport Layer). Handles millions of requests per second with ultra-low latency.

⚡ NLB Use Cases for anihpj
  • WebSocket Connections: Real-time job notifications, live chat support
  • TCP/UDP Traffic: Database connections, custom protocols
  • Static IP: Whitelisting requirements from enterprise clients
  • Extreme Performance: Millions of concurrent connections
  • PrivateLink: Expose services to other AWS accounts
⚖️ ALB vs NLB for anihpj:
  • Use ALB if: Standard Django web app with HTTP/HTTPS (most common)
  • Use NLB if: Need WebSockets for real-time features OR static IP required
  • Use Both: NLB for WebSocket traffic, ALB for HTTP traffic

2.3 Gateway Load Balancer (GWLB)

Deploy, scale, and manage third-party virtual appliances like firewalls, IDS/IPS, and deep packet inspection systems.

🛡️ GWLB for anihpj: Only needed for advanced security requirements like deploying third-party firewalls (Palo Alto, Fortinet) for enterprise clients. Most job portals use AWS WAF with ALB instead.

2.4 Classic Load Balancer (CLB) - Legacy

⚠️ Not recommended for new applications. Classic Load Balancer is the previous generation, now superseded by ALB and NLB.

🚫 Why NOT to use CLB:
  • Limited features compared to ALB
  • No path-based routing or host-based routing
  • Cannot target Lambda functions
  • Less efficient and more expensive
  • AWS recommends migrating to ALB or NLB

2.5 Detailed Load Balancer Comparison

Feature ALB NLB GWLB
OSI Layer Layer 7 (HTTP/HTTPS) Layer 4 (TCP/UDP) Layer 3 (IP)
Target Types EC2, IP, Lambda EC2, IP, ALB EC2, IP
Protocols HTTP, HTTPS, HTTP/2, WebSocket TCP, UDP, TLS IP Protocol
Static IP ❌ No (DNS only) ✅ Yes (Elastic IP) ✅ Yes
SSL Termination ✅ Yes ✅ Yes (TLS) ❌ No
Path Routing ✅ Yes ❌ No ❌ No
Host Routing ✅ Yes ❌ No ❌ No
Sticky Sessions ✅ Yes (cookie-based) ✅ Yes (flow hash) ✅ Yes
WAF Support ✅ Yes ❌ No ❌ No
Performance Good (ms latency) Excellent (μs latency) Excellent
Pricing $0.0225/hr + LCU $0.0225/hr + LCU $0.0125/hr + LCU
anihpj Recommendation ✅ Primary choice For WebSockets only Advanced security only

🚀 3. Getting Started with ELB ▼

3.1 Prerequisites

✅ Before Creating Your First Load Balancer
  1. AWS Account: Active AWS account with billing enabled
  2. VPC Setup: At least 2 subnets in different Availability Zones
    • Example: subnet-0abc123 in us-east-1a
    • Example: subnet-0def456 in us-east-1b
  3. EC2 Instances: At least 2 running instances with anihpj deployed
    • Django running on port 8000 (Gunicorn/uWSGI)
    • Security group allowing inbound traffic on port 8000
  4. Health Check Endpoint: Create /health/ view in Django
    • Returns HTTP 200 when application is healthy
  5. Security Groups:
    • ELB Security Group: Allow 80 (HTTP) and 443 (HTTPS) from 0.0.0.0/0
    • EC2 Security Group: Allow port 8000 from ELB security group
  6. SSL Certificate: (Optional but recommended)
    • Request certificate from AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)
    • Or upload existing certificate

3.2 Create ALB via AWS Console (Beginner-Friendly)

Step 1: Create Load Balancer
  1. Navigate to EC2 Dashboard → Load Balancers → Create Load Balancer
  2. Choose Application Load Balancer → Click Create
  3. Basic Configuration:
    • Name: anihpj-alb
    • Scheme: Internet-facing
    • IP address type: IPv4
  4. Network Mapping:
    • VPC: Select your VPC (e.g., vpc-0xyz789)
    • Mappings: Select at least 2 Availability Zones
      • ✅ us-east-1a → subnet-0abc123
      • ✅ us-east-1b → subnet-0def456
  5. Security Groups:
    • Create new: anihpj-alb-sg
    • Inbound rules:
      • HTTP (80) from 0.0.0.0/0
      • HTTPS (443) from 0.0.0.0/0
Step 2: Configure Target Group
  1. Under Listeners and Routing, click Create target group
  2. Basic Configuration:
    • Target type: Instances
    • Target group name: anihpj-web-tg
    • Protocol: HTTP
    • Port: 8000
    • VPC: Select your VPC
  3. Health Checks:
    • Protocol: HTTP
    • Path: /health/
    • Advanced settings:
      • Healthy threshold: 2
      • Unhealthy threshold: 3
      • Timeout: 5 seconds
      • Interval: 30 seconds
      • Success codes: 200
  4. Click Next
  5. Register Targets:
    • Select your EC2 instances (e.g., anihpj-web-1, anihpj-web-2)
    • Port: 8000
    • Click Include as pending below
  6. Click Create target group
Step 3: Configure Listeners
  1. Back in the ALB creation wizard, under Listeners:
  2. HTTP Listener (Port 80):
    • Protocol: HTTP
    • Port: 80
    • Default action: Redirect to HTTPS (Port 443, Status code 301)
  3. HTTPS Listener (Port 443):
    • Click Add listener
    • Protocol: HTTPS
    • Port: 443
    • Default action: Forward to → anihpj-web-tg
    • SSL certificate: Select from ACM or upload
  4. Click Create load balancer
✅ Success! Your load balancer will be provisioned in ~5 minutes. Once state is Active, you can access anihpj via the DNS name: 
anihpj-alb-123456789.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com

3.3 Create ALB via AWS CLI (Advanced)

Complete CLI Setup Script
#!/bin/bash
# anihpj ALB Setup Script
# Run this after EC2 instances are deployed with Django

# Variables
VPC_ID="vpc-0xyz789"
SUBNET_1="subnet-0abc123"  # us-east-1a
SUBNET_2="subnet-0def456"  # us-east-1b
CERT_ARN="arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789:certificate/abc-123"
INSTANCE_1="i-0abc123"
INSTANCE_2="i-0def456"

echo "🔧 Creating Security Group for ALB..."
SG_ID=$(aws ec2 create-security-group \
    --group-name anihpj-alb-sg \
    --description "Security group for anihpj ALB" \
    --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
    --query 'GroupId' \
    --output text)

echo "Security Group ID: $SG_ID"

# Allow HTTP and HTTPS
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id $SG_ID \
    --protocol tcp --port 80 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0

aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id $SG_ID \
    --protocol tcp --port 443 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0

echo "🌐 Creating Application Load Balancer..."
LB_ARN=$(aws elbv2 create-load-balancer \
    --name anihpj-alb \
    --subnets $SUBNET_1 $SUBNET_2 \
    --security-groups $SG_ID \
    --scheme internet-facing \
    --type application \
    --ip-address-type ipv4 \
    --tags Key=Application,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production \
    --query 'LoadBalancers[0].LoadBalancerArn' \
    --output text)

echo "Load Balancer ARN: $LB_ARN"

# Wait for ALB to become active
echo "⏳ Waiting for ALB to become active (this takes ~3 minutes)..."
aws elbv2 wait load-balancer-available --load-balancer-arns $LB_ARN

echo "🎯 Creating Target Group..."
TG_ARN=$(aws elbv2 create-target-group \
    --name anihpj-web-tg \
    --protocol HTTP \
    --port 8000 \
    --vpc-id $VPC_ID \
    --health-check-enabled \
    --health-check-protocol HTTP \
    --health-check-path /health/ \
    --health-check-interval-seconds 30 \
    --health-check-timeout-seconds 5 \
    --healthy-threshold-count 2 \
    --unhealthy-threshold-count 3 \
    --matcher HttpCode=200 \
    --tags Key=Application,Value=anihpj \
    --query 'TargetGroups[0].TargetGroupArn' \
    --output text)

echo "Target Group ARN: $TG_ARN"

echo "📦 Registering EC2 Instances..."
aws elbv2 register-targets \
    --target-group-arn $TG_ARN \
    --targets Id=$INSTANCE_1,Port=8000 Id=$INSTANCE_2,Port=8000

echo "🔒 Creating HTTPS Listener..."
aws elbv2 create-listener \
    --load-balancer-arn $LB_ARN \
    --protocol HTTPS \
    --port 443 \
    --certificates CertificateArn=$CERT_ARN \
    --default-actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$TG_ARN

echo "🔄 Creating HTTP to HTTPS Redirect Listener..."
aws elbv2 create-listener \
    --load-balancer-arn $LB_ARN \
    --protocol HTTP \
    --port 80 \
    --default-actions Type=redirect,RedirectConfig='{Protocol=HTTPS,Port=443,StatusCode=HTTP_301}'

# Get ALB DNS name
DNS_NAME=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers \
    --load-balancer-arns $LB_ARN \
    --query 'LoadBalancers[0].DNSName' \
    --output text)

echo ""
echo "✅ ALB Setup Complete!"
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo "ALB DNS Name: $DNS_NAME"
echo "Access anihpj at: https://$DNS_NAME"
echo ""
echo "Next Steps:"
echo "1. Create Route 53 CNAME: anihpj.com → $DNS_NAME"
echo "2. Update Django ALLOWED_HOSTS with anihpj.com"
echo "3. Monitor target health in AWS Console"
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

3.4 Django Integration with ELB

1. Create Health Check View (anihpj/jobpost/views.py)
from django.http import JsonResponse
from django.db import connection
from django.core.cache import cache

def health_check(request):
    """
    Health check endpoint for ELB target group.
    Returns 200 if all systems are operational.
    """
    health_status = {
        'status': 'healthy',
        'checks': {}
    }
    
    # Check database connectivity
    try:
        with connection.cursor() as cursor:
            cursor.execute("SELECT 1")
        health_status['checks']['database'] = 'ok'
    except Exception as e:
        health_status['status'] = 'unhealthy'
        health_status['checks']['database'] = f'error: {str(e)}'
        return JsonResponse(health_status, status=503)
    
    # Check cache (Redis/Memcached)
    try:
        cache.set('health_check', 'ok', 10)
        if cache.get('health_check') == 'ok':
            health_status['checks']['cache'] = 'ok'
        else:
            raise Exception('Cache read/write failed')
    except Exception as e:
        # Cache is optional, don't fail health check
        health_status['checks']['cache'] = f'warning: {str(e)}'
    
    return JsonResponse(health_status, status=200)
2. Add Health Check URL (anihpj/jobpost/urls.py)
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('', views.index, name='index'),
    path('health/', views.health_check, name='health_check'),  # ELB health check
    # ... other URLs
]
3. Update Django Settings (anihpj/anihpj/settings.py)
# SECURITY WARNING: Update ALLOWED_HOSTS with ALB DNS and custom domain
ALLOWED_HOSTS = [
    'anihpj.com',
    'www.anihpj.com',
    'anihpj-alb-123456789.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',  # ALB DNS name
    '.elasticloadbalancing.amazonaws.com',  # All ELB domains
]

# Trust ALB's X-Forwarded-Proto header for HTTPS detection
SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER = ('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO', 'https')

# Get client's real IP from X-Forwarded-For header
# (ALB adds client IP to this header)
USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST = True

# Session configuration for sticky sessions
SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True  # Only send over HTTPS
SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY = True  # Prevent JavaScript access
SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE = 'Lax'
SESSION_COOKIE_AGE = 86400  # 24 hours

# CSRF configuration
CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True
CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS = [
    'https://anihpj.com',
    'https://www.anihpj.com',
]
4. Gunicorn Configuration (gunicorn_config.py)
# Gunicorn config for use with ELB
bind = "0.0.0.0:8000"
workers = 4  # (2 x CPU cores) + 1
worker_class = "gthread"
threads = 2
timeout = 120  # Must be < ELB timeout (default 60s, increase ELB to 120s)
keepalive = 5

# Logging
accesslog = "/var/log/anihpj/access.log"
errorlog = "/var/log/anihpj/error.log"
loglevel = "info"

# Trust ALB proxy headers
forwarded_allow_ips = "*"  # ALB IPs change, so allow all

# Graceful shutdown for connection draining
graceful_timeout = 30  # Match ELB deregistration delay
5. Update Security Group for EC2 Instances
# Allow traffic only from ALB security group
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id sg-0ec2instances \
    --protocol tcp --port 8000 \
    --source-group sg-0alb123

# Remove direct internet access (best practice)
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id sg-0ec2instances \
    --protocol tcp --port 8000 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
🎯 Testing Your Setup:
  1. Health Check: curl https://anihpj-alb-xxx.elb.amazonaws.com/health/
  2. View Target Health: AWS Console → EC2 → Target Groups → anihpj-web-tg → Targets tab
  3. Test Load Balancing: Refresh page multiple times, check server hostname in response
  4. Check Logs: tail -f /var/log/anihpj/access.log on each EC2 instance

🔑 4. Key Features & Configuration ▼

4.1 Target Groups

Target groups route requests to registered targets (EC2 instances, IP addresses, Lambda functions) based on protocol and port.

🎯 Target Group Types for anihpj
Type Use Case Example
Instance Route to EC2 instances by instance ID i-0abc123 (anihpj-web-1)
IP Route to private IP addresses (on-premises, containers) 10.0.1.25 (Docker container)
Lambda Route to serverless Lambda function arn:aws:lambda:...:anihpj-api
Multiple Target Groups for Microservices
# Create target group for web frontend
aws elbv2 create-target-group \
    --name anihpj-web-tg \
    --protocol HTTP --port 8000 \
    --vpc-id vpc-0xyz789 \
    --health-check-path /health/ \
    --tags Key=Service,Value=web

# Create target group for API backend
aws elbv2 create-target-group \
    --name anihpj-api-tg \
    --protocol HTTP --port 8001 \
    --vpc-id vpc-0xyz789 \
    --health-check-path /api/health/ \
    --tags Key=Service,Value=api

# Create target group for admin panel
aws elbv2 create-target-group \
    --name anihpj-admin-tg \
    --protocol HTTP --port 8000 \
    --vpc-id vpc-0xyz789 \
    --health-check-path /admin/login/ \
    --matcher HttpCode=200,302 \
    --tags Key=Service,Value=admin

4.2 Health Checks

ELB continuously monitors target health and routes traffic only to healthy targets.

📊 Health Check Configuration
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         HEALTH CHECK FLOW                            │
│                                                                      │
│  Every 30 seconds:                                                   │
│  ELB → GET /health/ → Target Instance                               │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌──────────────────┐                                               │
│  │  Target Response │                                               │
│  └────────┬─────────┘                                               │
│           │                                                          │
│           ├─→ HTTP 200 + Response Time < 5s = ✅ Healthy            │
│           │   (2 consecutive successes → mark healthy)              │
│           │                                                          │
│           └─→ Timeout OR HTTP 5xx = ❌ Unhealthy                    │
│               (3 consecutive failures → mark unhealthy)             │
│                                                                      │
│  State Transitions:                                                  │
│  Initial → Healthy (2 successes)                                    │
│  Healthy → Unhealthy (3 failures)                                   │
│  Unhealthy → Healthy (2 successes)                                  │
│  Unhealthy → Draining → Removed (if deregistered)                   │
│                                                                      │
│  Actions on Unhealthy:                                               │
│  • Stop sending new requests                                        │
│  • Auto Scaling launches replacement (if configured)                │
│  • CloudWatch alarm triggered                                       │
│  • SNS notification sent (if configured)                            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Advanced Health Check Settings
# Django health check with detailed diagnostics
from django.http import JsonResponse
from django.db import connection
from django.core.cache import cache
import time

def health_check(request):
    """
    Detailed health check for ELB with multiple system checks.
    """
    start_time = time.time()
    health = {'status': 'healthy', 'checks': {}, 'timestamp': time.time()}
    
    # 1. Database check
    try:
        with connection.cursor() as cursor:
            cursor.execute("SELECT 1")
            db_start = time.time()
            cursor.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM jobpost_job")
            db_time = (time.time() - db_start) * 1000
            health['checks']['database'] = {'status': 'ok', 'response_time_ms': round(db_time, 2)}
    except Exception as e:
        health['status'] = 'unhealthy'
        health['checks']['database'] = {'status': 'error', 'error': str(e)}
        return JsonResponse(health, status=503)
    
    # 2. Cache check
    try:
        cache_start = time.time()
        cache.set('health_test', 'ok', 10)
        cache.get('health_test')
        cache_time = (time.time() - cache_start) * 1000
        health['checks']['cache'] = {'status': 'ok', 'response_time_ms': round(cache_time, 2)}
    except Exception as e:
        health['checks']['cache'] = {'status': 'degraded', 'error': str(e)}
    
    # 3. Disk space check
    import shutil
    stat = shutil.disk_usage('/')
    disk_percent = (stat.used / stat.total) * 100
    health['checks']['disk'] = {
        'status': 'ok' if disk_percent < 85 else 'warning',
        'used_percent': round(disk_percent, 1)
    }
    
    # 4. Response time
    health['total_response_time_ms'] = round((time.time() - start_time) * 1000, 2)
    
    return JsonResponse(health, status=200)
Configure Health Check via CLI
aws elbv2 modify-target-group \
    --target-group-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/abc123 \
    --health-check-enabled \
    --health-check-protocol HTTP \
    --health-check-path /health/ \
    --health-check-interval-seconds 30 \
    --health-check-timeout-seconds 5 \
    --healthy-threshold-count 2 \
    --unhealthy-threshold-count 3 \
    --matcher HttpCode=200

4.3 Listeners & Routing Rules

Listeners check for connection requests and route them based on configured rules.

Path-Based Routing for anihpj Microservices
# Listener rule priority determines evaluation order
# Lower numbers = higher priority

# Priority 1: API requests
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:listener/app/anihpj-alb/abc/123 \
    --priority 1 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/api/*' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-api-tg/xyz

# Priority 2: Admin panel
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:listener/app/anihpj-alb/abc/123 \
    --priority 2 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/admin/*' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-admin-tg/xyz

# Priority 3: Employer dashboard
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:listener/app/anihpj-alb/abc/123 \
    --priority 3 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/employer/*' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-employer-tg/xyz

# Default rule: Main web application (automatically created)

4.4 SSL/TLS Termination

Offload SSL/TLS encryption/decryption from your Django servers to the load balancer.

🔒 SSL/TLS Setup for anihpj
  1. Request SSL Certificate from ACM: 
    aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names www.anihpj.com api.anihpj.com \
    --validation-method DNS
  2. Validate Domain Ownership: Add CNAME records to Route 53
  3. Attach Certificate to HTTPS Listener: 
    aws elbv2 modify-listener \
    --listener-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:listener/app/anihpj-alb/abc/123 \
    --certificates CertificateArn=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789:certificate/abc-123
  4. Configure Security Policy: 
    aws elbv2 modify-listener \
    --listener-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:listener/app/anihpj-alb/abc/123 \
    --ssl-policy ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06
🔐 Recommended SSL Policy: ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06
  • TLS 1.3 and TLS 1.2 only (no SSLv3, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1)
  • Forward secrecy ciphers
  • PFS (Perfect Forward Secrecy) enabled
  • A+ rating on SSL Labs test

4.5 Sticky Sessions (Session Affinity)

Route returning users to the same target instance for session consistency.

Enable Sticky Sessions for Django Sessions
# Enable application-based (cookie) stickiness
aws elbv2 modify-target-group-attributes \
    --target-group-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/xyz \
    --attributes \
        Key=stickiness.enabled,Value=true \
        Key=stickiness.type,Value=lb_cookie \
        Key=stickiness.lb_cookie.duration_seconds,Value=86400  # 24 hours
How It Works
First Request:
  User → ALB → anihpj-web-2 (random selection)
  ALB sets cookie: AWSALB=abc123xyz...

Subsequent Requests (same user):
  User (cookie: AWSALB=abc123xyz) → ALB → anihpj-web-2 (same instance)
  
If Target Becomes Unhealthy:
  ALB automatically routes to different healthy target
  New cookie issued for new target
💡 Best Practice for anihpj:
  • Recommended: Use database-backed or Redis-backed sessions (stateless servers)
  • Alternative: Enable sticky sessions if using file-based sessions
  • Duration should match Django SESSION_COOKIE_AGE setting
# settings.py - Use Redis for sessions (recommended with ELB)
SESSION_ENGINE = 'django.contrib.sessions.backends.cache'
SESSION_CACHE_ALIAS = 'default'  # Redis cache

CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': 'redis://anihpj-redis.abc123.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com:6379/1',
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
        }
    }
}

4.6 Cross-Zone Load Balancing

Distribute traffic evenly across all registered targets in all enabled Availability Zones.

Cross-Zone Load Balancing Visualization
WITHOUT Cross-Zone (Default for NLB):
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ALB distributes 50% traffic to each AZ                      │
│                                                               │
│  AZ-1 (50% traffic)          AZ-2 (50% traffic)              │
│  ├─ Instance-1 (25%)         ├─ Instance-3 (16.67%)         │
│  └─ Instance-2 (25%)         ├─ Instance-4 (16.67%)         │
│                              └─ Instance-5 (16.67%)         │
│                                                               │
│  Problem: Uneven distribution if AZs have different counts   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

WITH Cross-Zone (Default for ALB):
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ALB distributes evenly across ALL instances                 │
│                                                               │
│  AZ-1                        AZ-2                            │
│  ├─ Instance-1 (20%)         ├─ Instance-3 (20%)            │
│  └─ Instance-2 (20%)         ├─ Instance-4 (20%)            │
│                              └─ Instance-5 (20%)            │
│                                                               │
│  Result: Each instance gets equal traffic regardless of AZ   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Enable Cross-Zone Load Balancing (NLB)
# ALB: Cross-zone is ENABLED by default (free)
# NLB: Cross-zone is DISABLED by default (costs extra)

# Enable for NLB
aws elbv2 modify-load-balancer-attributes \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:loadbalancer/net/anihpj-nlb/xyz \
    --attributes Key=load_balancing.cross_zone.enabled,Value=true
📊 anihpj Recommendation: Always use cross-zone load balancing for even traffic distribution, especially during Auto Scaling events when instance counts differ across AZs.

💰 5. Pricing & Cost Optimization ▼

5.1 Pricing Model

ELB pricing consists of hourly charges + usage charges (LCU - Load Balancer Capacity Units).

💵 Pricing Components (US East - N. Virginia)
Load Balancer Type Hourly Rate LCU Rate Monthly Base
Application LB (ALB) $0.0225/hour $0.008/LCU-hour $16.20
Network LB (NLB) $0.0225/hour $0.006/LCU-hour $16.20
Gateway LB (GWLB) $0.0125/hour $0.004/LCU-hour $9.00
Classic LB (CLB) $0.025/hour $0.008/GB $18.00
📊 What is an LCU (Load Balancer Capacity Unit)?

An LCU measures the dimensions used for processing traffic. The highest dimension consumed determines the LCU count:

  • New connections: 25 per second
  • Active connections: 3,000 per minute
  • Processed bytes: 1 GB per hour (for HTTP)
  • Rule evaluations: 1,000 per second

5.2 Cost Calculator for anihpj

💰 Monthly Cost Estimate for anihpj Job Portal
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  SCENARIO: Medium Traffic Job Portal                                │
│  • 500,000 page views/month                                          │
│  • 1 Application Load Balancer                                       │
│  • 3 EC2 instances across 2 AZs                                      │
│  • Average page size: 500 KB                                         │
│  • Peak traffic: 50 requests/second                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

COST BREAKDOWN:

1. Hourly Charges:
   $0.0225/hour × 730 hours/month = $16.43/month

2. LCU Charges (calculated by highest dimension):
   
   Dimension A: New Connections
   • 500,000 pageviews ÷ 30 days ÷ 86,400 seconds = 0.19 conn/sec
   • LCU = 0.19 ÷ 25 = 0.008 LCU
   
   Dimension B: Active Connections
   • Average concurrent users: 50
   • LCU = 50 ÷ 3,000 = 0.017 LCU
   
   Dimension C: Processed Bytes
   • 500,000 pageviews × 500 KB = 250 GB/month
   • 250 GB ÷ 730 hours = 0.34 GB/hour
   • LCU = 0.34 ÷ 1 = 0.34 LCU  ← HIGHEST
   
   Dimension D: Rule Evaluations
   • 0.19 requests/sec × 3 rules = 0.57 evaluations/sec
   • LCU = 0.57 ÷ 1,000 = 0.0006 LCU
   
   Highest LCU = 0.34
   LCU Cost = 0.34 LCU × $0.008/hour × 730 hours = $1.99/month

3. Data Transfer (separate from ELB pricing):
   • First 10 TB/month: $0.09/GB
   • 250 GB × $0.09 = $22.50/month

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
TOTAL MONTHLY COST:
  ALB Hourly:        $16.43
  ALB LCU:           $ 1.99
  Data Transfer:     $22.50
  ─────────────────────────
  TOTAL:             $40.92/month
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📈 Cost Scaling Examples
Traffic Level Monthly Pageviews Data Transfer ALB Cost Total Cost
Small 100,000 50 GB $16.43 $21.00
Medium 500,000 250 GB $18.42 $41.00
Large 2,000,000 1 TB $24.00 $114.00
Enterprise 10,000,000 5 TB $60.00 $510.00

5.3 Cost Optimization Strategies

💡 10 Ways to Reduce ELB Costs for anihpj
  1. Use CloudFront CDN: Serve static files from CloudFront, reduce ELB data transfer
    • Cost savings: ~60% reduction in data transfer costs
  2. Enable Compression: Gzip/Brotli compression reduces bandwidth 
    # settings.py
MIDDLEWARE = [
    'django.middleware.gzip.GZipMiddleware',  # Add this
    # ... other middleware
]
GZIP_COMPRESSION_LEVEL = 6
  3. Optimize Images: Use WebP format, lazy loading, responsive images
    • Reduces page size from 500 KB to 150 KB average
  4. Consolidate Load Balancers: Use path-based routing instead of multiple ALBs
    • Save $16/month per eliminated ALB
  5. Delete Unused Load Balancers: You're charged even with 0 traffic
  6. Use Auto Scaling: Scale down during low-traffic hours (nights, weekends)
  7. Minimize Rule Evaluations: Consolidate similar rules
    • Use /api/* instead of multiple /api/jobs/, /api/users/ rules
  8. Consider Regional Differences: Cheaper in some regions (but latency tradeoff)
  9. Monitor LCU Usage: Use CloudWatch to identify cost drivers 
    aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
    --namespace AWS/ApplicationELB \
    --metric-name ConsumedLCUs \
    --dimensions Name=LoadBalancer,Value=app/anihpj-alb/abc123 \
    --start-time 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z \
    --end-time 2024-01-31T23:59:59Z \
    --period 3600 \
    --statistics Average,Maximum
  10. Use S3 for Large Downloads: Direct links to S3 instead of proxying through ELB
    • Example: Resume downloads, job application PDFs

📈 6. Scalability & Performance ▼

6.1 Integration with Auto Scaling

ELB automatically registers/deregisters instances as Auto Scaling adds/removes them.

Auto Scaling Configuration for anihpj
# 1. Create Launch Template
aws ec2 create-launch-template \
    --launch-template-name anihpj-web-template \
    --version-description "Django app with Gunicorn" \
    --launch-template-data '{
        "ImageId": "ami-0abc123",
        "InstanceType": "t3.medium",
        "KeyName": "anihpj-key",
        "SecurityGroupIds": ["sg-0web123"],
        "UserData": "IyEvYmluL2Jhc2gKY2QgL2hvbWUvdWJ1bnR1L2FuaWhwagpzb3VyY2UgdmVudi9iaW4vYWN0aXZhdGUKZ3VuaWNvcm4gLWMgZ3VuaWNvcm5fY29uZmlnLnB5IGFuaWhwai53c2dpOmFwcGxpY2F0aW9u"
    }'

# 2. Create Auto Scaling Group
aws autoscaling create-auto-scaling-group \
    --auto-scaling-group-name anihpj-web-asg \
    --launch-template LaunchTemplateName=anihpj-web-template,Version='$Latest' \
    --min-size 2 \
    --max-size 10 \
    --desired-capacity 4 \
    --target-group-arns arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/xyz \
    --health-check-type ELB \
    --health-check-grace-period 300 \
    --vpc-zone-identifier "subnet-0abc123,subnet-0def456" \
    --tags Key=Application,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production

# 3. Create Scaling Policies

# Scale UP when CPU > 70%
aws autoscaling put-scaling-policy \
    --auto-scaling-group-name anihpj-web-asg \
    --policy-name scale-up-cpu \
    --policy-type TargetTrackingScaling \
    --target-tracking-configuration '{
        "PredefinedMetricSpecification": {
            "PredefinedMetricType": "ASGAverageCPUUtilization"
        },
        "TargetValue": 70.0
    }'

# Scale UP when ALB request count > 1000 per target
aws autoscaling put-scaling-policy \
    --auto-scaling-group-name anihpj-web-asg \
    --policy-name scale-up-requests \
    --policy-type TargetTrackingScaling \
    --target-tracking-configuration '{
        "PredefinedMetricSpecification": {
            "PredefinedMetricType": "ALBRequestCountPerTarget",
            "ResourceLabel": "app/anihpj-alb/abc123/targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/xyz"
        },
        "TargetValue": 1000.0
    }'

# Scheduled scaling for known traffic patterns
# Scale UP before business hours (8 AM)
aws autoscaling put-scheduled-action \
    --auto-scaling-group-name anihpj-web-asg \
    --scheduled-action-name scale-up-morning \
    --recurrence "0 8 * * MON-FRI" \
    --min-size 4 --max-size 10 --desired-capacity 6

# Scale DOWN after business hours (6 PM)
aws autoscaling put-scheduled-action \
    --auto-scaling-group-name anihpj-web-asg \
    --scheduled-action-name scale-down-evening \
    --recurrence "0 18 * * MON-FRI" \
    --min-size 2 --max-size 10 --desired-capacity 2

6.2 Connection Draining

Allows in-flight requests to complete before deregistering targets during scale-down or deployments.

Enable Connection Draining (Deregistration Delay)
# Set deregistration delay to 30 seconds
aws elbv2 modify-target-group-attributes \
    --target-group-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/xyz \
    --attributes Key=deregistration_delay.timeout_seconds,Value=30
How It Works
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  GRACEFUL SHUTDOWN PROCESS                                        │
│                                                                   │
│  t=0s:  Auto Scaling initiates instance termination              │
│         ├─→ ALB marks target as "Draining"                       │
│         └─→ New connections go to other healthy targets          │
│                                                                   │
│  t=0-30s: Existing connections complete                          │
│           • User A finishes job application form (15s)           │
│           • User B completes file upload (20s)                   │
│           • User C finishes search query (2s)                    │
│                                                                   │
│  t=30s: Deregistration delay timeout reached                     │
│         ├─→ ALB forcibly closes remaining connections            │
│         └─→ Target removed from target group                     │
│                                                                   │
│  t=31s: Auto Scaling terminates EC2 instance                     │
│                                                                   │
│  Result: Zero failed requests, seamless user experience          │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
✅ Best Practices for anihpj:
  • Set deregistration delay to match longest request duration
  • For anihpj: 30-60 seconds (job applications, file uploads)
  • Ensure Gunicorn graceful_timeout ≥ deregistration delay
  • Use lifecycle hooks for database cleanup before termination

6.3 Performance Tuning

⚡ Performance Optimization Checklist
  1. Enable HTTP/2: Multiplexing, header compression (enabled by default on ALB)
  2. Optimize Health Checks:
    • Use lightweight endpoint (avoid database queries)
    • Increase interval for stable systems (30s → 60s)
  3. Connection Idle Timeout: 
    aws elbv2 modify-load-balancer-attributes \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc \
    --attributes Key=idle_timeout.timeout_seconds,Value=60
  4. Enable Access Logs: Identify slow endpoints 
    aws elbv2 modify-load-balancer-attributes \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc \
    --attributes \
        Key=access_logs.s3.enabled,Value=true \
        Key=access_logs.s3.bucket,Value=anihpj-elb-logs
  5. Optimize Target Group Settings:
    • Slow start mode for new instances (warm-up period)
    • Reduce slow start duration after testing
    aws elbv2 modify-target-group-attributes \
    --target-group-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/xyz \
    --attributes Key=slow_start.duration_seconds,Value=60
  6. Monitor CloudWatch Metrics:
    • TargetResponseTime - optimize slow endpoints
    • UnHealthyHostCount - fix failing instances
    • RequestCount - capacity planning
    • HTTPCode_Target_5XX_Count - application errors

🎓 7. Advanced Topics ▼

7.1 Advanced Path-Based Routing

Complex Routing Rules for anihpj Microservices
# Rule 1: API v2 (new microservice architecture)
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 1 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/api/v2/*' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$API_V2_TG

# Rule 2: API v1 (legacy, redirect to v2)
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 2 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/api/v1/*' \
    --actions Type=redirect,RedirectConfig='{
        "Protocol": "HTTPS",
        "Port": "443",
        "Path": "/api/v2/#{path}",
        "StatusCode": "HTTP_301"
    }'

# Rule 3: GraphQL API
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 3 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/graphql' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$GRAPHQL_TG

# Rule 4: Employer dashboard (specific instance types)
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 4 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/employer/*' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$EMPLOYER_TG

# Rule 5: Admin panel (restrict by source IP)
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 5 \
    --conditions \
        Field=path-pattern,Values='/admin/*' \
        Field=source-ip,Values='203.0.113.0/24,198.51.100.0/24' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$ADMIN_TG

# Rule 6: Health checks return fixed response
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 6 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/health',Field=http-request-method,Values='GET' \
    --actions Type=fixed-response,FixedResponseConfig='{
        "StatusCode": "200",
        "ContentType": "application/json",
        "MessageBody": "{\"status\":\"healthy\",\"service\":\"anihpj-alb\"}"
    }'

7.2 Host-Based Routing (Multi-Tenant)

Route Different Domains to Different Target Groups
# Main job portal
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 10 \
    --conditions Field=host-header,Values='www.anihpj.com','anihpj.com' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$MAIN_WEB_TG

# API subdomain
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 11 \
    --conditions Field=host-header,Values='api.anihpj.com' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$API_TG

# Employer portal (separate subdomain)
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 12 \
    --conditions Field=host-header,Values='employer.anihpj.com' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$EMPLOYER_TG

# Blog (WordPress on separate instances)
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 13 \
    --conditions Field=host-header,Values='blog.anihpj.com' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$BLOG_TG

# Mobile API
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 14 \
    --conditions Field=host-header,Values='mobile.anihpj.com' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$MOBILE_API_TG

7.3 Lambda as Target

ALB can invoke Lambda functions directly without EC2 instances.

Hybrid Architecture: EC2 + Lambda
# Lambda function for lightweight API endpoints
import json

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Handle /api/stats endpoint - lightweight aggregation queries
    Event contains ALB request in event['requestContext']['elb']
    """
