    # Extract request data from ALB
    path = event['path']  # /api/stats
    method = event['httpMethod']  # GET
    headers = event['headers']
    query_params = event.get('queryStringParameters', {})
    
    # Your business logic
    stats = {
        'total_jobs': 15420,
        'active_employers': 320,
        'total_applications': 89500,
        'timestamp': '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z'
    }
    
    # Return ALB-compatible response
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'statusDescription': '200 OK',
        'headers': {
            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
            'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*'
        },
        'body': json.dumps(stats),
        'isBase64Encoded': False
    }
Register Lambda as Target
# 1. Add ALB invocation permission to Lambda
aws lambda add-permission \
    --function-name anihpj-stats-api \
    --statement-id AllowALBInvoke \
    --action lambda:InvokeFunction \
    --principal elasticloadbalancing.amazonaws.com \
    --source-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-lambda-tg/xyz

# 2. Create target group for Lambda
aws elbv2 create-target-group \
    --name anihpj-lambda-tg \
    --target-type lambda \
    --tags Key=Service,Value=lambda-api

# 3. Register Lambda function
aws elbv2 register-targets \
    --target-group-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-lambda-tg/xyz \
    --targets Id=arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789:function:anihpj-stats-api

# 4. Create routing rule
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 20 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/api/stats' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-lambda-tg/xyz

7.4 AWS WAF Integration

Protect anihpj from common web exploits (SQL injection, XSS, DDoS).

Setup WAF with ALB
# 1. Create Web ACL
aws wafv2 create-web-acl \
    --name anihpj-waf \
    --scope REGIONAL \
    --region us-east-1 \
    --default-action Allow={} \
    --rules file://waf-rules.json \
    --visibility-config \
        SampledRequestsEnabled=true,CloudWatchMetricsEnabled=true,MetricName=anihpjWAF

# 2. Associate WAF with ALB
aws wafv2 associate-web-acl \
    --web-acl-arn arn:aws:wafv2:us-east-1:123456789:regional/webacl/anihpj-waf/abc-123 \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/xyz
WAF Rules (waf-rules.json)
[
    {
        "Name": "RateLimitRule",
        "Priority": 1,
        "Statement": {
            "RateBasedStatement": {
                "Limit": 2000,
                "AggregateKeyType": "IP"
            }
        },
        "Action": { "Block": {} },
        "VisibilityConfig": {
            "SampledRequestsEnabled": true,
            "CloudWatchMetricsEnabled": true,
            "MetricName": "RateLimit"
        }
    },
    {
        "Name": "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet",
        "Priority": 2,
        "Statement": {
            "ManagedRuleGroupStatement": {
                "VendorName": "AWS",
                "Name": "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet"
            }
        },
        "OverrideAction": { "None": {} },
        "VisibilityConfig": {
            "SampledRequestsEnabled": true,
            "CloudWatchMetricsEnabled": true,
            "MetricName": "CommonRuleSet"
        }
    },
    {
        "Name": "SQLInjectionProtection",
        "Priority": 3,
        "Statement": {
            "ManagedRuleGroupStatement": {
                "VendorName": "AWS",
                "Name": "AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet"
            }
        },
        "OverrideAction": { "None": {} },
        "VisibilityConfig": {
            "SampledRequestsEnabled": true,
            "CloudWatchMetricsEnabled": true,
            "MetricName": "SQLInjection"
        }
    }
]

7.5 Access Logs & Analytics

Enable & Analyze Access Logs
# 1. Create S3 bucket for logs
aws s3 mb s3://anihpj-elb-logs --region us-east-1

# 2. Add bucket policy
cat > bucket-policy.json << 'EOF'
{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Principal": {
                "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::127311923021:root"
            },
            "Action": "s3:PutObject",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-elb-logs/*"
        }
    ]
}
EOF

aws s3api put-bucket-policy --bucket anihpj-elb-logs --policy file://bucket-policy.json

# 3. Enable access logs
aws elbv2 modify-load-balancer-attributes \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc \
    --attributes \
        Key=access_logs.s3.enabled,Value=true \
        Key=access_logs.s3.bucket,Value=anihpj-elb-logs \
        Key=access_logs.s3.prefix,Value=anihpj-alb

# 4. Analyze logs with Athena
# Create Athena table
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS alb_logs (
    type string,
    time string,
    elb string,
    client_ip string,
    client_port int,
    target_ip string,
    target_port int,
    request_processing_time double,
    target_processing_time double,
    response_processing_time double,
    elb_status_code string,
    target_status_code string,
    received_bytes bigint,
    sent_bytes bigint,
    request_verb string,
    request_url string,
    request_proto string,
    user_agent string,
    ssl_cipher string,
    ssl_protocol string,
    target_group_arn string,
    trace_id string
)
ROW FORMAT SERDE 'org.apache.hadoop.hive.serde2.RegexSerDe'
WITH SERDEPROPERTIES (
'serialization.format' = '1',
'input.regex' = '([^ ]*) ([^ ]*) ([^ ]*) ([^ ]*):([0-9]*) ([^ ]*)[:-]([0-9]*) ([-.0-9]*) ([-.0-9]*) ([-.0-9]*) (|[-0-9]*) (-|[-0-9]*) ([-0-9]*) ([-0-9]*) \"([^ ]*) ([^ ]*) (- |[^ ]*)\" \"([^\"]*)\" ([A-Z0-9-]+) ([A-Za-z0-9.-]*) ([^ ]*) \"([^\"]*)\"'
)
LOCATION 's3://anihpj-elb-logs/anihpj-alb/AWSLogs/123456789/elasticloadbalancing/us-east-1/';

# Query examples
-- Top 10 slowest requests
SELECT request_url, AVG(target_processing_time) as avg_time
FROM alb_logs
WHERE target_processing_time > 0
GROUP BY request_url
ORDER BY avg_time DESC
LIMIT 10;

-- Error rate by endpoint
SELECT request_url, 
       COUNT(*) as total_requests,
       SUM(CASE WHEN elb_status_code LIKE '5%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as errors,
       (SUM(CASE WHEN elb_status_code LIKE '5%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) as error_rate
FROM alb_logs
GROUP BY request_url
HAVING error_rate > 1
ORDER BY error_rate DESC;

✨ 8. Best Practices ▼

8.1 Security Best Practices

🔒 Security Checklist for anihpj
  1. Use HTTPS Only:
    • Redirect HTTP (80) → HTTPS (443)
    • Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) for free SSL certificates
    • Enable TLS 1.3 security policy
  2. Security Groups:
    • ALB: Allow 80, 443 from 0.0.0.0/0
    • EC2: Allow 8000 ONLY from ALB security group (not 0.0.0.0/0)
    # Good practice
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id sg-ec2instances \
    --protocol tcp --port 8000 \
    --source-group sg-alb

# BAD practice (don't do this)
# aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
#     --group-id sg-ec2instances \
#     --protocol tcp --port 8000 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
  3. Enable WAF:
    • Protect against SQL injection, XSS
    • Rate limiting (prevent DDoS)
    • IP blacklisting/whitelisting
  4. Enable Access Logs:
    • Audit trail for security investigations
    • Detect suspicious patterns
    • Retention: minimum 90 days
  5. Restrict Admin Access: 
    # Restrict /admin/* to office IP only
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 5 \
    --conditions \
        Field=path-pattern,Values='/admin/*' \
        Field=source-ip,Values='203.0.113.50/32' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$ADMIN_TG
  6. Drop Invalid Headers: 
    aws elbv2 modify-load-balancer-attributes \
    --load-balancer-arn $ALB_ARN \
    --attributes Key=routing.http.drop_invalid_header_fields.enabled,Value=true
  7. Enable Deletion Protection: 
    aws elbv2 modify-load-balancer-attributes \
    --load-balancer-arn $ALB_ARN \
    --attributes Key=deletion_protection.enabled,Value=true

8.2 High Availability Best Practices

🌍 HA Configuration for anihpj
  1. Multi-AZ Deployment:
    • Minimum 2 Availability Zones
    • Equal number of instances per AZ
    • Enable cross-zone load balancing
  2. Auto Scaling:
    • Minimum 2 instances (1 per AZ)
    • Desired capacity: 4+ for production
    • Health check type: ELB (not EC2)
  3. Database High Availability:
    • Use RDS Multi-AZ for Django database
    • ElastiCache Redis Multi-AZ for sessions
    • Read replicas for scaling reads
  4. Health Checks:
    • Interval: 30 seconds (production)
    • Timeout: 5 seconds
    • Healthy threshold: 2
    • Unhealthy threshold: 3
  5. Connection Draining:
    • Enable with 30-60 second delay
    • Match with application request timeout
  6. CloudWatch Alarms: 
    # Alarm on unhealthy targets
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name anihpj-unhealthy-targets \
    --alarm-description "Alert when targets become unhealthy" \
    --metric-name UnHealthyHostCount \
    --namespace AWS/ApplicationELB \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 60 \
    --threshold 1 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --evaluation-periods 2 \
    --dimensions Name=LoadBalancer,Value=app/anihpj-alb/abc Name=TargetGroup,Value=targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/xyz \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:anihpj-alerts

# Alarm on high response time
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name anihpj-high-response-time \
    --metric-name TargetResponseTime \
    --namespace AWS/ApplicationELB \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 300 \
    --threshold 2.0 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --evaluation-periods 2 \
    --dimensions Name=LoadBalancer,Value=app/anihpj-alb/abc \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:anihpj-alerts

8.3 Monitoring Best Practices

Essential CloudWatch Metrics to Monitor
Metric Description Alert Threshold
TargetResponseTime Application response time > 2 seconds
HealthyHostCount Number of healthy targets < 2
UnHealthyHostCount Number of unhealthy targets > 0
RequestCount Total requests Baseline +200%
HTTPCode_Target_5XX_Count Server errors from targets > 10/min
HTTPCode_ELB_5XX_Count ELB-generated errors > 0
RejectedConnectionCount Connections rejected (at capacity) > 0
ActiveConnectionCount Current active connections Monitor trend
ConsumedLCUs Load Balancer Capacity Units used Cost tracking

💼 9. Common Use Cases ▼

9.1 Web Applications (anihpj Primary Use Case)

Production Architecture for Django Job Portal
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                            ANIHPJ PRODUCTION SETUP                           │
│                                                                              │
│  Internet                                                                    │
│     │                                                                        │
│     ├─→ Route 53 (anihpj.com)                                               │
│          │                                                                   │
│          ├─→ CloudFront CDN (static files: /static/*, /media/*)             │
│          │    └─→ S3 Bucket (anihpj-static)                                 │
│          │                                                                   │
│          └─→ Application Load Balancer (ALB)                                │
│               │                                                              │
│               ├─→ WAF (SQL injection, XSS protection, rate limiting)        │
│               │                                                              │
│               └─→ Listeners:                                                │
│                    • HTTP :80 → Redirect to HTTPS                           │
│                    • HTTPS :443 → Routing Rules:                            │
│                                                                              │
│                      ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐            │
│                      │  ROUTING RULES (Priority Order)         │            │
│                      │                                          │            │
│                      │  /api/*     → API Target Group          │            │
│                      │  /admin/*   → Admin Target Group        │            │
│                      │  /employer/* → Employer Target Group    │            │
│                      │  /*         → Web Target Group (default)│            │
│                      └─────────────────────────────────────────┘            │
│                                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  WEB TARGET GROUP (Main Django App)                                 │   │
│  │                                                                      │   │
│  │  AZ us-east-1a            AZ us-east-1b                             │   │
│  │  ├─ anihpj-web-1          ├─ anihpj-web-3                           │   │
│  │  │  • t3.medium            │  • t3.medium                            │   │
│  │  │  • Gunicorn (4 workers) │  • Gunicorn (4 workers)                 │   │
│  │  │  • Health: ✅           │  • Health: ✅                           │   │
│  │  │                         │                                         │   │
│  │  └─ anihpj-web-2          └─ anihpj-web-4                           │   │
│  │     • t3.medium               • t3.medium                            │   │
│  │     • Gunicorn (4 workers)    • Gunicorn (4 workers)                 │   │
│  │     • Health: ✅              • Health: ✅                           │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                 │                                            │
│                                 ├─→ RDS PostgreSQL (Multi-AZ)               │
│                                 │   • db.t3.medium                           │
│                                 │   • Primary: us-east-1a                    │
│                                 │   • Standby: us-east-1b                    │
│                                 │                                            │
│                                 └─→ ElastiCache Redis (Multi-AZ)            │
│                                     • Sessions, cache                        │
│                                     • cache.t3.small                         │
│                                                                              │
│  Auto Scaling Policy:                                                        │
│  • Min: 2 instances                                                          │
│  • Max: 10 instances                                                         │
│  • Target: CPU < 70% OR Requests < 1000/target                              │
│  • Scale up: +2 instances when threshold exceeded for 2 min                 │
│  • Scale down: -1 instance when below threshold for 5 min                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

9.2 Microservices Architecture

Microservices with ALB Path-Based Routing
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  APPLICATION LOAD BALANCER                                           │
│  anihpj.com                                                          │
│                                                                      │
│  /api/jobs/*          → Job Service (Python/Django)                 │
│  │  GET /api/jobs/                                                  │
│  │  POST /api/jobs/                                                 │
│  │  Target Group: 3 EC2 instances (t3.small)                        │
│  │  Port: 8001                                                      │
│                                                                      │
│  /api/users/*         → User Service (Node.js/Express)              │
│  │  GET /api/users/profile                                          │
│  │  PUT /api/users/settings                                         │
│  │  Target Group: 2 EC2 instances (t3.small)                        │
│  │  Port: 3000                                                      │
│                                                                      │
│  /api/applications/*  → Application Service (Python/FastAPI)        │
│  │  POST /api/applications/                                         │
│  │  GET /api/applications/:id                                       │
│  │  Target Group: 4 EC2 instances (t3.medium)                       │
│  │  Port: 8002                                                      │
│                                                                      │
│  /api/notifications/* → Notification Service (Go)                   │
│  │  WebSocket connections for real-time alerts                      │
│  │  Target Type: NLB (for WebSocket persistence)                    │
│  │  Port: 8080                                                      │
│                                                                      │
│  /api/search/*        → Search Service (Elasticsearch + Lambda)     │
│  │  GET /api/search?q=python+developer                              │
│  │  Target Type: Lambda function (serverless)                       │
│  │  Response: Aggregated search results                             │
│                                                                      │
│  /api/analytics/*     → Analytics Service (Lambda)                  │
│  │  GET /api/analytics/stats                                        │
│  │  Target Type: Lambda function (serverless)                       │
│                                                                      │
│  /*                   → Frontend (React SPA)                         │
│     Served from S3 via CloudFront                                   │
│     ALB returns fixed-response redirect to CloudFront               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

9.3 Blue/Green Deployments

Zero-Downtime Deployment Strategy
# STEP 1: Current Production (Blue Environment)
# Target Group: anihpj-blue-tg
# Instances: anihpj-blue-1, anihpj-blue-2, anihpj-blue-3, anihpj-blue-4
# ALB forwards 100% traffic to Blue

# STEP 2: Deploy New Version to Green Environment
# Launch new instances with updated code
aws autoscaling create-auto-scaling-group \
    --auto-scaling-group-name anihpj-green-asg \
    --launch-template LaunchTemplateName=anihpj-web-v2-template \
    --min-size 4 --max-size 4 --desired-capacity 4 \
    --target-group-arns arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-green-tg/xyz \
    --vpc-zone-identifier "subnet-0abc123,subnet-0def456"

# Wait for instances to pass health checks
aws elbv2 wait target-in-service \
    --target-group-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-green-tg/xyz

# STEP 3: Test Green Environment
# Create test listener rule with specific header
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 1 \
    --conditions Field=http-header,HttpHeaderName=X-Test-Version,Values='green' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$GREEN_TG_ARN

# Test: curl -H "X-Test-Version: green" https://anihpj.com

# STEP 4: Switch 10% Traffic to Green (Canary Release)
aws elbv2 modify-rule \
    --rule-arn $DEFAULT_RULE_ARN \
    --actions Type=forward,ForwardConfig='{
        "TargetGroups": [
            {"TargetGroupArn": "'$BLUE_TG_ARN'", "Weight": 90},
            {"TargetGroupArn": "'$GREEN_TG_ARN'", "Weight": 10}
        ]
    }'

# Monitor for 15 minutes, check error rates

# STEP 5: Switch 100% Traffic to Green
aws elbv2 modify-rule \
    --rule-arn $DEFAULT_RULE_ARN \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$GREEN_TG_ARN

# STEP 6: Keep Blue Environment for Rollback (30 min)
# If issues detected, instant rollback:
# aws elbv2 modify-rule --rule-arn $DEFAULT_RULE_ARN --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$BLUE_TG_ARN

# STEP 7: Terminate Blue Environment (after 30 min of stable Green)
aws autoscaling delete-auto-scaling-group \
    --auto-scaling-group-name anihpj-blue-asg \
    --force-delete

🔧 10. Troubleshooting ▼

10.1 Common Issues & Solutions

🐛 Issue #1: Targets Failing Health Checks

Symptoms: Targets marked as "unhealthy", ALB returns 503 errors

Causes & Solutions:

Cause Check Solution
Django not running ps aux | grep gunicorn Start Gunicorn: gunicorn -c gunicorn_config.py anihpj.wsgi
Wrong port netstat -tlnp | grep 8000 Verify target group port matches Gunicorn bind port
Security group blocks ALB Check inbound rules on EC2 SG Allow port 8000 from ALB security group
/health/ endpoint error curl localhost:8000/health/ Fix health check view, ensure returns 200
Database connection failed Check RDS security group Allow 5432 from EC2 security group
Health check timeout Response time > 5 seconds Optimize health check (remove DB queries), or increase timeout
Debug Commands:
# Check target health status
aws elbv2 describe-target-health \
    --target-group-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/xyz

# Output shows:
# - TargetHealth.State: healthy, unhealthy, initial, draining
# - TargetHealth.Reason: Target.ResponseCodeMismatch, Target.Timeout, Target.FailedHealthChecks
# - TargetHealth.Description: Details about failure

# SSH to instance and test locally
ssh ubuntu@ec2-instance
curl localhost:8000/health/
# Should return HTTP 200

# Check Gunicorn logs
tail -f /var/log/anihpj/error.log

# Check Django logs
tail -f /var/log/anihpj/django.log
🐛 Issue #2: 502 Bad Gateway Errors

Symptoms: ALB returns 502, but targets show as healthy

Causes:

  • Application crashed mid-request: Target became unhealthy during request processing
  • Connection timeout: Target didn't respond within idle timeout (default 60s)
  • Keep-alive timeout mismatch: Backend closes connection before ALB

Solution:

# Fix keep-alive timeout in Gunicorn
# gunicorn_config.py
keepalive = 5  # Must be < ALB idle timeout (60s)
timeout = 120  # For long-running requests

# Also increase ALB idle timeout if needed
aws elbv2 modify-load-balancer-attributes \
    --load-balancer-arn $ALB_ARN \
    --attributes Key=idle_timeout.timeout_seconds,Value=120
🐛 Issue #3: Session Loss / Users Getting Logged Out

Symptoms: Users randomly logged out, lose shopping cart, session data missing

Cause: Different instances have different session data (file-based sessions)

Solution: Use centralized session storage

# settings.py - Use Redis for sessions
SESSION_ENGINE = 'django.contrib.sessions.backends.cache'
SESSION_CACHE_ALIAS = 'default'

CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': 'redis://anihpj-redis.abc123.cache.amazonaws.com:6379/1',
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
        }
    }
}

# Alternative: Enable sticky sessions (less recommended)
# aws elbv2 modify-target-group-attributes \
#     --target-group-arn $TG_ARN \
#     --attributes Key=stickiness.enabled,Value=true Key=stickiness.lb_cookie.duration_seconds,Value=86400

10.2 Diagnostic Tools

CloudWatch Insights Queries
# Query 1: Find slowest endpoints
fields @timestamp, request_url, target_processing_time
| filter target_processing_time > 2
| sort target_processing_time desc
| limit 20

# Query 2: Error rate by status code
fields @timestamp, elb_status_code, request_url
| stats count() by elb_status_code
| filter elb_status_code like /5\d\d/

# Query 3: Traffic distribution by target
fields @timestamp, target_ip, target_port
| stats count() by target_ip
| sort count() desc

# Query 4: Identify clients with high request rate
fields @timestamp, client_ip
| stats count() as request_count by client_ip
| filter request_count > 1000
| sort request_count desc

10.3 ELB Error Codes Reference

Error Code Meaning Cause Fix
HTTP 400 Bad Request Malformed request (invalid headers) Check client request format
HTTP 460 Client Closed Connection Client closed before ALB response Increase client timeout
HTTP 463 X-Forwarded-For > 30 IPs Too many proxy hops Remove unnecessary proxies
HTTP 500 Internal Server Error Application error on target Check Django logs, fix app error
HTTP 502 Bad Gateway Target connection failed/timeout Fix keep-alive, check target health
HTTP 503 Service Unavailable No healthy targets available Fix target health checks
HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout Target didn't respond in time Increase idle timeout, optimize app
HTTP 561 Unauthorized Target returned auth error to ALB Check target auth configuration
📊 Debugging Workflow:
  1. Check CloudWatch metrics for error spike patterns
  2. Review ALB access logs in S3 for error codes
  3. Describe target health to see failure reasons
  4. SSH to failing instance, test health endpoint locally
  5. Check application logs (Gunicorn, Django, system)
  6. Verify security groups allow ALB → Target communication
  7. Test with curl from ALB subnet to target

⌨️ 11. API & CLI Reference ▼

11.1 Essential AWS CLI Commands

Load Balancer Management
# List all load balancers
aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers

# Get specific load balancer details
aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers \
    --names anihpj-alb \
    --query 'LoadBalancers[0].[LoadBalancerName,DNSName,State.Code,VpcId]' \
    --output table

# Delete load balancer
aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc123

# Modify load balancer attributes
aws elbv2 modify-load-balancer-attributes \
    --load-balancer-arn $ALB_ARN \
    --attributes \
        Key=deletion_protection.enabled,Value=true \
        Key=idle_timeout.timeout_seconds,Value=120 \
        Key=routing.http.drop_invalid_header_fields.enabled,Value=true \
        Key=routing.http2.enabled,Value=true

# Add tags
aws elbv2 add-tags \
    --resource-arns $ALB_ARN \
    --tags Key=Environment,Value=production Key=Cost-Center,Value=engineering
Target Group Operations
# List all target groups
aws elbv2 describe-target-groups

# Describe specific target group
aws elbv2 describe-target-groups \
    --names anihpj-web-tg

# Check target health
aws elbv2 describe-target-health \
    --target-group-arn $TG_ARN \
    --query 'TargetHealthDescriptions[*].[Target.Id,TargetHealth.State,TargetHealth.Reason]' \
    --output table

# Register targets
aws elbv2 register-targets \
    --target-group-arn $TG_ARN \
    --targets Id=i-0abc123,Port=8000 Id=i-0def456,Port=8000

# Deregister targets
aws elbv2 deregister-targets \
    --target-group-arn $TG_ARN \
    --targets Id=i-0abc123

# Modify target group health check
aws elbv2 modify-target-group \
    --target-group-arn $TG_ARN \
    --health-check-protocol HTTP \
    --health-check-path /health/ \
    --health-check-interval-seconds 30 \
    --health-check-timeout-seconds 5 \
    --healthy-threshold-count 2 \
    --unhealthy-threshold-count 3 \
    --matcher HttpCode=200

# Delete target group
aws elbv2 delete-target-group \
    --target-group-arn $TG_ARN
Listener & Rules Management
# List listeners
aws elbv2 describe-listeners \
    --load-balancer-arn $ALB_ARN

# List rules for a listener
aws elbv2 describe-rules \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --query 'Rules[*].[Priority,Conditions,Actions]' \
    --output table

# Create listener rule
aws elbv2 create-rule \
    --listener-arn $LISTENER_ARN \
    --priority 10 \
    --conditions Field=path-pattern,Values='/api/*' \
    --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=$API_TG_ARN

# Modify rule priority
aws elbv2 set-rule-priorities \
    --rule-priorities RuleArn=$RULE_ARN,Priority=5

# Delete rule
aws elbv2 delete-rule \
    --rule-arn $RULE_ARN

11.2 Boto3 (Python SDK) Examples

Django Management Command: Monitor ELB Health
# anihpj/management/commands/monitor_elb.py
import boto3
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django.core.mail import send_mail

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Monitor ELB target health and send alerts'

    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        # Initialize boto3 client
        elb_client = boto3.client('elbv2', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Target group ARN
        target_group_arn = 'arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/abc123'
        
        try:
            # Get target health
            response = elb_client.describe_target_health(
                TargetGroupArn=target_group_arn
            )
            
            healthy_count = 0
            unhealthy_targets = []
            
            for target in response['TargetHealthDescriptions']:
                target_id = target['Target']['Id']
                health_state = target['TargetHealth']['State']
                
                if health_state == 'healthy':
                    healthy_count += 1
                else:
                    unhealthy_targets.append({
                        'id': target_id,
                        'state': health_state,
                        'reason': target['TargetHealth'].get('Reason', 'Unknown')
                    })
            
            self.stdout.write(f"Healthy targets: {healthy_count}")
            self.stdout.write(f"Unhealthy targets: {len(unhealthy_targets)}")
            
            # Alert if unhealthy targets
            if unhealthy_targets:
                self.stdout.write(self.style.ERROR('ALERT: Unhealthy targets detected!'))
                for target in unhealthy_targets:
                    self.stdout.write(f"  - {target['id']}: {target['state']} ({target['reason']})")
                
                # Send email alert
                send_mail(
                    subject='[ALERT] anihpj ELB Unhealthy Targets',
                    message=f'Unhealthy targets detected:\n\n{unhealthy_targets}',
                    from_email='alerts@anihpj.com',
                    recipient_list=['devops@anihpj.com'],
                    fail_silently=False,
                )
            
            # Check if critically low healthy count
            if healthy_count < 2:
                self.stdout.write(self.style.ERROR('CRITICAL: Less than 2 healthy targets!'))
                send_mail(
                    subject='[CRITICAL] anihpj ELB Critical Health Issue',
                    message=f'Only {healthy_count} healthy targets remaining!',
                    from_email='alerts@anihpj.com',
                    recipient_list=['devops@anihpj.com'],
                    fail_silently=False,
                )
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.stdout.write(self.style.ERROR(f'Error monitoring ELB: {str(e)}'))
Run Monitor as Cron Job
# Add to crontab (run every 5 minutes)
*/5 * * * * cd /home/ubuntu/anihpj && /home/ubuntu/anihpj/venv/bin/python manage.py monitor_elb >> /var/log/anihpj/elb-monitor.log 2>&1
Automated Blue/Green Deployment Script
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import boto3
import time
import sys

def blue_green_deployment(listener_arn, blue_tg_arn, green_tg_arn):
    """
    Perform blue/green deployment with gradual traffic shift.
    """
    elb_client = boto3.client('elbv2', region_name='us-east-1')
    
    print("Step 1: Testing green environment health...")
    
    # Check green targets are healthy
    green_health = elb_client.describe_target_health(TargetGroupArn=green_tg_arn)
    healthy_green = sum(1 for t in green_health['TargetHealthDescriptions'] 
                       if t['TargetHealth']['State'] == 'healthy')
    
    if healthy_green < 2:
        print(f"ERROR: Only {healthy_green} healthy green targets. Aborting.")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    print(f"✅ {healthy_green} healthy green targets")
    
    # Get default listener rule
    rules = elb_client.describe_rules(ListenerArn=listener_arn)
    default_rule = next(r for r in rules['Rules'] if r['IsDefault'])
    default_rule_arn = default_rule['RuleArn']
    
    print("\nStep 2: Shift 10% traffic to green (canary)...")
    elb_client.modify_rule(
        RuleArn=default_rule_arn,
        Actions=[{
            'Type': 'forward',
            'ForwardConfig': {
                'TargetGroups': [
                    {'TargetGroupArn': blue_tg_arn, 'Weight': 90},
                    {'TargetGroupArn': green_tg_arn, 'Weight': 10}
                ]
            }
        }]
    )
    print("✅ 10% traffic shifted to green")
    
    print("\nStep 3: Monitor for 5 minutes...")
    time.sleep(300)  # 5 minutes
    
    # Check for errors in green
    cw_client = boto3.client('cloudwatch', region_name='us-east-1')
    metrics = cw_client.get_metric_statistics(
        Namespace='AWS/ApplicationELB',
        MetricName='HTTPCode_Target_5XX_Count',
        Dimensions=[{'Name': 'TargetGroup', 'Value': green_tg_arn.split(':')[-1]}],
        StartTime=time.time() - 300,
        EndTime=time.time(),
        Period=300,
        Statistics=['Sum']
    )
    
    errors = metrics['Datapoints'][0]['Sum'] if metrics['Datapoints'] else 0
    
    if errors > 10:
        print(f"❌ Too many errors ({errors}) in green. Rolling back...")
        elb_client.modify_rule(
            RuleArn=default_rule_arn,
            Actions=[{'Type': 'forward', 'TargetGroupArn': blue_tg_arn}]
        )
        sys.exit(1)
    
    print(f"✅ Green environment stable ({errors} errors)")
    
    print("\nStep 4: Shift 100% traffic to green...")
    elb_client.modify_rule(
        RuleArn=default_rule_arn,
        Actions=[{'Type': 'forward', 'TargetGroupArn': green_tg_arn}]
    )
    print("✅ Deployment complete! 100% traffic on green")
    
    print("\nKeep blue environment for 30 min rollback window...")
    print("To rollback, run: aws elbv2 modify-rule --rule-arn {} --actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn={}".format(
        default_rule_arn, blue_tg_arn
    ))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    LISTENER_ARN = 'arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:listener/app/anihpj-alb/abc/123'
    BLUE_TG_ARN = 'arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:targetgroup/anihpj-blue-tg/xyz'
    GREEN_TG_ARN = 'arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789:targetgroup/anihpj-green-tg/xyz'
    
    blue_green_deployment(LISTENER_ARN, BLUE_TG_ARN, GREEN_TG_ARN)

11.3 CloudFormation Template

Complete ALB Stack for anihpj
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Application Load Balancer for anihpj Django job portal'

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC for ALB
  
  SubnetIds:
    Type: List
    Description: At least 2 subnets in different AZs
  
  CertificateArn:
    Type: String
    Description: ACM certificate ARN for HTTPS

Resources:
  # Security Group for ALB
  ALBSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupName: anihpj-alb-sg
      GroupDescription: Security group for anihpj ALB
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow HTTP from internet
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow HTTPS from internet
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: anihpj-alb-sg
  
  # Application Load Balancer
  ApplicationLoadBalancer:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer
    Properties:
      Name: anihpj-alb
      Type: application
      Scheme: internet-facing
      IpAddressType: ipv4
      Subnets: !Ref SubnetIds
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref ALBSecurityGroup
      LoadBalancerAttributes:
        - Key: deletion_protection.enabled
          Value: 'true'
        - Key: idle_timeout.timeout_seconds
          Value: '60'
        - Key: routing.http.drop_invalid_header_fields.enabled
          Value: 'true'
        - Key: routing.http2.enabled
          Value: 'true'
      Tags:
        - Key: Application
          Value: anihpj
        - Key: Environment
          Value: production
  
  # Target Group for Web Servers
  WebTargetGroup:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup
    Properties:
      Name: anihpj-web-tg
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      Protocol: HTTP
      Port: 8000
      TargetType: instance
      HealthCheckEnabled: true
      HealthCheckProtocol: HTTP
      HealthCheckPath: /health/
      HealthCheckIntervalSeconds: 30
      HealthCheckTimeoutSeconds: 5
      HealthyThresholdCount: 2
      UnhealthyThresholdCount: 3
      Matcher:
        HttpCode: '200'
      TargetGroupAttributes:
        - Key: deregistration_delay.timeout_seconds
          Value: '30'
        - Key: stickiness.enabled
          Value: 'true'
        - Key: stickiness.type
          Value: lb_cookie
        - Key: stickiness.lb_cookie.duration_seconds
          Value: '86400'
      Tags:
        - Key: Service
          Value: web
  
  # HTTPS Listener
  HTTPSListener:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::Listener
    Properties:
      LoadBalancerArn: !Ref ApplicationLoadBalancer
      Protocol: HTTPS
      Port: 443
      Certificates:
        - CertificateArn: !Ref CertificateArn
      SslPolicy: ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06
      DefaultActions:
        - Type: forward
          TargetGroupArn: !Ref WebTargetGroup
  
  # HTTP Listener (redirect to HTTPS)
  HTTPListener:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::Listener
    Properties:
      LoadBalancerArn: !Ref ApplicationLoadBalancer
      Protocol: HTTP
      Port: 80
      DefaultActions:
        - Type: redirect
          RedirectConfig:
            Protocol: HTTPS
            Port: '443'
            StatusCode: HTTP_301

Outputs:
  LoadBalancerDNS:
    Description: DNS name of the load balancer
    Value: !GetAtt ApplicationLoadBalancer.DNSName
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ALB-DNS'
  
  LoadBalancerArn:
    Description: ARN of the load balancer
    Value: !Ref ApplicationLoadBalancer
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ALB-ARN'
  
  TargetGroupArn:
    Description: ARN of the target group
    Value: !Ref WebTargetGroup
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-TG-ARN'
  
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: Security group ID for ALB
    Value: !Ref ALBSecurityGroup
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-SG-ID'
Deploy Stack
aws cloudformation create-stack \
    --stack-name anihpj-alb-stack \
    --template-body file://alb-stack.yaml \
    --parameters \
        ParameterKey=VpcId,ParameterValue=vpc-0xyz789 \
        ParameterKey=SubnetIds,ParameterValue=subnet-0abc123\\,subnet-0def456 \
        ParameterKey=CertificateArn,ParameterValue=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789:certificate/abc-123 \
    --tags Key=Application,Value=anihpj

# Wait for stack creation
aws cloudformation wait stack-create-complete \
    --stack-name anihpj-alb-stack

# Get outputs
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name anihpj-alb-stack \
    --query 'Stacks[0].Outputs' \
    --output table

11.4 Terraform Configuration

main.tf - Complete ALB Setup
# Security Group for ALB
resource "aws_security_group" "alb_sg" {
  name        = "anihpj-alb-sg"
  description = "Security group for anihpj ALB"
  vpc_id      = var.vpc_id

  ingress {
    description = "HTTP from internet"
    from_port   = 80
    to_port     = 80
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  ingress {
    description = "HTTPS from internet"
    from_port   = 443
    to_port     = 443
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-alb-sg"
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# Application Load Balancer
resource "aws_lb" "anihpj_alb" {
  name               = "anihpj-alb"
  internal           = false
  load_balancer_type = "application"
  security_groups    = [aws_security_group.alb_sg.id]
  subnets            = var.subnet_ids

  enable_deletion_protection = true
  enable_http2              = true

  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-alb"
    Application = "anihpj"
    Environment = "production"
  }
}

# Target Group
resource "aws_lb_target_group" "web_tg" {
  name     = "anihpj-web-tg"
  port     = 8000
  protocol = "HTTP"
  vpc_id   = var.vpc_id

  health_check {
    enabled             = true
    healthy_threshold   = 2
    unhealthy_threshold = 3
    timeout             = 5
    interval            = 30
    path                = "/health/"
    protocol            = "HTTP"
    matcher             = "200"
  }

  deregistration_delay = 30

  stickiness {
    type            = "lb_cookie"
    cookie_duration = 86400
    enabled         = true
  }

  tags = {
    Service = "web"
  }
}

# HTTPS Listener
resource "aws_lb_listener" "https" {
  load_balancer_arn = aws_lb.anihpj_alb.arn
  port              = "443"
  protocol          = "HTTPS"
  ssl_policy        = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06"
  certificate_arn   = var.certificate_arn

  default_action {
    type             = "forward"
    target_group_arn = aws_lb_target_group.web_tg.arn
  }
}

# HTTP Listener (redirect to HTTPS)
resource "aws_lb_listener" "http" {
  load_balancer_arn = aws_lb.anihpj_alb.arn
  port              = "80"
  protocol          = "HTTP"

  default_action {
    type = "redirect"
    redirect {
      port        = "443"
      protocol    = "HTTPS"
      status_code = "HTTP_301"
    }
  }
}

# Outputs
output "alb_dns_name" {
  description = "DNS name of the load balancer"
  value       = aws_lb.anihpj_alb.dns_name
}

output "alb_arn" {
  description = "ARN of the load balancer"
  value       = aws_lb.anihpj_alb.arn
}

output "target_group_arn" {
  description = "ARN of the target group"
  value       = aws_lb_target_group.web_tg.arn
}
Apply Terraform
terraform init
terraform plan -var-file="production.tfvars"
terraform apply -var-file="production.tfvars"

🔗 12. Integration with Other AWS Services ▼

12.1 EC2 & Auto Scaling Integration

Covered in Section 6.1 - Auto Scaling automatically registers/deregisters instances with target groups.

12.2 Amazon ECS Integration

Run anihpj as ECS Service with ALB
{
  "family": "anihpj-task",
  "networkMode": "awsvpc",
  "requiresCompatibilities": ["FARGATE"],
  "cpu": "512",
  "memory": "1024",
  "containerDefinitions": [
    {
      "name": "anihpj-web",
      "image": "123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj:latest",
      "portMappings": [
        {
          "containerPort": 8000,
          "protocol": "tcp"
        }
      ],
      "environment": [
        {"name": "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", "value": "anihpj.settings.production"},
        {"name": "DATABASE_URL", "value": "postgresql://..."}
      ],
      "logConfiguration": {
        "logDriver": "awslogs",
        "options": {
          "awslogs-group": "/ecs/anihpj",
          "awslogs-region": "us-east-1",
          "awslogs-stream-prefix": "web"
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
Create ECS Service with ALB
aws ecs create-service \
    --cluster anihpj-cluster \
    --service-name anihpj-web-service \
    --task-definition anihpj-task:1 \
    --desired-count 4 \
    --launch-type FARGATE \
    --network-configuration "awsvpcConfiguration={
        subnets=[subnet-0abc123,subnet-0def456],
        securityGroups=[sg-0ecs123],
        assignPublicIp=DISABLED
    }" \
    --load-balancers "targetGroupArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:targetgroup/anihpj-web-tg/xyz,containerName=anihpj-web,containerPort=8000" \
    --health-check-grace-period-seconds 60

12.3 Route 53 Integration

Create Alias Record for ALB
# Get ALB hosted zone ID (constants for each region)
# us-east-1: Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K
# us-east-2: Z3AADJGX6KTTL2
# us-west-1: Z368ELLRRE2KJ0
# us-west-2: Z1H1FL5HABSF5

# Create Route 53 hosted zone record
cat > change-batch.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Changes": [
    {
      "Action": "CREATE",
      "ResourceRecordSet": {
        "Name": "anihpj.com",
        "Type": "A",
        "AliasTarget": {
          "HostedZoneId": "Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K",
          "DNSName": "anihpj-alb-123456789.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
          "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "Action": "CREATE",
      "ResourceRecordSet": {
        "Name": "www.anihpj.com",
        "Type": "A",
        "AliasTarget": {
          "HostedZoneId": "Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K",
          "DNSName": "anihpj-alb-123456789.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
          "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
EOF

aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch file://change-batch.json

12.4 CloudWatch Monitoring Integration

CloudWatch Dashboard for ELB
cat > dashboard.json << 'EOF'
{
  "widgets": [
    {
      "type": "metric",
      "properties": {
        "metrics": [
          ["AWS/ApplicationELB", "TargetResponseTime", {"stat": "Average"}],
          [".", "RequestCount", {"stat": "Sum"}]
        ],
        "period": 300,
        "stat": "Average",
        "region": "us-east-1",
        "title": "ALB Performance"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "metric",
      "properties": {
        "metrics": [
          ["AWS/ApplicationELB", "HealthyHostCount", {"stat": "Average"}],
          [".", "UnHealthyHostCount", {"stat": "Average"}]
        ],
        "period": 60,
        "stat": "Average",
        "region": "us-east-1",
        "title": "Target Health"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "metric",
      "properties": {
        "metrics": [
          ["AWS/ApplicationELB", "HTTPCode_Target_5XX_Count", {"stat": "Sum"}],
          [".", "HTTPCode_ELB_5XX_Count", {"stat": "Sum"}],
          [".", "HTTPCode_Target_4XX_Count", {"stat": "Sum"}]
        ],
        "period": 300,
        "stat": "Sum",
        "region": "us-east-1",
        "title": "Error Rates"
      }
    }
  ]
}
EOF

aws cloudwatch put-dashboard \
    --dashboard-name anihpj-alb-dashboard \
    --dashboard-body file://dashboard.json

🔐 13. Security & Compliance ▼

13.1 Security Groups Configuration

Recommended Security Group Setup
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ALB SECURITY GROUP (anihpj-alb-sg)                          │
│                                                               │
│  Inbound:                                                     │
│  • HTTP (80) from 0.0.0.0/0                                  │
│  • HTTPS (443) from 0.0.0.0/0                                │
│                                                               │
│  Outbound:                                                    │
│  • Port 8000 to EC2 Security Group (anihpj-ec2-sg)          │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                            │
                            ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  EC2 SECURITY GROUP (anihpj-ec2-sg)                          │
│                                                               │
│  Inbound:                                                     │
│  • Port 8000 from ALB Security Group (anihpj-alb-sg)         │
│  • Port 22 from Bastion/Admin IP (203.0.113.50/32)          │
│                                                               │
│  Outbound:                                                    │
│  • Port 5432 to RDS Security Group (anihpj-rds-sg)           │
│  • Port 6379 to Redis Security Group (anihpj-redis-sg)      │
│  • Port 443 to 0.0.0.0/0 (for external API calls)           │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

13.2 SSL/TLS Certificates Management

SSL Certificate Best Practices
  1. Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM): Free, auto-renewable certificates
  2. Request Wildcard Certificate: *.anihpj.com covers all subdomains
  3. Enable Auto-Renewal: ACM automatically renews before expiration
  4. Use Latest Security Policy: ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06
  5. Monitor Certificate Expiration: CloudWatch alarm 30 days before expiry

13.3 Compliance & Audit

Compliance Features
  • Access Logs: Complete audit trail of all requests
  • CloudTrail Integration: Log all API calls to ELB
  • WAF Integration: PCI DSS, HIPAA compliance
  • Encryption in Transit: TLS 1.3 for all connections
  • VPC Flow Logs: Network traffic analysis
  • AWS Config: Track configuration changes
🎓 Key Takeaways for anihpj:
  • ✅ Use Application Load Balancer (ALB) for Django web applications
  • ✅ Deploy across at least 2 Availability Zones for high availability
  • ✅ Implement Auto Scaling with ELB health checks
  • ✅ Use HTTPS only with ACM certificates
  • ✅ Enable WAF for security protection
  • ✅ Store sessions in Redis/ElastiCache, not files
  • ✅ Configure health checks at /health/ endpoint
  • ✅ Use path-based routing for microservices architecture
  • ✅ Enable access logs for troubleshooting and compliance
  • ✅ Monitor with CloudWatch metrics and alarms

Amazon Route 53

Category: Networking & Content Delivery (DNS)  |  Use Case: Domain Registration, DNS Management, Traffic Routing  |  Pricing: Pay-per-query, $0.50/hosted zone/month  |  Last Updated: December 2025  |  Documentation: AWS Route 53 Docs

Highly available and scalable Domain Name System (DNS) web service designed to route end users to your anihpj job portal with ultra-low latency. Provides domain registration, DNS routing, and health checking in a single, reliable service.

📋 Overview & Introduction

Amazon Route 53 is a highly available and scalable DNS (Domain Name System) web service. It connects user requests to infrastructure running on AWS (such as EC2 instances, ELB load balancers, or S3 buckets) and can also route users to infrastructure outside of AWS. Route 53 is named after the well-known port 53 used by DNS services.

🎯 Key Benefits
  • High Availability: 100% availability SLA with globally distributed DNS servers
  • Low Latency: Anycast routing ensures users connect to nearest DNS server
  • Flexible Routing: 7 routing policies (simple, weighted, latency, failover, geolocation, geoproximity, multivalue)
  • Health Checks: Monitor endpoint health and automatically route traffic away from unhealthy resources
  • Domain Registration: Register and manage domains directly in Route 53
  • Cost-Effective: Pay only for what you use, no minimum fees
  • Integrated: Seamlessly works with all AWS services
🚀 Quick Start: Set Up anihpj.com in 10 Minutes
1 Create Hosted Zone: aws route53 create-hosted-zone --name anihpj.com
2 Get Name Servers: Note the 4 NS records provided by AWS
3 Update Domain Registrar: Point your domain to Route 53 name servers
4 Create A Record: Point anihpj.com to your ALB or EC2
5 Test: nslookup anihpj.com or dig anihpj.com
💡 When to Use Route 53 for anihpj: Essential for professional job portals. Route 53 provides your domain (anihpj.com) with DNS management, enables global traffic distribution, supports disaster recovery with automatic failover, and offers health checks to ensure users always reach available servers. Perfect for production deployments requiring 99.99% uptime.
🎯 DNS Provider Quick Comparison
Route 53: 100% SLA, 7 routing policies, native AWS
Cloudflare: Free DNS, CDN bundled, DDoS protection
GoDaddy: Domain registrar focus, basic DNS only
Google Cloud DNS: Low cost, GCP integration, 3 policies
Azure DNS: Microsoft Azure native, 99.99% SLA
NS1: Advanced traffic steering, real-time filters
⚖️ Route 53 vs Other DNS Providers (Detailed)
Feature Route 53 Cloudflare GoDaddy DNS Google Cloud DNS
Availability SLA 100% 99.99% No SLA 100%
Routing Policies 7 types Load balancing only Basic only 3 types
Health Checks Built-in Paid tier only Not available Not available
AWS Integration Native (Alias records) Via CNAME Via A/CNAME Limited
Cost (1M queries) $0.40 Free $14.99/month $0.20
Best For AWS workloads CDN + DNS combo Small websites GCP workloads
🏗️ Architecture Diagram
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           ROUTE 53 DNS ARCHITECTURE                                                  │
│                        (anihpj.com Job Portal with Multi-Region Setup)                               │
│                                                                                                      │
│  👥 Global Job Seekers & Employers                                                                   │
│  │                                                                                                   │
│  │ (1) User types: anihpj.com                                                                        │
│  ▼                                                                                                   │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │  🌐 ROUTE 53 HOSTED ZONE: anihpj.com                                                            ││
│  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ││
│  │  │  DNS Records (Zone File)                                                                   │ ││
│  │  │                                                                                             │ ││
│  │  │  📝 A Record (IPv4):                                                                       │ ││
│  │  │  anihpj.com → 52.45.123.89 (Primary ALB - us-east-1)                                      │ ││
│  │  │  TTL: 60 seconds | Routing Policy: Failover (Primary)                                     │ ││
│  │  │                                                                                             │ ││
│  │  │  📝 AAAA Record (IPv6):                                                                    │ ││
│  │  │  anihpj.com → 2600:1f18:6a4:3c00::1                                                        │ ││
│  │  │                                                                                             │ ││
│  │  │  📝 Alias Record (AWS Native):                                                             │ ││
│  │  │  anihpj.com → anihpj-alb-123.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com                                  │ ││
│  │  │  Type: A Record | Evaluate Target Health: Yes | No charge for queries                     │ ││
│  │  │                                                                                             │ ││
│  │  │  📝 CNAME Records:                                                                         │ ││
│  │  │  www.anihpj.com → anihpj.com                                                               │ ││
│  │  │  api.anihpj.com → anihpj-api-alb-456.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com                          │ ││
│  │  │  cdn.anihpj.com → d1a2b3c4d5.cloudfront.net                                                │ ││
│  │  │                                                                                             │ ││
│  │  │  📝 MX Records (Email):                                                                    │ ││
│  │  │  anihpj.com → 10 mail.anihpj.com                                                           │ ││
│  │  │                                                                                             │ ││
│  │  │  📝 TXT Records (Verification):                                                            │ ││
│  │  │  _dmarc.anihpj.com → "v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:dmarc@anihpj.com"               │ ││
│  │  │  anihpj.com → "google-site-verification=abc123"                                            │ ││
│  │  │                                                                                             │ ││
│  │  │  💚 Health Checks:                                                                         │ ││
│  │  │  • Primary ALB (us-east-1): HTTPS://anihpj.com/health/ → ✅ Healthy                       │ ││
│  │  │  • Secondary ALB (eu-west-1): HTTPS://eu.anihpj.com/health/ → ✅ Healthy (Standby)        │ ││
│  │  │  Check Interval: 30 seconds | Threshold: 3 failures = Unhealthy                           │ ││
│  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ││
│  │                                                                                                  ││
│  │  (2) Route 53 evaluates routing policy                                                          ││
│  │  (3) Returns IP address of closest/healthiest endpoint                                          ││
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                        │                                                              │
│                   ┌────────────────────┴────────────────────┐                                        │
│                   ▼                                         ▼                                        │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐          │
│  │  🌍 PRIMARY REGION: US-EAST-1          │   │  🌍 SECONDARY REGION: EU-WEST-1        │          │
│  │  (Failover Primary)                     │   │  (Failover Secondary)                   │          │
│  │                                         │   │                                         │          │
│  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────┐ │   │  ┌───────────────────────────────────┐ │          │
│  │  │  🌐 Application Load Balancer      │ │   │  │  🌐 Application Load Balancer      │ │          │
│  │  │  anihpj-alb (Primary)              │ │   │  │  anihpj-alb-eu (Secondary)         │ │          │
│  │  │  Status: ✅ Healthy                 │ │   │  │  Status: ✅ Healthy (Standby)      │ │          │
│  │  └───────────────────────────────────┘ │   │  └───────────────────────────────────┘ │          │
│  │            │                            │   │            │                            │          │
│  │            ▼                            │   │            ▼                            │          │
│  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────┐ │   │  ┌───────────────────────────────────┐ │          │
│  │  │  Django App Servers (3 instances) │ │   │  │  Django App Servers (3 instances) │ │          │
│  │  │  • EC2 Auto Scaling Group         │ │   │  │  • EC2 Auto Scaling Group         │ │          │
│  │  │  • JobPost views, Search API      │ │   │  │  • JobPost views, Search API      │ │          │
│  │  └───────────────────────────────────┘ │   │  └───────────────────────────────────┘ │          │
│  │            │                            │   │            │                            │          │
│  │            ▼                            │   │            ▼                            │          │
│  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────┐ │   │  ┌───────────────────────────────────┐ │          │
│  │  │  RDS PostgreSQL (Multi-AZ)        │ │   │  │  RDS PostgreSQL (Read Replica)    │ │          │
│  │  │  Primary Database                  │ ├───┼──▶  Cross-Region Replication         │ │          │
│  │  └───────────────────────────────────┘ │   │  └───────────────────────────────────┘ │          │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────┘   └─────────────────────────────────────────┘          │
│                                                                                                      │
│  📊 Traffic Flow Example:                                                                            │
│  1. User in New York → Route 53 → Latency-based routing → us-east-1 (35ms)                         │
│  2. User in London → Route 53 → Latency-based routing → eu-west-1 (12ms)                           │
│  3. If us-east-1 fails → Route 53 detects via health check → Auto failover to eu-west-1            │
│  4. DNS TTL = 60s → Changes propagate quickly                                                       │
│                                                                                                      │
│  💰 Monthly Cost Estimate:                                                                           │
│  • Hosted Zone: $0.50/month                                                                         │
│  • Queries: 10M queries × $0.40/1M = $4.00                                                          │
│  • Health Checks: 2 endpoints × $0.50 = $1.00                                                       │
│  • Total Route 53: ~$5.50/month                                                                     │
│                                                                                                      │
│  🔒 Security Features:                                                                               │
│  • DNSSEC for domain integrity                                                                      │
│  • IAM policies for access control                                                                  │
│  • CloudTrail logging for audit                                                                     │
│  • VPC private DNS for internal resolution                                                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🔧 2. Core Concepts ▼

Understanding Route 53 fundamentals for managing your anihpj domain effectively.

2.1 Hosted Zones

A hosted zone is a container for DNS records that define how to route traffic for a specific domain (e.g., anihpj.com) and its subdomains.

📁 Hosted Zone Types
Type Purpose Cost anihpj Use Case
Public Hosted Zone Routes internet traffic to public resources $0.50/month anihpj.com (job portal)
Private Hosted Zone Routes traffic within VPC (internal DNS) $0.50/month internal.anihpj.local (backend services)
Create Hosted Zone for anihpj.com
# Create public hosted zone
aws route53 create-hosted-zone \
    --name anihpj.com \
    --caller-reference "anihpj-$(date +%s)" \
    --hosted-zone-config Comment="Main job portal domain"

# Response includes 4 name servers:
# ns-123.awsdns-45.com
# ns-678.awsdns-90.net
# ns-1234.awsdns-56.org
# ns-5678.awsdns-12.co.uk

# Update these at your domain registrar

2.2 DNS Record Types

Route 53 supports all standard DNS record types plus AWS-specific Alias records.

📝 Essential Record Types for anihpj
Record Type Purpose Example TTL
A Maps domain to IPv4 address anihpj.com → 52.45.123.89 300s
AAAA Maps domain to IPv6 address anihpj.com → 2600:1f18... 300s
CNAME Alias to another domain www → anihpj.com 300s
Alias AWS resource (no charge) anihpj.com → ALB Auto
MX Mail server routing 10 mail.anihpj.com 3600s
TXT Verification/SPF/DMARC "v=spf1 include:..." 300s
NS Name server delegation subdomain NS records 172800s
Complete DNS Records for anihpj.com
# 1. Create Alias record to ALB (recommended for AWS resources)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K",
        "DNSName": "anihpj-alb-123.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# 2. Create www CNAME
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "www.anihpj.com",
      "Type": "CNAME",
      "TTL": 300,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "anihpj.com"}]
    }
  }]
}'

# 3. Create API subdomain
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "api.anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K",
        "DNSName": "anihpj-api-alb-456.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
      }
    }
  }]
}'

2.3 Routing Policies

Route 53 provides 7 routing policies to control how traffic is routed to your resources.

🎯 Routing Policies at a Glance
Simple: One resource, single IP address
Weighted: Traffic % distribution, A/B testing
Latency: Fastest response for global users
Failover: Primary/Secondary DR setup
Geolocation: Route by user's country/continent
Geoproximity: Distance + bias for traffic shaping
Multivalue: Simple DNS-based load balancing
🔀 Routing Policies Detailed Comparison
Policy Use Case How It Works anihpj Example
Simple Single resource Returns one IP Small site, single server
Weighted A/B testing, gradual rollout % of traffic to each 90% old, 10% new version
Latency Global users Lowest latency region US/EU users → nearest DC
Failover Disaster recovery Primary → Secondary us-east-1 → eu-west-1
Geolocation Regional content Based on user location EU users → GDPR-compliant DC
Geoproximity Traffic shaping Distance + bias Shift traffic to underutilized region
Multivalue Simple load balancing Random healthy IPs Return 8 healthy servers

2.4 Health Checks & Monitoring

Route 53 health checks monitor endpoint availability and automatically route traffic away from unhealthy resources.

💚 Health Check Configuration for anihpj
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ROUTE 53 HEALTH CHECK FLOW                         │
│                                                                       │
│  Every 30 seconds (or 10 seconds for fast interval):                 │
│  Route 53 → HTTPS GET /health/ → anihpj ALB                         │
│                                                                       │
│  ┌────────────────────┐                                              │
│  │  Health Check      │                                              │
│  │  Configuration     │                                              │
│  └────────┬───────────┘                                              │
│           │                                                           │
│           ├─→ Protocol: HTTPS                                        │
│           ├─→ Port: 443                                              │
│           ├─→ Path: /health/                                         │
│           ├─→ Interval: 30 seconds                                   │
│           ├─→ Failure Threshold: 3 consecutive failures              │
│           ├─→ String Matching: "status":"healthy" (optional)         │
│           ├─→ Measure Latency: Yes                                   │
│           └─→ Send SNS Alert: Yes (if unhealthy)                     │
│                                                                       │
│  Health States:                                                       │
│  ✅ Healthy: Endpoint responds 200 OK within 10 seconds              │
│  ⚠️  Checking: Initial state, waiting for enough data points         │
│  ❌ Unhealthy: 3 consecutive failures detected                       │
│                                                                       │
│  Actions on Unhealthy:                                                │
│  1. Route 53 stops routing traffic to this endpoint                  │
│  2. Failover to secondary region (if configured)                     │
│  3. CloudWatch alarm triggered                                       │
│  4. SNS notification sent to ops team                                │
│  5. Auto Scaling can launch replacement instances                    │
│                                                                       │
│  Cost: $0.50/endpoint/month (standard interval)                      │
│        $1.00/endpoint/month (fast 10-second interval)                │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Create Health Check for anihpj ALB
# Create health check
aws route53 create-health-check \
    --health-check-config \
        Protocol=HTTPS,\
        ResourcePath=/health/,\
        FullyQualifiedDomainName=anihpj.com,\
        Port=443,\
        Type=HTTPS,\
        RequestInterval=30,\
        FailureThreshold=3,\
        MeasureLatency=true,\
        EnableSNI=true \
    --caller-reference "anihpj-alb-health-$(date +%s)"

# Add CloudWatch alarm
aws route53 put-health-check-alarm \
    --health-check-id abc123-def456 \
    --alarm-name "anihpj-alb-unhealthy" \
    --comparison-operator LessThanThreshold \
    --evaluation-periods 1 \
    --threshold 1 \
    --statistic Minimum

# Configure SNS notification
aws sns subscribe \
    --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789:route53-health \
    --protocol email \
    --notification-endpoint ops@anihpj.com
🎓 Best Practice for anihpj: Use latency-based routing with health checks for global job seekers. Configure primary endpoint in us-east-1 and secondary in eu-west-1. Health checks ensure automatic failover if primary region becomes unavailable. This provides 99.99% uptime for your job portal.

🚀 3. Getting Started ▼

Step-by-step guide to configure DNS for your anihpj job portal.

3.1 Prerequisites

📋 Pre-Launch Checklist
  • ✅ AWS Account - Active with billing enabled
  • ✅ Domain Name - Registered (anihpj.com) at any registrar
  • ✅ EC2/ELB Resources - Running application infrastructure
  • ✅ IAM Permissions - route53:*, route53domains:* permissions
  • ✅ SSL Certificate - ACM certificate for HTTPS (optional but recommended)
  • ✅ Django Settings - ALLOWED_HOSTS configured for anihpj.com

3.2 Domain Registration

Register anihpj.com through Route 53 or transfer from another registrar.

Option 1: Register New Domain via Route 53
# Check domain availability
aws route53domains check-domain-availability \
    --domain-name anihpj.com

# Register domain (if available)
aws route53domains register-domain \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --duration-in-years 1 \
    --admin-contact \
        FirstName=Admin,LastName=User,\
        ContactType=PERSON,\
        AddressLine1="123 Main St",\
        City=Seattle,State=WA,\
        CountryCode=US,ZipCode=98101,\
        PhoneNumber="+1.2065551234",\
        Email=admin@anihpj.com \
    --registrant-contact [same as admin] \
    --tech-contact [same as admin] \
    --privacy-protect-admin-contact \
    --privacy-protect-registrant-contact \
    --privacy-protect-tech-contact \
    --auto-renew

# Cost: $12-13/year for .com domains
# Note: Hosted zone ($0.50/month) created automatically
Option 2: Transfer Existing Domain to Route 53
# 1. Unlock domain at current registrar
# 2. Get authorization code (EPP code)
# 3. Initiate transfer

aws route53domains transfer-domain \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --duration-in-years 1 \
    --auth-code "YOUR-EPP-CODE-FROM-REGISTRAR" \
    --admin-contact [...contact details...] \
    --auto-renew

# Transfer takes 5-7 days
# Domain remains functional during transfer

3.3 Hosted Zone Setup

⚡ Quick Setup: anihpj.com in 5 Minutes
  1. Create Hosted Zone → Get 4 name servers
  2. Update Registrar → Point to Route 53 name servers
  3. Wait for Propagation → 24-48 hours
  4. Create A Record → Point to ALB or EC2
  5. Test → dig anihpj.com +short
Console Setup (Web Interface)
AWS Console → Route 53 → Hosted zones → Create hosted zone

1. Domain name: anihpj.com
2. Type: Public hosted zone
3. Comment: Job portal main domain
4. Create hosted zone

Result: 4 Name Servers assigned
├─ ns-123.awsdns-45.com
├─ ns-678.awsdns-90.net
├─ ns-1234.awsdns-56.org
└─ ns-5678.awsdns-12.co.uk

Go to your domain registrar (GoDaddy, Namecheap, etc.)
→ Update nameservers with above 4 values
→ Save and wait 24-48 hours for propagation
CLI Setup (Automated)
# Create hosted zone
ZONE_ID=$(aws route53 create-hosted-zone \
    --name anihpj.com \
    --caller-reference "anihpj-$(date +%s)" \
    --hosted-zone-config Comment="anihpj job portal" \
    --query 'HostedZone.Id' \
    --output text)

echo "Created hosted zone: $ZONE_ID"

# Get name servers
aws route53 get-hosted-zone \
    --id $ZONE_ID \
    --query 'DelegationSet.NameServers' \
    --output table

# Output:
# --------------------------------
# |        NameServers          |
# +-----------------------------+
# |  ns-123.awsdns-45.com       |
# |  ns-678.awsdns-90.net       |
# |  ns-1234.awsdns-56.org      |
# |  ns-5678.awsdns-12.co.uk    |
# +-----------------------------+

3.4 Django Integration

Configure Django settings.py for anihpj domain.

anihpj/settings.py Configuration
# anihpj/settings.py

# ALLOWED_HOSTS - Critical for Django to accept requests
ALLOWED_HOSTS = [
    'anihpj.com',
    'www.anihpj.com',
    'api.anihpj.com',
    '*.anihpj.com',  # Wildcard for subdomains
    'localhost',
    '127.0.0.1',
]

# CSRF trusted origins (Django 4.0+)
CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS = [
    'https://anihpj.com',
    'https://www.anihpj.com',
    'https://api.anihpj.com',
]

# Force HTTPS redirect
SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT = True
SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True
CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True

# HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security)
SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 31536000  # 1 year
SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS = True
SECURE_HSTS_PRELOAD = True

# Custom domain email (optional)
if not DEBUG:
    DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL = 'noreply@anihpj.com'
    SERVER_EMAIL = 'errors@anihpj.com'
    
    # Email via SES
    EMAIL_BACKEND = 'django.core.mail.backends.smtp.EmailBackend'
    EMAIL_HOST = 'email-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'
    EMAIL_PORT = 587
    EMAIL_USE_TLS = True
    EMAIL_HOST_USER = os.environ.get('SES_SMTP_USER')
    EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD = os.environ.get('SES_SMTP_PASSWORD')
Health Check Endpoint
# jobpost/views.py

from django.http import JsonResponse
from django.db import connection
from django.core.cache import cache

def health_check(request):
    """
    Route 53 health check endpoint
    Returns 200 OK if all services are healthy
    """
    health_status = {
        'status': 'healthy',
        'checks': {}
    }
    
    # Check database
    try:
        with connection.cursor() as cursor:
            cursor.execute("SELECT 1")
        health_status['checks']['database'] = 'ok'
    except Exception as e:
        health_status['status'] = 'unhealthy'
        health_status['checks']['database'] = f'error: {str(e)}'
    
    # Check cache (Redis/ElastiCache)
    try:
        cache.set('health_check', 'ok', 10)
        cache.get('health_check')
        health_status['checks']['cache'] = 'ok'
    except Exception as e:
        health_status['checks']['cache'] = f'warning: {str(e)}'
    
    # Return appropriate status code
    status_code = 200 if health_status['status'] == 'healthy' else 503
    return JsonResponse(health_status, status=status_code)

# jobpost/urls.py
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('health/', views.health_check, name='health-check'),
    # ... other urls
]

3.5 Creating Your First Record

Create A Record Pointing to ALB
# Get your ALB DNS name
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers \
    --names anihpj-alb \
    --query 'LoadBalancers[0].DNSName' \
    --output text)

# Get ALB hosted zone ID
ALB_ZONE=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers \
    --names anihpj-alb \
    --query 'LoadBalancers[0].CanonicalHostedZoneId' \
    --output text)

# Create Alias record (recommended - no query charges)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "'$ALB_ZONE'",
        "DNSName": "'$ALB_DNS'",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# Create www subdomain
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "www.anihpj.com",
      "Type": "CNAME",
      "TTL": 300,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "anihpj.com"}]
    }
  }]
}'

3.6 Testing & Verification

Verify DNS Configuration
# Test DNS resolution
dig anihpj.com +short
# Should return: ALB IP address(es)

# Test with specific Route 53 name server
dig @ns-123.awsdns-45.com anihpj.com

# Check all record types
dig anihpj.com ANY

# Test HTTPS (if ACM certificate configured)
curl -I https://anihpj.com
# Should return: HTTP/2 200

# Test Django health endpoint
curl https://anihpj.com/health/
# Should return: {"status":"healthy","checks":{"database":"ok","cache":"ok"}}

# Monitor DNS propagation globally
# Visit: https://www.whatsmydns.net/#A/anihpj.com
⚠️ Common Issues:
  • 502 Bad Gateway → Check ALB target health and security groups
  • NXDOMAIN → Wait 24-48 hours for DNS propagation
  • DisallowedHost at / → Add domain to Django ALLOWED_HOSTS
  • Certificate Error → Request/import ACM certificate for anihpj.com

🔑 4. Key Features ▼

Advanced Route 53 capabilities for enterprise DNS management.

🌟 Route 53 Key Features at a Glance
Traffic Flow: Visual policy editor, complex routing ($50/month)
DNSSEC: Domain security, cryptographic signatures, spoofing protection
Resolver: Hybrid DNS, on-premises integration, VPC endpoints
Alias Records: FREE queries to AWS resources (ALB, CloudFront, S3)
Query Logging: CloudWatch Logs integration, security monitoring
Health Checks: Endpoint monitoring, automatic failover, CloudWatch alarms

4.1 Traffic Flow (Visual Policy Builder)

Create complex routing policies with a drag-and-drop visual editor.

🎨 Traffic Flow Use Cases
  • Multi-Region Failover - Primary (us-east-1) → Secondary (eu-west-1) → Tertiary (ap-south-1)
  • Weighted A/B Testing - 90% old version, 10% new version
  • Geolocation + Latency - EU users → GDPR-compliant DC, then lowest latency
  • Cost: $50/month per policy record + $0.50/million queries

4.2 DNSSEC (Domain Security)

Protect against DNS spoofing and cache poisoning attacks with cryptographic signatures.

Enable DNSSEC for anihpj.com
# 1. Enable DNSSEC signing
aws route53 enable-hosted-zone-dnssec \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC

# 2. Get DS records
aws route53 get-dnssec \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --query 'KeySigningKeys[0].DSRecord'

# Output (DS record):
# anihpj.com. 3600 IN DS 12345 13 2 ABCD1234...

# 3. Add DS record to your domain registrar
# (This creates chain of trust from root DNS to your domain)

# 4. Verify DNSSEC
dig anihpj.com +dnssec

# Cost: $0.50/month per hosted zone with DNSSEC
#       $0.50/million queries (same as standard)

4.3 Route 53 Resolver (Hybrid DNS)

Forward DNS queries between VPC and on-premises networks.

🔄 Resolver Endpoints Comparison
Endpoint Type Direction Use Case Cost
Inbound Endpoint On-prem → VPC Query AWS resources from on-premises $0.125/hour/IP
Outbound Endpoint VPC → On-prem Query on-premises DNS from AWS $0.125/hour/IP

Example: anihpj VPC needs to resolve corporate.anihpj.local (on-premises AD).
Solution: Outbound endpoint → Forward *.anihpj.local to 192.168.1.53 (on-prem DNS).

4.4 Alias Records (AWS-Specific)

Free alternative to CNAME records for AWS resources with automatic health checks.

💎 Alias vs CNAME
Feature Alias CNAME
Cost ✅ FREE (no query charges) ❌ $0.50/million queries
Zone Apex ✅ Can use at anihpj.com ❌ Only subdomains
Health Checks ✅ Automatic from target ❌ Manual configuration
TTL ✅ Managed by AWS ❌ Manual TTL setting
Targets ALB, NLB, CloudFront, S3, API Gateway Any domain name

🎯 Recommendation: Always use Alias records for AWS resources. For anihpj.com → ALB, use Alias to save $0.50/million queries and enable automatic health checks.

Alias Records for anihpj Resources
# boto3 script to create all anihpj alias records
import boto3

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
HOSTED_ZONE_ID = 'Z1234567890ABC'

# 1. Main domain → ALB
route53.change_resource_record_sets(
    HostedZoneId=HOSTED_ZONE_ID,
    ChangeBatch={
        'Changes': [{
            'Action': 'CREATE',
