            'ResourceRecordSet': {
                'Name': 'anihpj.com',
                'Type': 'A',
                'AliasTarget': {
                    'HostedZoneId': 'Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K',  # ALB zone
                    'DNSName': 'anihpj-alb-123.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
                    'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                }
            }
        }]
    }
)

# 2. Static assets → CloudFront
route53.change_resource_record_sets(
    HostedZoneId=HOSTED_ZONE_ID,
    ChangeBatch={
        'Changes': [{
            'Action': 'CREATE',
            'ResourceRecordSet': {
                'Name': 'cdn.anihpj.com',
                'Type': 'A',
                'AliasTarget': {
                    'HostedZoneId': 'Z2FDTNDATAQYW2',  # CloudFront zone
                    'DNSName': 'd1234abcd.cloudfront.net',
                    'EvaluateTargetHealth': False
                }
            }
        }]
    }
)

# 3. API Gateway
route53.change_resource_record_sets(
    HostedZoneId=HOSTED_ZONE_ID,
    ChangeBatch={
        'Changes': [{
            'Action': 'CREATE',
            'ResourceRecordSet': {
                'Name': 'api.anihpj.com',
                'Type': 'A',
                'AliasTarget': {
                    'HostedZoneId': 'Z1UJRXOUMOOFQ8',  # API Gateway zone
                    'DNSName': 'd-abc123.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com',
                    'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                }
            }
        }]
    }
)

print("✅ All alias records created successfully")

4.5 Query Logging

Log all DNS queries for security analysis, troubleshooting, and compliance.

Enable Query Logging
# 1. Create CloudWatch log group
aws logs create-log-group \
    --log-group-name /aws/route53/anihpj.com

# 2. Create query logging config
aws route53 create-query-logging-config \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --cloud-watch-logs-log-group-arn \
        arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789:log-group:/aws/route53/anihpj.com

# 3. Query the logs
aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name /aws/route53/anihpj.com \
    --filter-pattern "anihpj.com" \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s)000

# Sample log entry:
# {
#   "version": "1.0",
#   "account_id": "123456789",
#   "region": "us-east-1",
#   "vpc_id": "-",
#   "query_timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:30:45Z",
#   "query_name": "anihpj.com.",
#   "query_type": "A",
#   "query_class": "IN",
#   "rcode": "NOERROR",
#   "answers": [{"Rdata": "52.45.123.89", "Type": "A", "Class": "IN"}],
#   "srcaddr": "203.0.113.42",
#   "srcport": "54321",
#   "transport": "UDP",
#   "srcids": {"instance": "i-abc123"}
# }

# Cost: CloudWatch Logs pricing
#   - $0.50/GB ingested
#   - $0.03/GB storage
#   - Typical: $5-10/month for small site
🔐 Security Insight: Query logging helps detect DNS tunneling attacks, DDoS patterns, and unauthorized subdomain enumeration. For anihpj, monitor queries for unusual subdomains (admin.anihpj.com, test.anihpj.com) that could indicate reconnaissance activity.

💰 5. Pricing & Cost Optimization ▼

Transparent pricing model with no upfront costs or traffic minimums.

5.1 Pricing Model

💵 Route 53 Pricing Components
Component Cost Notes
Hosted Zone $0.50/month Per zone (first 25 free for 12 months)
Standard Queries $0.40/million First 1 billion/month
Alias Queries FREE To AWS resources (ALB, CloudFront, S3)
Latency-Based Routing $0.60/million +50% premium
Geo/Geoproximity Routing $0.70/million +75% premium
Health Checks $0.50/month Per endpoint (AWS or external)
Traffic Flow $50/month Per policy record
Domain Registration $12-13/year .com/.net/.org domains

5.2 Cost Examples

💵 Cost Scenarios Overview
Startup: $4-5/month (100K visits, single region)
Growth: $15-20/month (500K visits, multi-region)
Enterprise: $200-250/month (5M+ visits, advanced routing)
Alias Savings: Use FREE alias records to ALB/CloudFront
Health Checks: $0.50 each (AWS), $1.00 (non-AWS)
Traffic Flow: $50/month for visual policy editor
📊 anihpj Cost Scenarios
SCENARIO 1: Small Job Portal (100K visits/month)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Component                    │ Qty      │ Cost         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Hosted zone (anihpj.com)     │ 1        │ $0.50        │
│ DNS queries                   │ 5M       │ $2.00        │
│   - Users: 100K * 50 queries  │          │              │
│ Health checks (ALB)           │ 1        │ $0.50        │
│ Domain registration           │ 1/year   │ $1.08/mo     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ MONTHLY TOTAL                 │          │ $4.08        │
│ ANNUAL TOTAL                  │          │ $48.96       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

SCENARIO 2: Medium Job Portal (500K visits/month, Multi-Region)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Component                    │ Qty      │ Cost         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Hosted zone                   │ 1        │ $0.50        │
│ Latency-based routing queries │ 25M      │ $15.00       │
│   - Users: 500K * 50 queries  │          │              │
│ Health checks (2 regions)     │ 2        │ $1.00        │
│ Alias queries (to CloudFront) │ FREE     │ $0.00        │
│ Domain registration           │ 1/year   │ $1.08/mo     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ MONTHLY TOTAL                 │          │ $17.58       │
│ ANNUAL TOTAL                  │          │ $210.96      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

SCENARIO 3: Enterprise Job Portal (5M visits/month, Advanced Routing)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Component                    │ Qty      │ Cost         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Hosted zones                  │ 3        │ $1.50        │
│   - anihpj.com, api.anihpj    │          │              │
│   - internal.anihpj.local     │          │              │
│ Geolocation routing queries   │ 250M     │ $175.00      │
│   - Users: 5M * 50 queries    │          │              │
│ Health checks (5 endpoints)   │ 5        │ $2.50        │
│ Traffic Flow policy           │ 1        │ $50.00       │
│ Query logging (10GB/month)    │ 10GB     │ $5.00        │
│ Domain registration           │ 1/year   │ $1.08/mo     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ MONTHLY TOTAL                 │          │ $235.08      │
│ ANNUAL TOTAL                  │          │ $2,820.96    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

5.3 Cost Optimization Strategies

💡 10 Ways to Reduce Route 53 Costs
  1. Use Alias Records → Save $0.40/million queries vs CNAME
  2. Consolidate Hosted Zones → Use subdomains instead of separate zones
  3. Increase TTL → Higher TTL = fewer queries (300s → 3600s)
  4. Use CloudFront → Alias queries to CloudFront are FREE
  5. Limit Health Checks → Only monitor critical endpoints
  6. Avoid Traffic Flow → Use standard routing policies when possible
  7. Optimize Query Logging → Sample logs instead of 100% capture
  8. Delete Unused Zones → $0.50/month adds up for unused domains
  9. Use Weighted Routing → Cheaper than geo-routing for A/B tests
  10. Monitor with Cost Explorer → Track Route 53 spend by service
Cost Optimization for anihpj
# Calculate potential savings with alias records
import boto3

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')

# Get query count for last month
response = cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
    Namespace='AWS/Route53',
    MetricName='QueryCount',
    Dimensions=[{'Name': 'HostedZoneId', 'Value': 'Z1234567890ABC'}],
    StartTime='2025-01-01',
    EndTime='2025-02-01',
    Period=2592000,  # 30 days
    Statistics=['Sum']
)

total_queries = response['Datapoints'][0]['Sum']
print(f"Total queries last month: {total_queries:,.0f}")

# Calculate costs
standard_cost = (total_queries / 1_000_000) * 0.40
alias_cost = 0  # FREE for AWS resources

savings = standard_cost - alias_cost
print(f"\n💰 Cost Comparison:")
print(f"Standard CNAME: ${standard_cost:.2f}")
print(f"Alias Record:   ${alias_cost:.2f}")
print(f"Monthly Savings: ${savings:.2f}")
print(f"Annual Savings:  ${savings * 12:.2f}")

# Output:
# Total queries last month: 5,000,000
#
# 💰 Cost Comparison:
# Standard CNAME: $2.00
# Alias Record:   $0.00
# Monthly Savings: $2.00
# Annual Savings:  $24.00

5.4 Free Tier

🎁 AWS Free Tier for Route 53
  • 25 Hosted Zones - FREE for first 12 months (then $0.50/month each)
  • 1 Million Standard Queries - FREE per month forever
  • Alias Queries - Always FREE (no time limit)
  • 1 Million Queries to Alias Records - FREE per month forever

For anihpj: With careful planning, you can run a small job portal for $0/month within free tier limits (assuming < 1M queries and using hosted zone during first 12 months).

📊 Cost Tracking Tip: Set up AWS Budgets alert for Route 53 spending. Create budget for $10/month and receive email when 80% threshold reached. This prevents unexpected bills from query spikes or misconfigured records.

📈 6. Scalability & Performance ▼

Route 53 automatically scales to handle any query volume with consistent low latency.

6.1 Performance Metrics

⚡ Route 53 Performance SLA
Metric Performance Details
Availability 100% SLA Credit if < 100%
Query Latency < 100ms (global avg) Measured from edge locations
Query Capacity Unlimited Auto-scales to any volume
Propagation Time 60 seconds (typically) Record changes go live fast
Anycast Network 200+ edge locations Same as CloudFront

6.2 Global Infrastructure

🌍 Route 53 Anycast Network Architecture
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     ROUTE 53 GLOBAL DNS NETWORK                               │
│                                                                               │
│  User Request: dig anihpj.com                                                 │
│        │                                                                       │
│        ├──→ Routed to nearest Route 53 edge location (200+ worldwide)        │
│        │                                                                       │
│  ┌─────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐           │
│  │                    EDGE LOCATIONS                             │           │
│  │                                                                │           │
│  │  North America:  50+  │  Query latency: 20-30ms              │           │
│  │  Europe:         40+  │  Query latency: 25-35ms              │           │
│  │  Asia Pacific:   60+  │  Query latency: 30-50ms              │           │
│  │  South America:  15+  │  Query latency: 40-60ms              │           │
│  │  Middle East:    20+  │  Query latency: 35-55ms              │           │
│  │  Africa:         10+  │  Query latency: 50-80ms              │           │
│  └────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘           │
│                           │                                                   │
│                           ├──→ Authoritative Name Servers (4 per zone)       │
│                           │    - ns-123.awsdns-45.com                         │
│                           │    - ns-678.awsdns-90.net                         │
│                           │    - ns-1234.awsdns-56.org                        │
│                           │    - ns-5678.awsdns-12.co.uk                      │
│                           │                                                   │
│                           └──→ Hosted Zone (anihpj.com)                       │
│                                ├─ A record: anihpj.com → ALB                  │
│                                ├─ CNAME: www → anihpj.com                     │
│                                ├─ MX: mail.anihpj.com                         │
│                                └─ TXT: SPF/DMARC records                      │
│                                                                               │
│  🎯 Key Benefits:                                                             │
│  ✓ Anycast routing → User hits closest edge (lowest latency)                 │
│  ✓ DDoS protection → Distributed network absorbs attacks                     │
│  ✓ Automatic failover → If one edge fails, traffic reroutes                  │
│  ✓ No single point of failure → Redundancy at every layer                    │
│                                                                               │
│  For anihpj users:                                                            │
│  - US job seeker (NYC)      → Route 53 edge in Ashburn, VA  (15ms)          │
│  - EU job seeker (London)   → Route 53 edge in London       (8ms)           │
│  - APAC job seeker (Mumbai) → Route 53 edge in Mumbai       (12ms)          │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

6.3 Traffic Management at Scale

📊 Handling Traffic Spikes for anihpj
Scenario Traffic Pattern Route 53 Response Cost Impact
Normal Day 5M queries/day Standard performance $2/month
Job Fair Event 50M queries/day (10x spike) Auto-scales, no config needed $20/month (pay per use)
DDoS Attack 500M queries/day (100x spike) Absorbs attack, stays available $200/month (+ AWS Shield)
Global Expansion 100M queries/day (steady) Latency-based routing to regions $60/month

6.4 Caching & TTL Strategy

Optimal TTL Settings for anihpj
# TTL (Time To Live) determines how long DNS resolvers cache your records

# STRATEGY 1: High availability (recommended for anihpj)
# Short TTL = faster failover, more queries, higher cost

# A record for main domain (short TTL for quick failover)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "UPSERT",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "TTL": 60,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "52.45.123.89"}]
    }
  }]
}'
# TTL: 60 seconds
# Failover time: 1 minute
# Cost: Higher query volume

# STRATEGY 2: Cost optimization (for static resources)
# Long TTL = fewer queries, lower cost, slower changes

# CNAME for static CDN subdomain
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "UPSERT",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "cdn.anihpj.com",
      "Type": "CNAME",
      "TTL": 86400,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "d1234.cloudfront.net"}]
    }
  }]
}'
# TTL: 86400 seconds (24 hours)
# Failover time: 24 hours
# Cost: Minimal query volume

# RECOMMENDED TTL VALUES FOR ANIHPJ:
# - Critical records (A, Alias): 60-300 seconds
# - Subdomains (www, api):       300 seconds
# - Mail records (MX):            3600 seconds (1 hour)
# - Static CDN (CNAME):           86400 seconds (24 hours)
# - TXT records (SPF/DMARC):      3600 seconds

6.5 Monitoring & Performance Tracking

CloudWatch Metrics for Route 53
# Monitor Route 53 query performance
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')

# Get query count (last 7 days)
response = cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
    Namespace='AWS/Route53',
    MetricName='QueryCount',
    Dimensions=[
        {'Name': 'HostedZoneId', 'Value': 'Z1234567890ABC'}
    ],
    StartTime=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=7),
    EndTime=datetime.now(),
    Period=86400,  # Daily
    Statistics=['Sum']
)

print("📊 Route 53 Query Volume (Last 7 Days):")
for point in sorted(response['Datapoints'], key=lambda x: x['Timestamp']):
    date = point['Timestamp'].strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
    count = point['Sum']
    print(f"  {date}: {count:,.0f} queries")

# Get health check status
route53 = boto3.client('route53')
health_checks = route53.list_health_checks()

print("\n💚 Health Check Status:")
for hc in health_checks['HealthChecks']:
    hc_id = hc['Id']
    status = route53.get_health_check_status(HealthCheckId=hc_id)
    
    healthy_count = sum(1 for s in status['HealthCheckObservations'] 
                        if s['StatusReport']['Status'] == 'Success')
    total_count = len(status['HealthCheckObservations'])
    
    print(f"  {hc['HealthCheckConfig']['FullyQualifiedDomainName']}: "
          f"{healthy_count}/{total_count} regions healthy")

# Output:
# 📊 Route 53 Query Volume (Last 7 Days):
#   2025-01-08: 4,523,891 queries
#   2025-01-09: 5,234,112 queries
#   2025-01-10: 4,891,445 queries
#   2025-01-11: 3,992,334 queries  (weekend dip)
#   2025-01-12: 3,881,223 queries
#   2025-01-13: 5,445,667 queries
#   2025-01-14: 5,789,012 queries
#
# 💚 Health Check Status:
#   anihpj.com: 15/15 regions healthy
#   api.anihpj.com: 14/15 regions healthy (1 degraded)
🚀 Performance Tip: Route 53 propagates record changes in ~60 seconds globally. For planned maintenance, reduce TTL to 60 seconds 24 hours before changes. This ensures quick DNS updates when you switch traffic to new infrastructure.

🎓 7. Advanced Topics ▼

Enterprise-grade DNS features for complex routing scenarios.

7.1 Geolocation Routing

Route traffic based on user's geographic location (continent, country, or US state).

Regional Content for anihpj Job Portal
# Scenario: Route EU users to GDPR-compliant servers
# US users to us-east-1, EU users to eu-west-1, Rest to ap-south-1

# 1. Create record for EU users
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "SetIdentifier": "EU-Users",
      "GeoLocation": {
        "ContinentCode": "EU"
      },
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z32O12XQLNTSW2",
        "DNSName": "anihpj-eu-alb.eu-west-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# 2. Create record for US users
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "SetIdentifier": "US-Users",
      "GeoLocation": {
        "CountryCode": "US"
      },
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K",
        "DNSName": "anihpj-us-alb.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# 3. Create default record (for rest of world)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "SetIdentifier": "Default",
      "GeoLocation": {
        "ContinentCode": "*"
      },
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z1LMS91P8CMLE5",
        "DNSName": "anihpj-apac-alb.ap-south-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# Cost: $0.70/million queries (geo routing premium)

7.2 Geoproximity Routing (Traffic Flow)

Route traffic based on geographic location with bias to shift traffic between regions.

🌍 Geoproximity with Bias
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│            GEOPROXIMITY ROUTING WITH BIAS                       │
│                                                                 │
│  Resources:                                                     │
│  - us-east-1 (Virginia)   : Coordinates: 38°N, 77°W           │
│  - eu-west-1 (Ireland)    : Coordinates: 53°N, 6°W            │
│                                                                 │
│  Scenario 1: No Bias (Default)                                 │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐                                      │
│  │ User in NYC (40°N,74°W)                                     │
│  │ → Distance to us-east-1: ~350 km                            │
│  │ → Distance to eu-west-1: ~5000 km                           │
│  │ → Routed to: us-east-1 ✅                                   │
│  └──────────────────────┘                                      │
│                                                                 │
│  Scenario 2: Positive Bias (+50 on eu-west-1)                  │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐                                      │
│  │ User in London (51°N,0°W)                                   │
│  │ → Normally routes to eu-west-1                              │
│  │ → With bias, expand eu-west-1 coverage area 50%             │
│  │ → More users from East Coast US now route to eu-west-1     │
│  └──────────────────────┘                                      │
│                                                                 │
│  Scenario 3: Negative Bias (-30 on us-east-1)                  │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐                                      │
│  │ Reduce us-east-1 load by 30%                                │
│  │ → Push California users to ap-south-1 instead              │
│  │ → Cost: $0.70/million queries + $50/month policy           │
│  └──────────────────────┘                                      │
│                                                                 │
│  Use Case for anihpj:                                           │
│  - eu-west-1 has excess capacity → Set bias +40                │
│  - Shift some UK/European traffic there to balance load        │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

7.3 Failover Routing (Disaster Recovery)

Active-Passive Failover for anihpj
# Create health check for primary region
PRIMARY_HC=$(aws route53 create-health-check \
    --health-check-config \
        Protocol=HTTPS,\
        ResourcePath=/health/,\
        FullyQualifiedDomainName=anihpj-us-alb.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com,\
        Port=443,\
        Type=HTTPS,\
        RequestInterval=30,\
        FailureThreshold=3 \
    --query 'HealthCheck.Id' \
    --output text)

# Create PRIMARY record (us-east-1)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "SetIdentifier": "Primary",
      "Failover": "PRIMARY",
      "HealthCheckId": "'$PRIMARY_HC'",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K",
        "DNSName": "anihpj-us-alb.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": false
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# Create SECONDARY record (eu-west-1) - no health check needed
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "SetIdentifier": "Secondary",
      "Failover": "SECONDARY",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z32O12XQLNTSW2",
        "DNSName": "anihpj-eu-alb.eu-west-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# How it works:
# 1. Traffic goes to PRIMARY (us-east-1) by default
# 2. Health check monitors PRIMARY every 30 seconds
# 3. If PRIMARY fails 3 consecutive checks (90 seconds):
#    - Route 53 marks PRIMARY as unhealthy
#    - All traffic automatically routes to SECONDARY (eu-west-1)
#    - Failover time: ~2 minutes (90s health check + 60s TTL)
# 4. When PRIMARY recovers, traffic switches back

# Cost: $0.50/month per health check

7.4 Weighted Routing (A/B Testing)

Gradual Django Version Rollout
# anihpj A/B test: Roll out new Django 5.0 version gradually
# Start with 10% traffic to new version, increase if stable

import boto3

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
HOSTED_ZONE_ID = 'Z1234567890ABC'

# OLD VERSION (Django 4.2) - 90% traffic
route53.change_resource_record_sets(
    HostedZoneId=HOSTED_ZONE_ID,
    ChangeBatch={
        'Changes': [{
            'Action': 'UPSERT',
            'ResourceRecordSet': {
                'Name': 'anihpj.com',
                'Type': 'A',
                'SetIdentifier': 'Old-Version-Django42',
                'Weight': 90,  # 90% of traffic
                'AliasTarget': {
                    'HostedZoneId': 'Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K',
                    'DNSName': 'anihpj-alb-old.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
                    'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                }
            }
        }]
    }
)

# NEW VERSION (Django 5.0) - 10% traffic
route53.change_resource_record_sets(
    HostedZoneId=HOSTED_ZONE_ID,
    ChangeBatch={
        'Changes': [{
            'Action': 'UPSERT',
            'ResourceRecordSet': {
                'Name': 'anihpj.com',
                'Type': 'A',
                'SetIdentifier': 'New-Version-Django50',
                'Weight': 10,  # 10% of traffic
                'AliasTarget': {
                    'HostedZoneId': 'Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K',
                    'DNSName': 'anihpj-alb-new.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
                    'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                }
            }
        }]
    }
)

print("✅ A/B test configured: 90% old, 10% new")
print("Monitor metrics for 24 hours, then increase to 50/50")

# Rollout schedule:
# Day 1: 90/10 (old/new)
# Day 3: 70/30 (if no issues)
# Day 5: 50/50 (if metrics stable)
# Day 7: 30/70 (prepare full switch)
# Day 10: 0/100 (full migration)

# Cost: $0.40/million queries (standard routing)

7.5 Multivalue Answer Routing

Return up to 8 healthy IP addresses for simple load distribution.

🔄 Multivalue vs Application Load Balancer
Feature Multivalue Routing ALB
Load Distribution DNS-based (client chooses) Layer 7 (ALB decides)
Health Checks Route 53 checks ALB target health
Max Targets 8 IPs returned Unlimited
Cost $0.40/M queries + $4/month HC $16.20/month + LCU
Best For Simple setups, cost-sensitive Enterprise apps, advanced routing
Multivalue Routing for anihpj
# Create 4 EC2 instances with health checks
for i in 1 2 3 4; do
    # Create health check for each instance
    HC_ID=$(aws route53 create-health-check \
        --health-check-config \
            Protocol=HTTP,\
            ResourcePath=/health/,\
            IPAddress=52.45.123.$((80 + i)),\
            Port=80,\
            Type=HTTP,\
            RequestInterval=30,\
            FailureThreshold=3 \
        --query 'HealthCheck.Id' \
        --output text)
    
    # Create multivalue record
    aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
        --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
        --change-batch '{
      "Changes": [{
        "Action": "CREATE",
        "ResourceRecordSet": {
          "Name": "anihpj.com",
          "Type": "A",
          "SetIdentifier": "Server-'$i'",
          "MultiValueAnswer": true,
          "TTL": 60,
          "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "52.45.123.'$((80 + i))'"}],
          "HealthCheckId": "'$HC_ID'"
        }
      }]
    }'
done

# Result: Route 53 returns up to 8 healthy IPs
# Client randomly picks one from the list
# If server fails health check, it's removed from responses
💡 When to Use Each Routing Policy:
  • Simple - Single resource (staging.anihpj.com)
  • Weighted - A/B testing new features (90/10 split)
  • Latency - Global users (nearest region)
  • Failover - Disaster recovery (primary → backup)
  • Geolocation - Compliance (GDPR, data residency)
  • Geoproximity - Fine-tune traffic distribution
  • Multivalue - Simple load balancing without ALB

7.6 Latency-Based Routing

Route users to the AWS region with lowest network latency for optimal performance.

🌍 How Latency Routing Works

Route 53 measures latency from 50+ global locations to your AWS regions and automatically routes users to the region providing the best performance. Perfect for global applications like anihpj with users worldwide.

User Location Fastest Region Avg Latency Routed To
🇺🇸 New York us-east-1 (Virginia) 15ms 54.123.45.67
🇬🇧 London eu-west-1 (Ireland) 12ms 52.210.34.89
🇯🇵 Tokyo ap-northeast-1 (Tokyo) 8ms 54.238.12.45
🇧🇷 São Paulo sa-east-1 (São Paulo) 18ms 54.207.89.23
Configure Latency Routing for Global anihpj
# Deploy anihpj to 3 regions and configure latency routing
import boto3

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
ZONE_ID = 'Z1234567890ABC'

# Regions and their ALB endpoints
regions = {
    'us-east-1': 'anihpj-us-1234567890.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
    'eu-west-1': 'anihpj-eu-1234567890.eu-west-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
    'ap-southeast-1': 'anihpj-ap-1234567890.ap-southeast-1.elb.amazonaws.com'
}

for region, alb_dns in regions.items():
    # Create latency-based record for each region
    response = route53.change_resource_record_sets(
        HostedZoneId=ZONE_ID,
        ChangeBatch={
            'Changes': [{
                'Action': 'CREATE',
                'ResourceRecordSet': {
                    'Name': 'anihpj.com',
                    'Type': 'A',
                    'SetIdentifier': f'anihpj-{region}',
                    'Region': region,  # Enable latency-based routing
                    'AliasTarget': {
                        'HostedZoneId': 'Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K',  # ALB hosted zone
                        'DNSName': alb_dns,
                        'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                    }
                }
            }]
        }
    )
    print(f"✅ Created latency record for {region}: {alb_dns}")

# Result: Users automatically routed to nearest region
# - New York users → us-east-1 (15ms latency)
# - London users → eu-west-1 (12ms latency)
# - Singapore users → ap-southeast-1 (10ms latency)

print("\n🌍 Global Latency Routing Configured!")
print("Test from different locations:")
print("  curl -w '@curl-format.txt' https://anihpj.com")
AWS CLI Configuration
# Create latency record for US region
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "SetIdentifier": "US-East",
      "Region": "us-east-1",
      "TTL": 60,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "54.123.45.67"}]
    }
  }]
}'

# Create latency record for EU region
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "SetIdentifier": "EU-West",
      "Region": "eu-west-1",
      "TTL": 60,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "52.210.34.89"}]
    }
  }]
}'

# Test from different locations
# From US: dig anihpj.com → returns 54.123.45.67
# From EU: dig anihpj.com → returns 52.210.34.89
⚡ Latency vs Geolocation
Latency Routing Optimizes for performance - Routes based on actual network latency measurements. Best for speed.
Geolocation Routing Optimizes for compliance - Routes based on user's geographic location. Best for GDPR, data residency.

Recommendation for anihpj: Use Latency Routing for best user experience, unless you have compliance requirements forcing geolocation routing.

💡 Real-World Performance Gains:
  • Page Load Time - Reduced by 40-60% for international users
  • API Response Time - Decreased from 500ms to 80ms average
  • Bounce Rate - Improved by 25% with faster regional routing
  • SEO Ranking - Google favors faster loading times

✨ 8. Best Practices ▼

Production-ready DNS configuration strategies for anihpj job portal.

8.1 Security Checklist

🔒 Route 53 Security Checklist for anihpj
Security Control Priority Status
Enable DNSSEC for domain integrity CRITICAL ☐
Implement least-privilege IAM policies CRITICAL ☐
Enable MFA for users managing DNS HIGH ☐
Enable query logging to CloudWatch HIGH ☐
Configure CloudWatch alarms for anomalies HIGH ☐
Enable AWS CloudTrail for audit logging MEDIUM ☐
Regular audits of unused DNS records MEDIUM ☐
Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform/CFN) LOW ☐
Least-Privilege IAM Policy for anihpj DNS Management
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "ReadOnlyAccess",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "route53:GetHostedZone",
        "route53:ListHostedZones",
        "route53:ListResourceRecordSets",
        "route53:GetChange",
        "route53:GetHealthCheck",
        "route53:ListHealthChecks"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    },
    {
      "Sid": "ManageAnihpjRecordsOnly",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "route53:ChangeResourceRecordSets"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:route53:::hostedzone/Z1234567890ABC",
      "Condition": {
        "StringEquals": {
          "route53:ChangeResourceRecordSetsNormalizedRecordNames": [
            "anihpj.com",
            "*.anihpj.com"
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "Sid": "DenyDeleteHostedZone",
      "Effect": "Deny",
      "Action": [
        "route53:DeleteHostedZone"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}

8.2 Performance Optimization

⚡ DNS Performance Tuning for anihpj
Strategy Implementation Impact Priority
Use Alias Records Point to ALB, CloudFront, S3 FREE queries, instant updates 🔴 HIGH
Optimize TTL 60s for dynamic, 300s+ for static Reduce query volume 80% 🔴 HIGH
Enable Latency Routing Multi-region deployment 40-60% faster page loads 🟡 MEDIUM
Minimize Record Count Consolidate subdomains Faster DNS responses 🟢 LOW
TTL Strategy for Different Record Types
# Critical production records (fast failover needed)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets --hosted-zone-id Z123 \
--change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "UPSERT",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "TTL": 60,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "54.123.45.67"}]
    }
  }]
}'

# Static content CDN (rarely changes)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets --hosted-zone-id Z123 \
--change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "UPSERT",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "cdn.anihpj.com",
      "Type": "CNAME",
      "TTL": 86400,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "d123abc.cloudfront.net"}]
    }
  }]
}'

# Email records (MX/TXT - very stable)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets --hosted-zone-id Z123 \
--change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "UPSERT",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "MX",
      "TTL": 3600,
      "ResourceRecords": [
        {"Value": "10 mail.anihpj.com"}
      ]
    }
  }]
}'
💡 Performance Benchmarks:
  • DNS Query Time: < 10ms globally with Route 53 anycast
  • Failover Time: 60s (with TTL=60) to detect and switch
  • Propagation: Near-instant globally vs 24-48hrs traditional DNS
  • Throughput: Unlimited queries per second per hosted zone

8.3 Backup & DR Strategy

Disaster Recovery Configuration for anihpj
# Multi-region failover for anihpj job portal
import boto3

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
ZONE_ID = 'Z1234567890ABC'

# Primary Region: us-east-1 (N. Virginia)
# Secondary Region: eu-west-1 (Ireland)

# 1. Create health check for primary region
primary_hc = route53.create_health_check(
    HealthCheckConfig={
        'Type': 'HTTPS',
        'ResourcePath': '/health/',
        'FullyQualifiedDomainName': 'anihpj-primary.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
        'Port': 443,
        'RequestInterval': 30,
        'FailureThreshold': 2,  # Fail after 2 consecutive failures (60s)
        'MeasureLatency': True
    }
)
primary_hc_id = primary_hc['HealthCheck']['Id']
print(f"✅ Primary health check created: {primary_hc_id}")

# 2. Create PRIMARY record with failover
route53.change_resource_record_sets(
    HostedZoneId=ZONE_ID,
    ChangeBatch={
        'Changes': [{
            'Action': 'UPSERT',
            'ResourceRecordSet': {
                'Name': 'anihpj.com',
                'Type': 'A',
                'SetIdentifier': 'Primary-US-East',
                'Failover': 'PRIMARY',
                'HealthCheckId': primary_hc_id,
                'AliasTarget': {
                    'HostedZoneId': 'Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K',
                    'DNSName': 'anihpj-primary.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
                    'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                }
            }
        }]
    }
)
print("✅ Primary failover record created (us-east-1)")

# 3. Create SECONDARY record (no health check needed)
route53.change_resource_record_sets(
    HostedZoneId=ZONE_ID,
    ChangeBatch={
        'Changes': [{
            'Action': 'UPSERT',
            'ResourceRecordSet': {
                'Name': 'anihpj.com',
                'Type': 'A',
                'SetIdentifier': 'Secondary-EU-West',
                'Failover': 'SECONDARY',
                'AliasTarget': {
                    'HostedZoneId': 'Z32O12XQLNTSW2',
                    'DNSName': 'anihpj-backup.eu-west-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
                    'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                }
            }
        }]
    }
)
print("✅ Secondary failover record created (eu-west-1)")

print("\n🆘 Disaster Recovery Configuration Complete!")
print("RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 2 minutes")
print("RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 0 (active-active replication)")
print("\n📊 Failover Logic:")
print("  1. Primary us-east-1 health check runs every 30s")
print("  2. If 2 consecutive failures (60s), route to eu-west-1")
print("  3. When primary recovers, traffic returns automatically")
🆘 DR Testing Checklist
  1. Monthly Failover Tests - Simulate primary region failure
  2. Measure RTO - Time from failure to full recovery
  3. Verify Data Sync - Ensure RDS read replica is current
  4. Test DNS Propagation - Confirm users route to backup
  5. Document Runbook - Step-by-step failover procedures
  6. Alert Validation - Ensure CloudWatch alarms trigger

8.4 Tagging Strategy

Implement Resource Tags for anihpj DNS
# Tag hosted zone for cost allocation and management
aws route53 change-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-type hostedzone \
    --resource-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --add-tags \
        Key=Project,Value=anihpj \
        Key=Environment,Value=production \
        Key=CostCenter,Value=engineering \
        Key=ManagedBy,Value=terraform \
        Key=Owner,Value=devops@anihpj.com

# Tag health checks
aws route53 change-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-type healthcheck \
    --resource-id abc123-health-check-id \
    --add-tags \
        Key=Application,Value=anihpj-jobportal \
        Key=Region,Value=us-east-1 \
        Key=Endpoint,Value=primary-alb

# Query resources by tag
aws route53 list-tags-for-resources \
    --resource-type hostedzone \
    --resource-ids Z1234567890ABC

# Output:
# {
#   "ResourceTagSets": [{
#     "ResourceType": "hostedzone",
#     "ResourceId": "Z1234567890ABC",
#     "Tags": [
#       {"Key": "Project", "Value": "anihpj"},
#       {"Key": "Environment", "Value": "production"},
#       {"Key": "CostCenter", "Value": "engineering"}
#     ]
#   }]
# }
🏷️ Recommended Tag Schema
Tag Key Example Value Purpose
Project anihpj Identify project ownership
Environment production, staging, dev Separate environments
CostCenter engineering, marketing Cost allocation reports
Owner devops@anihpj.com Contact for issues
ManagedBy terraform, manual Infrastructure management
💡 Tagging Benefits:
  • Cost Attribution - Track DNS costs per project/team
  • Resource Discovery - Find all anihpj-related resources
  • Automation - Trigger actions based on tags (e.g., cleanup)
  • Access Control - IAM policies based on resource tags
  • Compliance - Identify resources requiring specific certifications

💼 9. Use Cases ▼

Real-world DNS scenarios for job portal applications.

9.1 Web Application Hosting

Complete anihpj DNS Setup
# Full production DNS configuration for job portal

# 1. Main domain → ALB (alias record - FREE)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K",
        "DNSName": "anihpj-alb.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# 2. WWW redirect
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "www.anihpj.com",
      "Type": "CNAME",
      "TTL": 300,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "anihpj.com"}]
    }
  }]
}'

# 3. Static assets → CloudFront
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "cdn.anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z2FDTNDATAQYW2",
        "DNSName": "d1234abcd.cloudfront.net",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": false
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# 4. Email (SES)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [
    {
      "Action": "CREATE",
      "ResourceRecordSet": {
        "Name": "anihpj.com",
        "Type": "MX",
        "TTL": 3600,
        "ResourceRecords": [
          {"Value": "10 inbound-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com"}
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "Action": "CREATE",
      "ResourceRecordSet": {
        "Name": "anihpj.com",
        "Type": "TXT",
        "TTL": 300,
        "ResourceRecords": [
          {"Value": "\\"v=spf1 include:amazonses.com ~all\\""}
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}'

9.2 Disaster Recovery

Multi-Region DR Architecture
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              DISASTER RECOVERY WITH ROUTE 53                       │
│                                                                    │
│  Normal Operation:                                                 │
│  User → Route 53 → PRIMARY (us-east-1) → anihpj Django app       │
│                                                                    │
│  Outage Detection:                                                 │
│  Route 53 Health Check → PRIMARY fails 3 times (90 seconds)      │
│  └─→ Automatic failover initiated                                 │
│                                                                    │
│  Failover Mode:                                                    │
│  User → Route 53 → SECONDARY (eu-west-1) → anihpj Django app     │
│                                                                    │
│  Recovery:                                                         │
│  PRIMARY comes back online → Health check passes                  │
│  └─→ Traffic automatically routes back to PRIMARY                 │
│                                                                    │
│  RTO (Recovery Time Objective): ~2 minutes                        │
│  RPO (Recovery Point Objective): ~0 (active-active replication)   │
│                                                                    │
│  Cost for anihpj:                                                  │
│  - Hosted zone: $0.50/month                                       │
│  - Health checks (2): $1.00/month                                 │
│  - Queries: $2-5/month                                            │
│  - Total: ~$3.50-6.50/month for automatic DR                      │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

9.3 Global Job Portal

🌎 Regional Routing Strategy
Region Target Routing Policy Use Case
North America us-east-1 ALB Latency-based US/Canada job seekers
Europe eu-west-1 ALB Geolocation GDPR compliance
Asia Pacific ap-south-1 ALB Latency-based India/APAC users
Default us-east-1 ALB Default Fallback for other regions

9.4 Blue-Green Deployments

Zero-Downtime Django Deployment
# Weighted routing for gradual traffic shift
import boto3
import time

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
HOSTED_ZONE_ID = 'Z1234567890ABC'

def shift_traffic(blue_weight, green_weight):
    """Shift traffic between blue (old) and green (new) environments"""
    
    # Update blue environment weight
    route53.change_resource_record_sets(
        HostedZoneId=HOSTED_ZONE_ID,
        ChangeBatch={
            'Changes': [{
                'Action': 'UPSERT',
                'ResourceRecordSet': {
                    'Name': 'anihpj.com',
                    'Type': 'A',
                    'SetIdentifier': 'Blue-Environment',
                    'Weight': blue_weight,
                    'AliasTarget': {
                        'HostedZoneId': 'Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K',
                        'DNSName': 'anihpj-blue-alb.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
                        'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                    }
                }
            }]
        }
    )
    
    # Update green environment weight
    route53.change_resource_record_sets(
        HostedZoneId=HOSTED_ZONE_ID,
        ChangeBatch={
            'Changes': [{
                'Action': 'UPSERT',
                'ResourceRecordSet': {
                    'Name': 'anihpj.com',
                    'Type': 'A',
                    'SetIdentifier': 'Green-Environment',
                    'Weight': green_weight,
                    'AliasTarget': {
                        'HostedZoneId': 'Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K',
                        'DNSName': 'anihpj-green-alb.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
                        'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                    }
                }
            }]
        }
    )
    
    print(f"✅ Traffic shift: Blue={blue_weight}%, Green={green_weight}%")

# Deployment process
print("🚀 Starting blue-green deployment...")
shift_traffic(100, 0)   # Start: 100% on blue
time.sleep(300)          # Wait 5 minutes

shift_traffic(90, 10)    # Shift 10% to green
time.sleep(600)          # Monitor for 10 minutes

shift_traffic(50, 50)    # Shift 50% to green
time.sleep(600)          # Monitor for 10 minutes

shift_traffic(10, 90)    # Shift 90% to green
time.sleep(600)          # Monitor for 10 minutes

shift_traffic(0, 100)    # Full cutover to green
print("✅ Deployment complete! 100% traffic on green environment")
🎯 anihpj Use Case Summary: Route 53 enables global job portal with 99.99% uptime, automatic failover to secondary region, and latency-based routing for international job seekers. Total DNS cost: $5-10/month for enterprise-grade reliability.

9.4 Microservices Architecture

DNS routing strategies for microservices-based job portal applications.

🏗️ Microservices DNS Architecture for anihpj

Route 53 enables service discovery and traffic management across multiple microservices. Each microservice gets its own subdomain for independent scaling and deployment.

anihpj.com Microservices Architecture:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                   │
│  anihpj.com (Main Entry Point - CloudFront)                     │
│  │                                                                │
│  ├─► api.anihpj.com (API Gateway)                               │
│  │   ├─► auth.api.anihpj.com    → Authentication Service        │
│  │   ├─► jobs.api.anihpj.com    → Job Listings Service          │
│  │   ├─► users.api.anihpj.com   → User Profile Service          │
│  │   ├─► search.api.anihpj.com  → Search & Filter Service       │
│  │   └─► apply.api.anihpj.com   → Application Processing        │
│  │                                                                │
│  ├─► cdn.anihpj.com (CloudFront Distribution)                   │
│  │   ├─► static.anihpj.com  → CSS, JS, Images                   │
│  │   └─► media.anihpj.com   → User Uploads                      │
│  │                                                                │
│  ├─► admin.anihpj.com (Django Admin Panel)                      │
│  ├─► analytics.anihpj.com (Analytics Dashboard)                 │
│  └─► notifications.anihpj.com (Notification Service)            │
│                                                                   │
│  Internal Services (Private Hosted Zone):                        │
│  └─► internal.anihpj.com                                         │
│      ├─► db.internal.anihpj.com        → RDS PostgreSQL          │
│      ├─► cache.internal.anihpj.com     → ElastiCache Redis       │
│      ├─► queue.internal.anihpj.com     → SQS Queue Endpoint      │
│      └─► events.internal.anihpj.com    → Event Bus               │
│                                                                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Configure Microservices DNS with Service Discovery
# Setup Route 53 for anihpj microservices architecture
import boto3
import json

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
servicediscovery = boto3.client('servicediscovery')

ZONE_ID = 'Z1234567890ABC'

# Microservices configuration
services = {
    'auth.api.anihpj.com': {
        'alb': 'anihpj-auth-alb-123456.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
        'weight': 100,
        'health_check': '/health/',
        'description': 'User authentication & authorization'
    },
    'jobs.api.anihpj.com': {
        'alb': 'anihpj-jobs-alb-789012.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
        'weight': 100,
        'health_check': '/health/',
        'description': 'Job listings CRUD operations'
    },
    'users.api.anihpj.com': {
        'alb': 'anihpj-users-alb-345678.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
        'weight': 100,
        'health_check': '/health/',
        'description': 'User profile management'
    },
    'search.api.anihpj.com': {
        'alb': 'anihpj-search-alb-901234.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
        'weight': 100,
        'health_check': '/search/health/',
        'description': 'Elasticsearch-powered search'
    }
}

def create_microservice_dns(subdomain, config):
    """Create DNS record for microservice"""
    
    # Create health check
    health_check = route53.create_health_check(
        HealthCheckConfig={
            'Type': 'HTTPS',
            'ResourcePath': config['health_check'],
            'FullyQualifiedDomainName': config['alb'],
            'Port': 443,
            'RequestInterval': 30,
            'FailureThreshold': 2
        }
    )
    hc_id = health_check['HealthCheck']['Id']
    
    # Create Alias record pointing to ALB
    route53.change_resource_record_sets(
        HostedZoneId=ZONE_ID,
        ChangeBatch={
            'Changes': [{
                'Action': 'CREATE',
                'ResourceRecordSet': {
                    'Name': subdomain,
                    'Type': 'A',
                    'AliasTarget': {
                        'HostedZoneId': 'Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K',  # ALB hosted zone
                        'DNSName': config['alb'],
                        'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                    }
                }
            }]
        }
    )
    
    print(f"✅ Created DNS for {subdomain}")
    print(f"   ALB: {config['alb']}")
    print(f"   Health Check: {hc_id}")
    return hc_id

# Deploy all microservices
print("🚀 Configuring Route 53 for Microservices Architecture...\n")
health_checks = {}

for subdomain, config in services.items():
    hc_id = create_microservice_dns(subdomain, config)
    health_checks[subdomain] = hc_id
    print()

print("✅ Microservices DNS Configuration Complete!")
print(f"\n📊 Services deployed: {len(services)}")
print("\n🔍 Test services:")
for subdomain in services.keys():
    print(f"  curl -I https://{subdomain}/health/")
Service Discovery for ECS/Fargate
# Create private hosted zone for service discovery
aws route53 create-hosted-zone \
    --name internal.anihpj.com \
    --vpc VPCRegion=us-east-1,VPCId=vpc-abc123 \
    --caller-reference $(date +%s) \
    --hosted-zone-config \
        Comment="Private zone for anihpj microservices",\
        PrivateZone=true

# Create service discovery namespace
aws servicediscovery create-private-dns-namespace \
    --name internal.anihpj.com \
    --vpc vpc-abc123 \
    --description "Service discovery for anihpj microservices"

# Create service discovery service
aws servicediscovery create-service \
    --name jobs-service \
    --namespace-id ns-abc123 \
    --dns-config \
        NamespaceId=ns-abc123,\
        RoutingPolicy=WEIGHTED,\
        DnsRecords=[{Type=A,TTL=60}] \
    --health-check-custom-config FailureThreshold=1

# Result: ECS tasks automatically register as:
# jobs-service.internal.anihpj.com
# Returns IPs of all healthy tasks

# Use in Django settings
DNS_RESOLVER = "jobs-service.internal.anihpj.com"
🔀 Traffic Splitting for Canary Deployments
Phase v1.0 Weight v2.0 Weight Duration
Initial 100% 0% -
Canary 95% 5% 30 min
Ramp Up 75% 25% 1 hour
Half Split 50% 50% 2 hours
Full Rollout 0% 100% -
💡 Microservices Best Practices with Route 53
  • Subdomain Naming - Use descriptive names: auth.api.anihpj.com, not service1.anihpj.com
  • Private Hosted Zones - Keep internal services (databases, caches) private
  • Health Checks - Every microservice needs /health/ endpoint
  • Low TTL - Use 60s TTL for faster service updates
  • Service Discovery - Use AWS Cloud Map for ECS/Fargate auto-registration
  • Weighted Routing - Enable canary deployments and gradual rollouts
  • Cost Optimization - Use Alias records for AWS services (FREE)
📊 Microservices Benefits for anihpj:
  • Independent Scaling - Scale search service separately from job listings
  • Zero-Downtime Deploys - Update one service without affecting others
  • Fault Isolation - If search fails, users can still browse jobs
  • Team Autonomy - Different teams manage different services
  • Cost Efficiency - Right-size resources per service (not monolith)

🔧 10. Troubleshooting ▼

Common DNS issues and resolution strategies.

10.1 Common Issues & Solutions

⚠️ Top 10 Route 53 Issues
Issue Symptoms Solution
NXDOMAIN Domain doesn't exist Wait 24-48h for NS propagation
502 Bad Gateway DNS resolves, but ALB fails Check ALB target health
Slow DNS Resolution > 500ms query time Increase TTL, use Alias records
Health Check Failures Endpoint marked unhealthy Verify /health/ endpoint returns 200
DisallowedHost Error Django rejects request Add domain to ALLOWED_HOSTS

10.2 Diagnostic Tools

Complete DNS Diagnostics for anihpj
# 1. Check DNS resolution
dig anihpj.com +short
# Expected: IP address or CNAME
# Problem: NXDOMAIN → NS records not updated at registrar

# 2. Test specific name server
dig @ns-123.awsdns-45.com anihpj.com
# Verifies Route 53 has correct records

# 3. Trace DNS query path
dig anihpj.com +trace
# Shows full resolution from root to authoritative NS

# 4. Check all record types
dig anihpj.com ANY
# Returns A, AAAA, MX, TXT, NS, SOA records

# 5. Verify TTL
dig anihpj.com | grep "^anihpj.com"
# Shows TTL value (e.g., anihpj.com. 300 IN A 52.45.123.89)

# 6. Test from multiple locations
for location in us-east-1 eu-west-1 ap-south-1; do
    echo "Testing from $location:"
    dig @ns-123.awsdns-45.com anihpj.com +short
done

# 7. Check DNS propagation globally
# Visit: https://www.whatsmydns.net/#A/anihpj.com

# 8. Test HTTPS certificate
openssl s_client -connect anihpj.com:443 -servername anihpj.com
# Verifies SSL/TLS handshake

# 9. Check health check status
aws route53 get-health-check-status \
    --health-check-id abc123-def456 \
    --query 'HealthCheckObservations[*].[Region,StatusReport.Status]' \
    --output table

# 10. Query Route 53 logs
aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name /aws/route53/anihpj.com \
    --filter-pattern "anihpj.com" \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s)000 \
    --query 'events[*].message' \
    --output table

10.3 DNS Propagation Issues

🕐 Understanding Propagation Delays
DNS Propagation Timeline:
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ T+0 min:   Record created in Route 53                      │
│            - Change ID returned (e.g., C1234567890ABC)     │
│                                                             │
│ T+1 min:   Route 53 propagates to all edge locations      │
│            - Query Route 53 directly → sees new record     │
│                                                             │
│ T+5 min:   ISP DNS resolvers start seeing changes         │
│            - Depends on TTL of old record                  │
│            - If TTL was 300s (5 min), old record expires   │
│                                                             │
│ T+24 hr:   ~80% of global DNS resolvers updated           │
│            - Some ISPs cache longer than TTL               │
│                                                             │
│ T+48 hr:   ~99% of resolvers updated                      │
│            - Considered "fully propagated"                 │
│                                                             │
│ BEST PRACTICE FOR ANIHPJ:                                  │
│ 1. Reduce TTL to 60s (24 hours before change)             │
│ 2. Make DNS record change                                  │
│ 3. Wait 60 seconds for new record to propagate            │
│ 4. Increase TTL back to 300s (to reduce query costs)      │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

10.4 Health Check Debugging

Why Is My Health Check Failing?
# Debug Route 53 health check failures
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')

def debug_health_check(health_check_id):
    # Get health check config
    hc = route53.get_health_check(HealthCheckId=health_check_id)
    config = hc['HealthCheck']['HealthCheckConfig']
    
    print(f"🔍 Health Check Configuration:")
    print(f"  Target: {config.get('FullyQualifiedDomainName', config.get('IPAddress'))}")
    print(f"  Protocol: {config['Type']}")
    print(f"  Port: {config.get('Port', 'N/A')}")
    print(f"  Path: {config.get('ResourcePath', '/')}")
    print(f"  Interval: {config.get('RequestInterval', 30)}s")
    print(f"  Failure Threshold: {config.get('FailureThreshold', 3)}")
    
    # Get current status from all regions
    status = route53.get_health_check_status(HealthCheckId=health_check_id)
    
    print(f"\n💚 Health Check Status (15 regions):")
    healthy = 0
    unhealthy = 0
    
    for obs in status['HealthCheckObservations']:
        region = obs['Region']
        status_report = obs['StatusReport']['Status']
        
        if status_report == 'Success':
            healthy += 1
            icon = '✅'
        else:
            unhealthy += 1
            icon = '❌'
            # Print error details for failed checks
            print(f"  {icon} {region}: {status_report}")
            if 'StatusReport' in obs and 'StatusReport' in obs['StatusReport']:
                print(f"      Error: {obs['StatusReport'].get('StatusReport', 'Unknown')}")
    
    print(f"\n📊 Summary: {healthy} healthy, {unhealthy} unhealthy")
    
    # Get CloudWatch metrics
    response = cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
        Namespace='AWS/Route53',
        MetricName='HealthCheckStatus',
        Dimensions=[{'Name': 'HealthCheckId', 'Value': health_check_id}],
        StartTime=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=1),
        EndTime=datetime.now(),
        Period=300,
        Statistics=['Average']
    )
    
    print(f"\n📈 Last Hour Metrics:")
    for point in sorted(response['Datapoints'], key=lambda x: x['Timestamp']):
        timestamp = point['Timestamp'].strftime('%H:%M')
        health_pct = point['Average'] * 100
        print(f"  {timestamp}: {health_pct:.0f}% healthy")

# Run diagnostics
debug_health_check('abc123-def456')

# Common failure reasons:
# 1. Security group doesn't allow Route 53 IPs (0.0.0.0/0 on port 443)
# 2. /health/ endpoint returns 404 or 500
# 3. Response time > 10 seconds (timeout)
# 4. SSL certificate expired or invalid
# 5. String matching enabled but response doesn't contain expected string
🚨 Emergency Troubleshooting: If anihpj.com is down, check in this order: (1) Route 53 health checks, (2) ALB target health, (3) Security groups, (4) Django /health/ endpoint, (5) DNS propagation. Use AWS Personal Health Dashboard for service-wide issues.

10.4 FAQs

❓ Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: How long does DNS propagation take with Route 53?

A: Route 53 updates propagate to all AWS edge locations within 60 seconds. However, end-user ISPs may cache old records based on TTL. With TTL=60s, most users see changes within 1-2 minutes. Traditional DNS providers take 24-48 hours.

Q2: What's the difference between Alias records and CNAME records?

A: Alias records are FREE (no query charges) and can be used at the root domain (anihpj.com). CNAME records cost $0.40/million queries and cannot be used at root. Always use Alias for AWS resources (ALB, CloudFront, S3).

Q3: Can I use Route 53 with a domain registered elsewhere?

A: Yes! Register domain anywhere (GoDaddy, Namecheap), then update name servers at your registrar to point to Route 53's 4 NS records. You get Route 53's routing features without transferring domain registration.

Q4: How much does Route 53 cost for a small job portal like anihpj?

A: Typical costs for small production site:

  • Hosted zone: $0.50/month
  • Standard queries (1M/month): $0.40
  • Health checks (2 endpoints): $2.00
  • Alias record queries: FREE
  • Total: $3-6/month

Q5: Can Route 53 handle DDoS attacks on DNS?

A: Yes! Route 53 includes AWS Shield Standard (free DDoS protection) with unlimited query capacity. During attacks, Route 53's anycast network distributes queries across 200+ edge locations, absorbing volumetric attacks automatically.

Q6: What's the SLA for Route 53?

A: Route 53 guarantees 100% availability SLA. If availability drops below 100% in a month, AWS provides service credits. In practice, Route 53 has never had a global outage since launch (2010).

Q7: How do I implement disaster recovery with Route 53?

A: Use Failover routing with health checks. Configure primary region (us-east-1) as PRIMARY with health check, and backup region (eu-west-1) as SECONDARY. If primary fails 2 consecutive health checks, Route 53 automatically routes to backup. RTO: ~2 minutes.

Q8: Can I use Route 53 for internal/private DNS?

A: Yes! Create private hosted zones for VPC-only DNS. Example: internal.anihpj.com for database endpoints (db.internal.anihpj.com), cache servers, and internal APIs. Private zones work only within specified VPCs.

Q9: What's the best TTL setting for production?

A: Depends on use case:

  • Dynamic/failover records: 60 seconds (fast changes)
  • Production applications: 300 seconds (5 minutes, balanced)
  • Static content/CDN: 3600-86400 seconds (1-24 hours)
  • Email records (MX): 3600 seconds (rarely change)

Q10: How do I migrate from another DNS provider to Route 53?

A: Follow these steps:

  1. Export current DNS records from old provider
  2. Create hosted zone in Route 53 (anihpj.com)
  3. Import all DNS records to Route 53
  4. Reduce TTL to 60s at old provider (24 hours before switch)
  5. Update name servers at domain registrar to Route 53 NS
  6. Wait 48 hours, verify with dig anihpj.com
  7. Delete old DNS provider (optional)

Q11: Can I automate DNS changes in my CI/CD pipeline?

A: Absolutely! Use AWS CLI in CI/CD or Infrastructure as Code:

  • Terraform: aws_route53_record resource
  • CloudFormation: AWS::Route53::RecordSet
  • AWS CLI: aws route53 change-resource-record-sets
  • Boto3: route53.change_resource_record_sets()

Q12: Does Route 53 support DNSSEC?

A: Yes! Enable DNSSEC signing on your hosted zone, get the DS record, and add it to your domain registrar. DNSSEC prevents DNS spoofing attacks by cryptographically signing DNS responses. Same cost as regular hosted zone ($0.50/month).

💬 Still Have Questions? Check AWS Route 53 documentation at docs.aws.amazon.com/route53 or post in AWS re:Post forums. For anihpj-specific DNS questions, review this documentation's use cases and troubleshooting sections.

⌨️ 11. API & CLI Reference ▼

Automate DNS management with AWS CLI, Boto3, CloudFormation, and Terraform.

11.1 AWS CLI Commands

Essential Route 53 CLI Commands
# List all hosted zones
aws route53 list-hosted-zones

# Get specific hosted zone details
aws route53 get-hosted-zone --id Z1234567890ABC

# List all records in hosted zone
aws route53 list-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC

# Create A record
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch file://change-batch.json

# Delete record
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "DELETE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "old.anihpj.com",
      "Type": "CNAME",
      "TTL": 300,
      "ResourceRecords": [{"Value": "anihpj.com"}]
    }
  }]
}'

# Test DNS resolution
aws route53 test-dns-answer \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --record-name anihpj.com \
    --record-type A

# List health checks
aws route53 list-health-checks

# Get health check status
aws route53 get-health-check-status \
    --health-check-id abc123-def456

# Enable query logging
aws route53 create-query-logging-config \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --cloud-watch-logs-log-group-arn \
        arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789:log-group:/aws/route53/anihpj

11.2 Boto3 (Python SDK)

Route 53 Automation with Python
# Complete Route 53 management script for anihpj
import boto3
import json

class Route53Manager:
    def __init__(self, hosted_zone_id):
        self.route53 = boto3.client('route53')
        self.hosted_zone_id = hosted_zone_id
    
    def create_alias_record(self, name, target_dns, target_zone_id):
        """Create alias record pointing to AWS resource"""
        response = self.route53.change_resource_record_sets(
            HostedZoneId=self.hosted_zone_id,
            ChangeBatch={
                'Comment': f'Create alias for {name}',
                'Changes': [{
                    'Action': 'UPSERT',
                    'ResourceRecordSet': {
                        'Name': name,
                        'Type': 'A',
                        'AliasTarget': {
                            'HostedZoneId': target_zone_id,
                            'DNSName': target_dns,
                            'EvaluateTargetHealth': True
                        }
                    }
                }]
            }
        )
        return response['ChangeInfo']['Id']
    
    def create_cname_record(self, name, target, ttl=300):
        """Create CNAME record"""
        response = self.route53.change_resource_record_sets(
            HostedZoneId=self.hosted_zone_id,
            ChangeBatch={
                'Changes': [{
                    'Action': 'UPSERT',
                    'ResourceRecordSet': {
                        'Name': name,
                        'Type': 'CNAME',
                        'TTL': ttl,
                        'ResourceRecords': [{'Value': target}]
                    }
                }]
            }
        )
        return response['ChangeInfo']['Id']
    
    def get_all_records(self):
        """List all DNS records"""
        records = []
        paginator = self.route53.get_paginator('list_resource_record_sets')
        
        for page in paginator.paginate(HostedZoneId=self.hosted_zone_id):
            records.extend(page['ResourceRecordSets'])
        
        return records
    
    def export_zone_file(self, filename):
        """Export zone to BIND format"""
        records = self.get_all_records()
        
        with open(filename, 'w') as f:
            f.write("; Zone file for anihpj.com\n")
            f.write(f"; Exported: {datetime.now()}\n\n")
            
            for record in records:
                name = record['Name']
                rtype = record['Type']
                ttl = record.get('TTL', 'ALIAS')
                
                if 'ResourceRecords' in record:
                    for rr in record['ResourceRecords']:
                        f.write(f"{name}\t{ttl}\tIN\t{rtype}\t{rr['Value']}\n")
                elif 'AliasTarget' in record:
                    f.write(f"{name}\tALIAS\tIN\t{rtype}\t{record['AliasTarget']['DNSName']}\n")
        
        print(f"✅ Zone exported to {filename}")

# Usage
manager = Route53Manager('Z1234567890ABC')

# Create main domain alias
manager.create_alias_record(
    'anihpj.com',
    'anihpj-alb.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
    'Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K'
)

# Create www CNAME
manager.create_cname_record('www.anihpj.com', 'anihpj.com')

# Export zone file for backup
manager.export_zone_file('anihpj-zone-backup.txt')

11.3 CloudFormation

Infrastructure as Code (CloudFormation)
# route53-stack.yaml - Complete DNS setup for anihpj
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Route 53 configuration for anihpj job portal'

Parameters:
  DomainName:
    Type: String
    Default: anihpj.com
    Description: Domain name
  
  ALBDNSName:
    Type: String
    Description: ALB DNS name
  
  ALBHostedZoneId:
    Type: String
    Default: Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K
    Description: ALB hosted zone ID (us-east-1)

Resources:
  # Hosted Zone
  HostedZone:
    Type: AWS::Route53::HostedZone
    Properties:
      Name: !Ref DomainName
      HostedZoneConfig:
        Comment: !Sub '${DomainName} job portal'
  
  # Main domain A record (Alias to ALB)
  MainDomainRecord:
    Type: AWS::Route53::RecordSet
    Properties:
      HostedZoneId: !Ref HostedZone
      Name: !Ref DomainName
      Type: A
      AliasTarget:
        HostedZoneId: !Ref ALBHostedZoneId
        DNSName: !Ref ALBDNSName
        EvaluateTargetHealth: true
  
  # WWW CNAME
  WWWRecord:
    Type: AWS::Route53::RecordSet
    Properties:
      HostedZoneId: !Ref HostedZone
      Name: !Sub 'www.${DomainName}'
      Type: CNAME
      TTL: 300
      ResourceRecords:
        - !Ref DomainName
  
  # Health Check
  HealthCheck:
    Type: AWS::Route53::HealthCheck
    Properties:
      HealthCheckConfig:
        Type: HTTPS
        ResourcePath: /health/
        FullyQualifiedDomainName: !Ref DomainName
        Port: 443
        RequestInterval: 30
        FailureThreshold: 3
        MeasureLatency: true
      HealthCheckTags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${DomainName}-health-check'

Outputs:
  HostedZoneId:
    Description: Hosted Zone ID
    Value: !Ref HostedZone
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-HostedZoneId'
  
  NameServers:
    Description: Name Servers (update at registrar)
    Value: !Join [', ', !GetAtt HostedZone.NameServers]

11.4 Terraform

Terraform Configuration
# route53.tf - Terraform for anihpj DNS
terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
  }
}

provider "aws" {
  region = "us-east-1"
}

# Hosted Zone
resource "aws_route53_zone" "main" {
  name    = "anihpj.com"
  comment = "Job portal main domain"
  
  tags = {
    Environment = "production"
    Project     = "anihpj"
  }
}

# Main domain A record (Alias to ALB)
resource "aws_route53_record" "main" {
  zone_id = aws_route53_zone.main.zone_id
  name    = "anihpj.com"
  type    = "A"
  
  alias {
    name                   = aws_lb.main.dns_name
    zone_id                = aws_lb.main.zone_id
    evaluate_target_health = true
  }
}

# WWW CNAME
resource "aws_route53_record" "www" {
  zone_id = aws_route53_zone.main.zone_id
  name    = "www.anihpj.com"
  type    = "CNAME"
  ttl     = 300
  records = ["anihpj.com"]
}

# API subdomain
resource "aws_route53_record" "api" {
  zone_id = aws_route53_zone.main.zone_id
  name    = "api.anihpj.com"
  type    = "A"
  
  alias {
    name                   = aws_lb.api.dns_name
    zone_id                = aws_lb.api.zone_id
    evaluate_target_health = true
  }
}

# Health Check
resource "aws_route53_health_check" "main" {
  fqdn              = "anihpj.com"
  port              = 443
  type              = "HTTPS"
  resource_path     = "/health/"
  failure_threshold = 3
  request_interval  = 30
  measure_latency   = true
  
  tags = {
    Name = "anihpj-health-check"
  }
}

# CloudWatch Alarm for Health Check
resource "aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm" "health_check" {
  alarm_name          = "route53-health-check-failed"
  comparison_operator = "LessThanThreshold"
  evaluation_periods  = 2
  metric_name         = "HealthCheckStatus"
  namespace           = "AWS/Route53"
  period              = 60
  statistic           = "Minimum"
  threshold           = 1
  alarm_description   = "Alert when health check fails"
  alarm_actions       = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]
  
  dimensions = {
    HealthCheckId = aws_route53_health_check.main.id
  }
}

# Outputs
output "name_servers" {
  description = "Name servers to configure at registrar"
  value       = aws_route53_zone.main.name_servers
}

output "hosted_zone_id" {
  description = "Hosted zone ID"
  value       = aws_route53_zone.main.zone_id
}
🔧 Automation Recommendation: Use Terraform for infrastructure as code. Store state in S3 with DynamoDB locking. This allows team collaboration and prevents accidental DNS changes. For anihpj, maintain separate Terraform workspaces for dev/staging/prod.

🔗 12. AWS Service Integration ▼

Seamless integration with AWS services for anihpj infrastructure.

🔗 Route 53 AWS Integrations Overview
ELB/ALB: Alias records, zero-query cost, health checks
CloudFront: CDN distribution with custom domains
S3 Static Website: Host static sites with DNS routing
API Gateway: Custom domains for REST/WebSocket APIs
Elastic Beanstalk: Environment URLs with CNAME
CloudWatch: DNS query logging and metrics

12.1 Integration with ELB

Route 53 → Application Load Balancer
# Get ALB details
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers \
    --names anihpj-alb \
    --query 'LoadBalancers[0].DNSName' \
    --output text)

ALB_ZONE=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers \
    --names anihpj-alb \
    --query 'LoadBalancers[0].CanonicalHostedZoneId' \
    --output text)

echo "ALB DNS: $ALB_DNS"
echo "ALB Zone: $ALB_ZONE"

# Create Alias record (FREE queries)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "UPSERT",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "'$ALB_ZONE'",
        "DNSName": "'$ALB_DNS'",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# Benefits:
# ✓ FREE DNS queries (no $0.40/million charge)
# ✓ Automatic health check inheritance from ALB
# ✓ No TTL management (AWS handles it)
# ✓ Works at zone apex (anihpj.com, not just www.anihpj.com)

12.2 Integration with CloudFront

CDN for Static Assets
# Create CloudFront distribution and Route 53 record
import boto3

cloudfront = boto3.client('cloudfront')
route53 = boto3.client('route53')

# 1. Create CloudFront distribution for anihpj static files
distribution = cloudfront.create_distribution(
    DistributionConfig={
        'CallerReference': 'anihpj-static-assets',
        'Comment': 'CDN for anihpj job portal static files',
        'Enabled': True,
        'DefaultCacheBehavior': {
            'TargetOriginId': 'anihpj-s3-static',
            'ViewerProtocolPolicy': 'redirect-to-https',
            'AllowedMethods': {'Quantity': 2, 'Items': ['GET', 'HEAD']},
            'ForwardedValues': {
                'QueryString': False,
                'Cookies': {'Forward': 'none'}
            },
            'MinTTL': 86400,  # 24 hours
            'DefaultTTL': 86400,
            'MaxTTL': 31536000  # 1 year
        },
        'Origins': {
            'Quantity': 1,
            'Items': [{
                'Id': 'anihpj-s3-static',
                'DomainName': 'anihpj-static.s3.amazonaws.com',
                'S3OriginConfig': {
                    'OriginAccessIdentity': ''
                }
            }]
        },
        'Aliases': {
            'Quantity': 1,
            'Items': ['cdn.anihpj.com']
        },
        'ViewerCertificate': {
            'ACMCertificateArn': 'arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789:certificate/abc-123',
            'SSLSupportMethod': 'sni-only',
            'MinimumProtocolVersion': 'TLSv1.2_2021'
        }
    }
)

cf_domain = distribution['Distribution']['DomainName']
print(f"CloudFront domain: {cf_domain}")

# 2. Create Route 53 alias record
route53.change_resource_record_sets(
    HostedZoneId='Z1234567890ABC',
    ChangeBatch={
        'Changes': [{
            'Action': 'CREATE',
            'ResourceRecordSet': {
                'Name': 'cdn.anihpj.com',
                'Type': 'A',
                'AliasTarget': {
                    'HostedZoneId': 'Z2FDTNDATAQYW2',  # CloudFront zone
                    'DNSName': cf_domain,
                    'EvaluateTargetHealth': False
                }
            }
        }]
    }
)

print("✅ CDN configured: https://cdn.anihpj.com")

# Django settings.py update:
# STATIC_URL = 'https://cdn.anihpj.com/static/'
# MEDIA_URL = 'https://cdn.anihpj.com/media/'

12.3 Integration with S3

S3 Static Website Hosting
# Create S3 bucket for static site
aws s3 mb s3://anihpj-static-site

# Enable static website hosting
aws s3 website s3://anihpj-static-site \
    --index-document index.html \
    --error-document error.html

# Upload static files
aws s3 sync ./static/ s3://anihpj-static-site/static/ --acl public-read

# Create Route 53 alias to S3 website endpoint
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "CREATE",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "static.anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z3AQBSTGFYJSTF",
        "DNSName": "s3-website-us-east-1.amazonaws.com",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": false
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# Access static site: https://static.anihpj.com

12.4 Integration with API Gateway

🔌 AWS Service Integrations Summary
Service Use Case Record Type Cost
ALB/NLB Main web app Alias A FREE
CloudFront CDN for static assets Alias A FREE
S3 Website Static site hosting Alias A FREE
API Gateway REST/GraphQL API Alias A FREE
Elastic Beanstalk Managed Django app Alias A FREE
SES Email sending/receiving MX, TXT $0.40/M
💡 Integration Best Practice: Always use Alias records for AWS services. They're FREE, support health checks, and work at the zone apex. For anihpj, this saves $5-20/month vs CNAME records.

🚚 13. Migration to Route 53 ▼

Migrate anihpj DNS from other providers to Route 53 with zero downtime.

13.1 DNS Provider Migration

🔄 Zero-Downtime Migration Strategy

Migrating anihpj.com from GoDaddy/Cloudflare/Namecheap to Route 53 without affecting users:

Migration Timeline (Total: 72 hours for safety):
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ T-48 hours: Preparation Phase                                 │
│   1. Export all DNS records from current provider             │
│   2. Create Route 53 hosted zone (anihpj.com)                 │
│   3. Import all records to Route 53                           │
│   4. Test Route 53 directly: dig @ns-123.awsdns-45.com        │
│   5. Lower TTL to 300s (5 minutes) at current provider        │
│                                                                 │
│ T-24 hours: Pre-Migration Verification                        │
│   1. Verify all records match (A, MX, TXT, CNAME)            │
│   2. Test email delivery with new MX records                  │
│   3. Document 4 Route 53 name servers                         │
│                                                                 │
│ T-0 hours: Name Server Switch                                 │
│   1. Go to domain registrar (GoDaddy/etc)                     │
│   2. Update name servers to Route 53 NS records               │
│   3. Save changes (takes 5-15 minutes to submit)              │
│                                                                 │
│ T+5 min: Initial Propagation                                  │
