│   1. Test: dig anihpj.com @8.8.8.8 (Google DNS)              │
│   2. Should see Route 53 name servers                         │
│   3. Browse anihpj.com from multiple locations                │
│                                                                 │
│ T+2 hours: Majority Propagation                               │
│   - 80% of global DNS resolvers updated                       │
│   - Monitor CloudWatch for query increase                     │
│                                                                 │
│ T+24 hours: Full Propagation                                  │
│   - 99%+ of resolvers updated                                 │
│   - Safe to delete old DNS provider                           │
│                                                                 │
│ T+48 hours: Cleanup                                           │
│   - Increase TTL back to 300-3600s                            │
│   - Cancel old DNS provider subscription                       │
│   - Document new Route 53 setup                               │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Step-by-Step Migration Script
# Complete migration automation for anihpj.com
import boto3
import time
from datetime import datetime

route53 = boto3.client('route53')

# Step 1: Create hosted zone in Route 53
def create_hosted_zone():
    response = route53.create_hosted_zone(
        Name='anihpj.com',
        CallerReference=str(datetime.now().timestamp()),
        HostedZoneConfig={
            'Comment': 'Production DNS for anihpj job portal',
            'PrivateZone': False
        }
    )
    zone_id = response['HostedZone']['Id']
    name_servers = response['DelegationSet']['NameServers']
    
    print(f"✅ Created hosted zone: {zone_id}")
    print("\n📋 Update these name servers at your registrar:")
    for i, ns in enumerate(name_servers, 1):
        print(f"   {i}. {ns}")
    
    return zone_id, name_servers

# Step 2: Import existing DNS records
def import_dns_records(zone_id):
    """Import records from exported DNS file"""
    
    # Example records from GoDaddy export
    records = [
        {'Name': 'anihpj.com', 'Type': 'A', 'TTL': 300, 'Value': '52.45.123.89'},
        {'Name': 'www.anihpj.com', 'Type': 'CNAME', 'TTL': 300, 'Value': 'anihpj.com'},
        {'Name': 'api.anihpj.com', 'Type': 'A', 'TTL': 300, 'Value': '54.123.45.67'},
        {'Name': 'anihpj.com', 'Type': 'MX', 'TTL': 3600, 'Value': '10 mail.anihpj.com'},
        {'Name': 'anihpj.com', 'Type': 'TXT', 'TTL': 300, 
         'Value': '"v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all"'},
    ]
    
    for record in records:
        try:
            route53.change_resource_record_sets(
                HostedZoneId=zone_id,
                ChangeBatch={
                    'Changes': [{
                        'Action': 'CREATE',
                        'ResourceRecordSet': {
                            'Name': record['Name'],
                            'Type': record['Type'],
                            'TTL': record['TTL'],
                            'ResourceRecords': [{'Value': record['Value']}]
                        }
                    }]
                }
            )
            print(f"✅ Imported {record['Type']} record for {record['Name']}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Failed to import {record['Name']}: {e}")

# Step 3: Verify records before migration
def verify_records(zone_id):
    """Compare Route 53 records with current DNS"""
    
    response = route53.list_resource_record_sets(HostedZoneId=zone_id)
    records = response['ResourceRecordSets']
    
    print("\n🔍 Current Route 53 Records:")
    for record in records:
        if record['Type'] in ['A', 'AAAA', 'CNAME', 'MX', 'TXT']:
            print(f"   {record['Type']:6} {record['Name']:30} → {record.get('ResourceRecords', [{}])[0].get('Value', 'N/A')}")

# Step 4: Monitor post-migration
def monitor_migration(zone_id):
    """Monitor DNS query traffic after migration"""
    
    cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
    
    print("\n📊 Monitoring DNS queries...")
    for i in range(12):  # Monitor for 1 hour (5 min intervals)
        response = cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
            Namespace='AWS/Route53',
            MetricName='QueryCount',
            Dimensions=[{'Name': 'HostedZoneId', 'Value': zone_id}],
            StartTime=datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(minutes=5),
            EndTime=datetime.utcnow(),
            Period=300,
            Statistics=['Sum']
        )
        
        if response['Datapoints']:
            query_count = response['Datapoints'][0]['Sum']
            print(f"   {datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M')} - Queries: {int(query_count):,}")
        
        time.sleep(300)  # Wait 5 minutes

# Execute migration
print("🚀 Starting Route 53 Migration for anihpj.com\n")
zone_id, name_servers = create_hosted_zone()
import_dns_records(zone_id)
verify_records(zone_id)

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("⚠️  MANUAL STEP REQUIRED:")
print("1. Go to your domain registrar (GoDaddy/Namecheap/etc)")
print("2. Find DNS settings → Name Servers")
print("3. Replace current name servers with these 4:")
for ns in name_servers:
    print(f"   - {ns}")
print("4. Save changes (takes 5-15 minutes)")
print("5. Run this script again with --monitor flag")
print("="*60)

# After name server update:
# monitor_migration(zone_id)
Verification Commands
# 1. Check current name servers
dig anihpj.com NS +short
# Before migration: shows old provider NS
# After migration: shows Route 53 NS (ns-XXX.awsdns-XX.com)

# 2. Test directly against Route 53
dig @ns-123.awsdns-45.com anihpj.com +short
# Should return correct IP immediately

# 3. Check propagation from multiple locations
for dns in 8.8.8.8 1.1.1.1 208.67.222.222; do
    echo "Testing $dns:"
    dig @$dns anihpj.com +short
done

# 4. Verify all record types
dig anihpj.com ANY +noall +answer

# 5. Test email delivery
dig anihpj.com MX +short

# 6. Check WHOIS for name server update
whois anihpj.com | grep "Name Server"

# 7. Global propagation check
# Visit: https://www.whatsmydns.net/#NS/anihpj.com

13.2 On-Premises DNS to Route 53

Migrate Corporate DNS to Route 53
# Scenario: Company DNS on BIND/Windows DNS → Route 53

# Step 1: Export zone file from BIND
# On your BIND server:
named-checkzone anihpj.com /var/named/anihpj.com.zone
cat /var/named/anihpj.com.zone > anihpj-export.zone

# Sample zone file format:
# $ORIGIN anihpj.com.
# $TTL 300
# @       IN SOA   ns1.anihpj.com. admin.anihpj.com. (
#                  2025010101 ; serial
#                  7200       ; refresh
#                  3600       ; retry
#                  1209600    ; expire
#                  300 )      ; minimum TTL
# @       IN NS    ns1.anihpj.com.
# @       IN A     52.45.123.89
# www     IN CNAME anihpj.com.
# api     IN A     54.123.45.67

# Step 2: Convert BIND zone to Route 53 format
python3 << 'EOF'
import boto3

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
ZONE_ID = 'Z1234567890ABC'

# Parse BIND zone file and create Route 53 records
with open('anihpj-export.zone', 'r') as f:
    for line in f:
        if line.startswith(';') or line.startswith('$') or not line.strip():
            continue
        
        parts = line.split()
        if len(parts) >= 4:
            name = parts[0] if parts[0] != '@' else 'anihpj.com'
            if not name.endswith('.'):
                name = f"{name}.anihpj.com"
            
            rtype = parts[2] if parts[1] == 'IN' else parts[1]
            value = ' '.join(parts[3:])
            
            if rtype in ['A', 'AAAA', 'CNAME', 'MX', 'TXT']:
                print(f"Importing {rtype} record: {name} → {value}")
                # Create record in Route 53...
EOF

# Step 3: Setup hybrid DNS with Route 53 Resolver
aws route53resolver create-resolver-endpoint \
    --creator-request-id $(date +%s) \
    --security-group-ids sg-abc123 \
    --direction INBOUND \
    --ip-addresses \
        SubnetId=subnet-abc123,Ip=10.0.1.100 \
        SubnetId=subnet-def456,Ip=10.0.2.100 \
    --name anihpj-inbound-resolver

# Result: Corporate network can query:
# - 10.0.1.100 and 10.0.2.100 for Route 53 resolution
# - Allows gradual migration

# Step 4: Configure on-prem DNS forwarders
# Add these forwarders in BIND/Windows DNS:
#   anihpj.com → 10.0.1.100, 10.0.2.100

# Step 5: Monitor and cutover
# - Test resolution from corporate network
# - Gradually move services to AWS
# - Finally update public name servers
⚠️ Migration Checklist
Task Status
Export all DNS records from current provider ☐
Create Route 53 hosted zone (anihpj.com) ☐
Import all records (A, MX, TXT, CNAME) ☐
Test Route 53 directly (dig @ns-123.awsdns-45.com) ☐
Lower TTL to 300s at current provider (48 hours before) ☐
Document 4 Route 53 name servers ☐
Update name servers at domain registrar ☐
Monitor DNS propagation (24-48 hours) ☐
Verify email delivery (test MX records) ☐
Cancel old DNS provider subscription ☐
🎯 Migration Success Metrics:
  • Zero Downtime: Users never see anihpj.com unreachable
  • Email Continuity: All emails delivered throughout migration
  • Fast Cutover: 99% propagation within 24 hours
  • Cost Savings: Reduce DNS costs by 50-70% vs commercial providers
  • Performance Boost: < 50ms DNS query time globally

🛡️ 14. Compliance & Governance ▼

Enterprise compliance and security governance for Route 53.

14.1 Compliance Certifications

📜 Route 53 Compliance Certifications
Certification Description anihpj Use Case
PCI DSS Level 1 Payment card data protection Job application payments
HIPAA Eligible Healthcare data (with BAA) Healthcare job listings
ISO 27001 Information security management Enterprise client requirements
SOC 1/2/3 Service organization controls Audit reports for clients
GDPR EU data protection regulation European user privacy
FedRAMP US government cloud security Government job contracts
FIPS 140-2 Cryptographic module validation Secure communications

💡 For anihpj: These certifications enable you to serve enterprise clients, government agencies, and healthcare organizations who require compliance documentation.

14.2 Security Summary

🔐 Route 53 Security Controls for anihpj

1. IAM & Access Control

  • ✅ Least-privilege IAM policies (specific to anihpj.com domain)
  • ✅ MFA required for users managing DNS
  • ✅ IAM roles for automation (CI/CD, Terraform)
  • ✅ CloudTrail logging of all DNS changes

2. DNSSEC & Integrity

  • ✅ DNSSEC enabled for cryptographic validation
  • ✅ DS record published at domain registrar
  • ✅ KSK (Key Signing Key) managed by AWS
  • ✅ Protection against DNS spoofing attacks

3. DDoS Protection

  • ✅ AWS Shield Standard (free) - automatic DDoS mitigation
  • ✅ Anycast network distributes queries across 200+ locations
  • ✅ Unlimited query capacity per hosted zone
  • ✅ CloudWatch alarms for anomalous query volumes

4. Monitoring & Auditing

  • ✅ Query logging to CloudWatch Logs
  • ✅ Health check status monitoring
  • ✅ CloudTrail audit trail (who changed what, when)
  • ✅ SNS alerts for health check failures

5. Private DNS & VPC Integration

  • ✅ Private hosted zones for internal services
  • ✅ Route 53 Resolver for hybrid cloud
  • ✅ VPC-only DNS (internal.anihpj.com)
  • ✅ Split-horizon DNS (public vs private views)
Compliance Audit Report Generator
# Generate compliance report for anihpj DNS
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

route53 = boto3.client('route53')
cloudtrail = boto3.client('cloudtrail')

def generate_compliance_report(zone_id):
    """Generate comprehensive security and compliance report"""
    
    print("=" * 70)
    print(f"Route 53 Compliance Report for anihpj.com")
    print(f"Generated: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}")
    print("=" * 70)
    
    # 1. DNSSEC Status
    try:
        dnssec = route53.get_dnssec(HostedZoneId=zone_id)
        status = dnssec['Status']['ServeSignature']
        print(f"\n✅ DNSSEC: {status}")
    except:
        print("\n❌ DNSSEC: NOT ENABLED")
    
    # 2. Query Logging
    try:
        logs = route53.list_query_logging_configs(HostedZoneId=zone_id)
        if logs['QueryLoggingConfigs']:
            print(f"✅ Query Logging: ENABLED")
        else:
            print(f"❌ Query Logging: NOT ENABLED")
    except:
        print("❌ Query Logging: NOT ENABLED")
    
    # 3. Recent DNS Changes (last 7 days)
    events = cloudtrail.lookup_events(
        LookupAttributes=[{
            'AttributeKey': 'ResourceType',
            'AttributeValue': 'AWS::Route53::HostedZone'
        }],
        StartTime=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=7),
        EndTime=datetime.now()
    )
    
    print(f"\n📊 DNS Changes (Last 7 Days): {len(events['Events'])}")
    for event in events['Events'][:5]:
        user = event.get('Username', 'Unknown')
        action = event['EventName']
        timestamp = event['EventTime'].strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')
        print(f"   - {timestamp} | {user:20} | {action}")
    
    # 4. Health Checks
    health_checks = route53.list_health_checks()['HealthChecks']
    print(f"\n💚 Active Health Checks: {len(health_checks)}")
    for hc in health_checks:
        hc_id = hc['Id']
        status = route53.get_health_check_status(HealthCheckId=hc_id)
        healthy_count = sum(1 for obs in status['HealthCheckObservations'] 
                           if obs['StatusReport']['Status'] == 'Success')
        total_count = len(status['HealthCheckObservations'])
        print(f"   - {hc_id}: {healthy_count}/{total_count} locations healthy")
    
    # 5. Record Count
    records = route53.list_resource_record_sets(HostedZoneId=zone_id)
    record_types = {}
    for record in records['ResourceRecordSets']:
        rtype = record['Type']
        record_types[rtype] = record_types.get(rtype, 0) + 1
    
    print(f"\n📝 DNS Records by Type:")
    for rtype, count in sorted(record_types.items()):
        print(f"   - {rtype:6}: {count} records")
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
    print("Compliance Status: ✅ PASS")
    print("=" * 70)

# Run report
generate_compliance_report('Z1234567890ABC')

🎉 Route 53 Documentation Complete!

You now have enterprise-grade DNS for anihpj job portal with:

  • ✅ 100% availability SLA
  • ✅ Global anycast network (200+ edge locations)
  • ✅ Multi-region failover (us-east-1 → eu-west-1)
  • ✅ Health checks with automatic traffic routing
  • ✅ DNSSEC for domain security
  • ✅ Query logging for threat detection
  • ✅ Infrastructure as Code (Terraform/CloudFormation)
  • ✅ Zero-downtime migration from any provider
  • ✅ 7 compliance certifications (PCI DSS, HIPAA, ISO, SOC, GDPR)

Total Cost: $5-10/month for production-ready DNS

AWS CloudFront

Category: Networking & Content Delivery (CDN)  |  Use Case: Global Content Delivery, Static/Dynamic Asset Acceleration  |  Pricing: Pay-per-GB transferred, Free Tier: 1TB out/month for 12 months  |  Last Updated: December 2025  |  Documentation: AWS CloudFront Docs

Fast, secure, and programmable content delivery network (CDN) that accelerates delivery of websites, APIs, video content, and other web assets. Delivers your anihpj job portal content to users globally with low latency through 450+ edge locations worldwide.

📋 Overview & Introduction

Amazon CloudFront is AWS's global Content Delivery Network (CDN) that caches and delivers your content from edge locations closest to your users. It integrates seamlessly with AWS services like S3, EC2, ELB, and Route 53 to provide fast, secure, and cost-effective content delivery for your anihpj Django application.

🚀 Quick Start Guide (5 Minutes):
  1. Create S3 bucket for static assets → Upload files
  2. Create CloudFront distribution → Point to S3 origin
  3. Configure cache behaviors → Set TTLs
  4. Update Django settings → Use CloudFront domain
  5. Test → Verify faster load times globally
🎯 CloudFront at a Glance
⚡ Performance
Sub-10ms latency
99% coverage globally
450+ edge locations
🔒 Security
DDoS protection (Shield)
Free SSL/TLS certs
WAF integration
💰 Cost Effective
Pay-as-you-go
1TB free (12 months)
$0.085/GB
🔧 Easy Setup
5-minute setup
No server management
AWS console/CLI/SDK
🌍 Global Reach
48 countries
90+ cities
6 continents
⚙️ Programmable
Lambda@Edge
CloudFront Functions
Edge computing
🎯 Key Benefits
  • Global Performance: 450+ Points of Presence (PoPs) in 90+ cities across 48 countries
  • Low Latency: Sub-10ms latency to 99% of global internet users
  • Security Built-In: AWS Shield Standard (DDoS), WAF, SSL/TLS, signed URLs/cookies
  • Cost Effective: Pay only for data transfer, no minimum commitments
  • Easy Integration: Works with S3, EC2, ALB, API Gateway, and custom origins
  • Programmable: Lambda@Edge and CloudFront Functions for edge computing
  • Real-Time Metrics: CloudWatch integration for monitoring and alerts
🎯 CDN Quick Comparison
CloudFront: 450+ PoPs, AWS native, Lambda@Edge, Shield DDoS
Cloudflare: 330+ PoPs, Free tier, DDoS included, Workers edge functions
Fastly: Instant purge, VCL programming, real-time analytics
Akamai: 4,100+ PoPs, enterprise focus, advanced security
Google Cloud CDN: Google network, low cost, Cloud Load Balancer integration
Azure CDN: Microsoft network, Azure integration, multiple providers
⚖️ CloudFront vs Other CDNs (Detailed)
Feature CloudFront Cloudflare Fastly Akamai
PoPs/Edge Locations 450+ 330+ 80+ 4,100+
AWS Integration Native (S3, ALB, Route 53) API/DNS integration API integration Custom integration
Edge Computing Lambda@Edge + Functions Workers (V8 isolates) Compute@Edge (Wasm) EdgeWorkers
DDoS Protection Shield Standard (free) Included (all plans) Additional cost Kona Site Defender
Free Tier 1TB/month (12 months) Unlimited (basic CDN) $50 credit None (enterprise only)
Cost (1TB/month) $85-$170 $0 (Free) or $200 $200+ Custom pricing
Best For AWS workloads Small-medium sites Real-time apps Enterprise (Fortune 500)
💡 When to Use CloudFront for anihpj: Use CloudFront when your job portal serves users globally and you need to reduce latency for static assets (CSS, JS, images), media files (job seeker resumes, company logos), or API responses. CloudFront is ideal when your Django app is hosted on AWS (EC2, ELB, or Elastic Beanstalk).
🗺️ CloudFront Implementation Roadmap
PHASE 1: Foundation (Week 1-2)
├─ Day 1-3: S3 Setup & Static Assets
│  ├─ Create S3 bucket (anihpj-static-assets)
│  ├─ Upload CSS, JS, images, fonts
│  ├─ Configure bucket policy for CloudFront
│  └─ Test: Direct S3 access works
│
├─ Day 4-7: CloudFront Distribution
│  ├─ Create distribution (origin: S3)
│  ├─ Configure cache behaviors (static/* = 1 year)
│  ├─ Add custom domain (cdn.anihpj.com)
│  ├─ Request ACM SSL certificate
│  └─ Test: CloudFront serves S3 content
│
└─ Day 8-14: Django Integration
   ├─ Install django-storages, boto3
   ├─ Configure settings.py (AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN)
   ├─ Update collectstatic command
   ├─ Deploy changes to production
   └─ Test: All static assets load from CloudFront

PHASE 2: Advanced Features (Week 3-4)
├─ Week 3: Security & Optimization
│  ├─ Enable Origin Access Identity (OAI)
│  ├─ Configure WAF rules (SQL injection, XSS)
│  ├─ Add custom error pages (404, 500)
│  ├─ Enable access logs → S3
│  └─ Set up CloudWatch alarms
│
└─ Week 4: Edge Computing
   ├─ Deploy CloudFront Function (URL rewriting)
   ├─ Optional: Lambda@Edge (A/B testing)
   ├─ Configure signed URLs (private content)
   └─ Enable real-time logs

PHASE 3: Production Optimization (Week 5-6)
├─ Week 5: Performance Tuning
│  ├─ Analyze cache hit ratio (target: >85%)
│  ├─ Optimize TTL settings per content type
│  ├─ Enable compression (Gzip/Brotli)
│  └─ Configure origin shield (cost optimization)
│
└─ Week 6: Monitoring & Alerts
   ├─ Set up CloudWatch dashboards
   ├─ Configure alarms (4xx/5xx error rates)
   ├─ Enable request-level logs
   └─ Document runbooks for incidents

🎯 SUCCESS METRICS:
├─ Performance: 50-70% latency reduction globally
├─ Cost: 30-40% bandwidth savings (origin traffic)
├─ Security: 0 DDoS incidents (Shield protection)
└─ Availability: 99.99% uptime (CloudFront SLA)
🏗️ CloudFront Architecture for anihpj Job Portal
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    CLOUDFRONT GLOBAL CDN ARCHITECTURE                    │
│                                                                          │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                    USER REQUESTS (Global)                         │  │
│  │  🌍 US User    🌏 Asia User    🌍 EU User    🌎 Brazil User       │  │
│  └───────┬──────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────┘  │
│          │              │              │              │                  │
│          ▼              ▼              ▼              ▼                  │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │           CloudFront Edge Locations (450+ PoPs)                  │   │
│  │  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐        │   │
│  │  │ us-east-1│  │ ap-south-1│  │ eu-west-1│  │ sa-east-1│        │   │
│  │  │   PoP    │  │   PoP     │  │   PoP    │  │   PoP    │        │   │
│  │  └────┬─────┘  └────┬──────┘  └────┬─────┘  └────┬─────┘        │   │
│  │       │ CACHE HIT    │ CACHE HIT    │ CACHE MISS  │ CACHE HIT    │   │
│  │       │ (instant)    │ (instant)    │ (→ origin)  │ (instant)    │   │
│  └───────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────┼──────────────┘   │
│          │              │              │             │                   │
│          │              │              ▼             │                   │
│          │              │         Origin Request     │                   │
│          │              │              │             │                   │
│  ┌───────┴──────────────┴──────────────┴─────────────┴──────────────┐  │
│  │                    CloudFront Distribution                        │  │
│  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │  │
│  │  │  Distribution ID: E1234ABCD5678                             │ │  │
│  │  │  Domain: d1234abcd.cloudfront.net                           │ │  │
│  │  │  Custom Domain: anihpj.com (Route 53 ALIAS)                 │ │  │
│  │  │  SSL Certificate: ACM certificate (*.anihpj.com)            │ │  │
│  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │  │
│  │                                                                    │  │
│  │  Cache Behaviors:                                                 │  │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│  │
│  │  │ Path: /static/*  → S3 Bucket (CSS, JS, images)              ││  │
│  │  │   TTL: 86400s (1 day), Compress: Yes                        ││  │
│  │  │ Path: /media/*   → S3 Bucket (user uploads, logos)          ││  │
│  │  │   TTL: 3600s (1 hour), Signed URLs: Yes                     ││  │
│  │  │ Path: /api/*     → ALB (Django REST API)                    ││  │
│  │  │   TTL: 0s (no cache), Forward headers: Authorization        ││  │
│  │  │ Path: /*         → ALB (Django dynamic pages)               ││  │
│  │  │   TTL: 300s (5 min), Forward cookies: sessionid, csrftoken ││  │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│  │
│  └────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                              │
│                           ▼                                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                        ORIGINS                                   │   │
│  │                                                                  │   │
│  │  Origin 1: S3 Bucket (anihpj-static)                           │   │
│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐                     │   │
│  │  │ Static Assets:                          │                     │   │
│  │  │  - /static/css/*.css                    │                     │   │
│  │  │  - /static/js/*.js                      │                     │   │
│  │  │  - /static/images/*.png                 │                     │   │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘                     │   │
│  │                                                                  │   │
│  │  Origin 2: S3 Bucket (anihpj-media)                            │   │
│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐                     │   │
│  │  │ User Uploads:                           │                     │   │
│  │  │  - /media/resumes/*.pdf                 │                     │   │
│  │  │  - /media/company-logos/*.jpg           │                     │   │
│  │  │  - /media/avatars/*.png                 │                     │   │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘                     │   │
│  │                                                                  │   │
│  │  Origin 3: Application Load Balancer (anihpj-alb)              │   │
│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │   │
│  │  │ ALB: anihpj-alb-1234567890.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com │     │   │
│  │  │  ▼                                    ▼                  │     │   │
│  │  │ ┌──────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐         │     │   │
│  │  │ │ EC2 Instance 1   │      │ EC2 Instance 2   │         │     │   │
│  │  │ │ Django App       │      │ Django App       │         │     │   │
│  │  │ │ (us-east-1a)     │      │ (us-east-1b)     │         │     │   │
│  │  │ └──────────────────┘      └──────────────────┘         │     │   │
│  │  │                                                          │     │   │
│  │  │ Dynamic Content:                                         │     │   │
│  │  │  - Job listings (/)                                      │     │   │
│  │  │  - Job detail (/jobs//)                             │     │   │
│  │  │  - API endpoints (/api/jobs/, /api/apply/)              │     │   │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │   │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                                                         │
│  Security & Monitoring:                                                 │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ AWS Shield Standard: ✅ DDoS protection (free)                  │   │
│  │ AWS WAF: ✅ SQL injection, XSS, rate limiting ($5/month)        │   │
│  │ SSL/TLS: ✅ ACM certificate (*.anihpj.com)                      │   │
│  │ CloudWatch: ✅ Real-time metrics (requests, errors, latency)    │   │
│  │ CloudWatch Logs: ✅ Access logs → S3 (anihpj-cloudfront-logs)  │   │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                         │
│  Performance Metrics:                                                   │
│  ├─ Cache Hit Ratio: 85-95% (target)                                   │
│  ├─ Origin Response Time: <100ms (ALB → Django)                        │
│  ├─ Edge Response Time: <10ms (cache hit)                              │
│  ├─ Global Latency: <50ms (99th percentile)                            │
│  └─ Data Transfer Out: 1TB/month = $85 (US/EU), $170 (Asia)           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🎓 Architecture Insights:
  • Multi-Origin Strategy: S3 for static assets (instant scaling), ALB for dynamic Django content
  • Cache Behaviors: Different TTL per path pattern (long TTL for static, short/no cache for dynamic)
  • Global Performance: 450+ PoPs cache content close to users (sub-10ms latency)
  • Cost Optimization: 85%+ cache hit ratio = 85% fewer origin requests = lower EC2/ALB costs
  • Security Layers: Shield (DDoS) → WAF (app-level) → SSL/TLS (encryption) → Signed URLs (access control)

🔧 2. Core Concepts ▼

Understanding CloudFront's key components and how they work together.

2.1 Distributions

A distribution is the primary configuration entity that tells CloudFront where to fetch content from and how to deliver it.

📡 Distribution Types
Type Use Case Protocols Best For
Web Distribution Static & dynamic content HTTP, HTTPS, WebSocket Websites, APIs, Django apps
RTMP Distribution Media streaming (deprecated) RTMP, RTMPE Legacy Flash video (use HLS instead)
Create Web Distribution for anihpj
# Create CloudFront distribution via AWS CLI
aws cloudfront create-distribution \
    --origin-domain-name anihpj-alb-123456.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com \
    --default-root-object index.html \
    --distribution-config '{
  "CallerReference": "anihpj-'$(date +%s)'",
  "Comment": "anihpj job portal CDN",
  "Enabled": true,
  "Origins": {
    "Quantity": 2,
    "Items": [
      {
        "Id": "S3-anihpj-static",
        "DomainName": "anihpj-static.s3.amazonaws.com",
        "S3OriginConfig": {
          "OriginAccessIdentity": "origin-access-identity/cloudfront/E1234ABCD5678"
        }
      },
      {
        "Id": "ALB-anihpj",
        "DomainName": "anihpj-alb-123456.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
        "CustomOriginConfig": {
          "HTTPPort": 80,
          "HTTPSPort": 443,
          "OriginProtocolPolicy": "https-only"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "DefaultCacheBehavior": {
    "TargetOriginId": "ALB-anihpj",
    "ViewerProtocolPolicy": "redirect-to-https",
    "AllowedMethods": {
      "Quantity": 7,
      "Items": ["GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "PUT", "POST", "PATCH", "DELETE"]
    },
    "CachedMethods": {
      "Quantity": 2,
      "Items": ["GET", "HEAD"]
    },
    "MinTTL": 0,
    "DefaultTTL": 86400,
    "MaxTTL": 31536000,
    "Compress": true
  },
  "CacheBehaviors": {
    "Quantity": 1,
    "Items": [
      {
        "PathPattern": "/static/*",
        "TargetOriginId": "S3-anihpj-static",
        "ViewerProtocolPolicy": "redirect-to-https",
        "MinTTL": 86400,
        "DefaultTTL": 2592000,
        "MaxTTL": 31536000,
        "Compress": true
      }
    ]
  }
}'

# Get distribution details
aws cloudfront get-distribution --id E1234ABCD5678

# Output shows:
# - Domain: d1a2b3c4d5e6f7.cloudfront.net
# - Status: InProgress → Deployed (15-20 minutes)
# - ARN: arn:aws:cloudfront::123456789012:distribution/E1234ABCD5678

2.2 Origins & Origin Groups

Origins are the source servers where CloudFront fetches content when it's not cached.

🎯 Origin Types
S3 Bucket: Static files, images, videos (OAI for security)
S3 Static Website: HTML site with redirects, error pages
ELB/ALB: Django app, dynamic content, API endpoints
EC2 Instance: Direct EC2 (public IP or Elastic IP)
Custom Origin: On-premises server, external CDN
MediaStore: Live/VOD video streaming
🔧 Origin Configuration Examples
# Boto3: Configure multiple origins for anihpj
import boto3

cloudfront = boto3.client('cloudfront')

distribution_config = {
    'Origins': {
        'Quantity': 3,
        'Items': [
            # Origin 1: S3 for static assets
            {
                'Id': 'S3-Static',
                'DomainName': 'anihpj-static.s3.amazonaws.com',
                'S3OriginConfig': {
                    'OriginAccessIdentity': 'origin-access-identity/cloudfront/E1234'
                },
                'ConnectionAttempts': 3,
                'ConnectionTimeout': 10,
                'OriginShield': {
                    'Enabled': True,
                    'OriginShieldRegion': 'us-east-1'  # Reduces origin load
                }
            },
            # Origin 2: S3 for media uploads
            {
                'Id': 'S3-Media',
                'DomainName': 'anihpj-media.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com',
                'S3OriginConfig': {
                    'OriginAccessIdentity': 'origin-access-identity/cloudfront/E5678'
                }
            },
            # Origin 3: ALB for Django app
            {
                'Id': 'ALB-Django',
                'DomainName': 'anihpj-alb-123.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
                'CustomOriginConfig': {
                    'HTTPPort': 80,
                    'HTTPSPort': 443,
                    'OriginProtocolPolicy': 'https-only',
                    'OriginSslProtocols': {
                        'Quantity': 1,
                        'Items': ['TLSv1.2']
                    },
                    'OriginReadTimeout': 30,
                    'OriginKeepaliveTimeout': 5
                },
                'CustomHeaders': {
                    'Quantity': 1,
                    'Items': [{
                        'HeaderName': 'X-Custom-Origin-Header',
                        'HeaderValue': 'CloudFront'  # Verify request is from CDN
                    }]
                }
            }
        ]
    }
}

print("✅ Multi-origin configuration ready for anihpj")
💡 Origin Groups (Failover): Create origin groups with primary and secondary origins. If primary fails (5xx errors), CloudFront automatically switches to secondary. Perfect for anihpj high availability: Primary ALB (us-east-1) → Secondary ALB (eu-west-1).

2.3 Cache Behaviors

Cache behaviors define how CloudFront handles requests based on path patterns.

💾 Cache Behavior Configuration
Path Pattern Origin TTL Cache Settings
/static/* S3-Static 1 day - 1 year Cache all, compress, no cookies
/media/* S3-Media 1 hour - 1 day Signed URLs, private content
/api/* ALB-Django 0 (no cache) Forward all headers, Auth header
/jobs/* ALB-Django 5 minutes Cache, forward query strings
/* (Default) ALB-Django 1 hour Forward sessionid, csrftoken

2.4 Edge Locations & Regional Edge Caches

CloudFront's two-tier caching architecture for optimal performance.

🌍 CloudFront Global Infrastructure
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              CLOUDFRONT TWO-TIER CACHING ARCHITECTURE            │
│                                                                  │
│  User Request (anihpj.com/jobs/123/)                            │
│         │                                                        │
│         ▼                                                        │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐                  │
│  │   TIER 1: Edge Location (PoP)            │                  │
│  │   450+ locations worldwide                │                  │
│  ├──────────────────────────────────────────┤                  │
│  │ 📍 North America: 100+                    │                  │
│  │    - United States: 70+ (major cities)    │                  │
│  │    - Canada: 5                            │                  │
│  │    - Mexico: 3                            │                  │
│  │                                           │                  │
│  │ 📍 Europe: 90+                            │                  │
│  │    - UK: 10, Germany: 12, France: 8      │                  │
│  │    - Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm         │                  │
│  │                                           │                  │
│  │ 📍 Asia Pacific: 110+                     │                  │
│  │    - India: 15, Japan: 18, Australia: 6  │                  │
│  │    - Singapore, Seoul, Hong Kong          │                  │
│  │                                           │                  │
│  │ 📍 South America: 15+                     │                  │
│  │    - Brazil: 6, Argentina: 2             │                  │
│  │                                           │                  │
│  │ 📍 Middle East & Africa: 20+              │                  │
│  │    - UAE: 2, South Africa: 2             │                  │
│  └────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘                  │
│               │ Cache Miss                                       │
│               ▼                                                  │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐                  │
│  │   TIER 2: Regional Edge Cache (REC)      │                  │
│  │   13 locations (higher capacity)          │                  │
│  ├──────────────────────────────────────────┤                  │
│  │ US East: Virginia, Ohio                   │                  │
│  │ US West: Oregon, N. California            │                  │
│  │ Europe: Ireland, Frankfurt, London        │                  │
│  │ Asia: Tokyo, Singapore, Mumbai            │                  │
│  │ South America: São Paulo                  │                  │
│  │ Australia: Sydney                         │                  │
│  │                                           │                  │
│  │ Features:                                 │                  │
│  │ ✓ Larger cache (terabytes)                │                  │
│  │ ✓ Stores infrequently accessed objects    │                  │
│  │ ✓ Reduces origin load by 50-70%          │                  │
│  └────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘                  │
│               │ Cache Miss                                       │
│               ▼                                                  │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐                  │
│  │   ORIGIN: ALB + Django (us-east-1)       │                  │
│  │   anihpj-alb-123.us-east-1.elb.aws.com  │                  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────┘                  │
│                                                                  │
│  Request Flow Example:                                          │
│  ─────────────────────────                                     │
│  1. User in Mumbai requests /jobs/123/                         │
│  2. DNS resolves to Mumbai Edge Location (ap-south-1)          │
│  3. Edge checks local cache → MISS                             │
│  4. Edge queries Mumbai REC → MISS                             │
│  5. REC fetches from us-east-1 origin → 150ms latency         │
│  6. REC caches object (subsequent requests: 5ms)               │
│  7. Edge caches object (subsequent requests: <1ms)             │
│  8. Response to user: 150ms (first) → <10ms (cached)          │
│                                                                  │
│  Cache Hit Metrics:                                             │
│  ├─ Edge Location hit: ~85% (instant response)                 │
│  ├─ Regional Edge hit: ~10% (5-10ms response)                  │
│  └─ Origin fetch: ~5% (100-300ms response)                     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
⚡ Performance Impact for anihpj:
  • Without CloudFront: Mumbai user → us-east-1 ALB = 200-300ms latency (every request)
  • With CloudFront: Mumbai user → local edge = <10ms (85% of requests), ~5% hit origin
  • Cost Savings: 95% cache hit = 95% fewer origin requests = lower ALB/EC2 costs
  • Scalability: Edge handles traffic spikes (job posting goes viral), origin stays protected

🚀 3. Getting Started ▼

Step-by-step guide to set up CloudFront for your anihpj Django application.

3.1 Prerequisites

📋 Pre-Launch Checklist
  • ✅ AWS Account - Active with billing enabled
  • ✅ Origin Resources - S3 bucket or ALB/EC2 running
  • ✅ Domain Name - anihpj.com registered (optional but recommended)
  • ✅ SSL Certificate - ACM certificate in us-east-1 for custom domain
  • ✅ IAM Permissions - cloudfront:*, s3:GetObject, acm:ListCertificates
  • ✅ Django Settings - STATIC_URL and MEDIA_URL configured

3.2 S3 Distribution Setup (Static Files)

Serve Django static assets (CSS, JS, images) from S3 via CloudFront.

Step 1: Create S3 Bucket & Upload Static Files
# Create S3 bucket for static files
aws s3 mb s3://anihpj-static --region us-east-1

# Collect Django static files
cd /path/to/anihpj
python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

# Upload to S3
aws s3 sync staticfiles/ s3://anihpj-static/static/ \
    --acl private \
    --cache-control "max-age=31536000, public" \
    --metadata-directive REPLACE

# Verify upload
aws s3 ls s3://anihpj-static/static/ --recursive | head -10
Step 2: Create Origin Access Identity (OAI)
# Create OAI (allows CloudFront to access private S3 bucket)
OAI_ID=$(aws cloudfront create-cloud-front-origin-access-identity \
    --cloud-front-origin-access-identity-config \
        CallerReference="anihpj-oai-$(date +%s)",\
        Comment="anihpj S3 static files access" \
    --query 'CloudFrontOriginAccessIdentity.Id' \
    --output text)

echo "OAI ID: $OAI_ID"

# Update S3 bucket policy to allow OAI
aws s3api put-bucket-policy --bucket anihpj-static --policy '{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Sid": "CloudFrontOAI",
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Principal": {
      "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::cloudfront:user/CloudFront Origin Access Identity '$OAI_ID'"
    },
    "Action": "s3:GetObject",
    "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-static/*"
  }]
}'

# Block public access (CloudFront-only access)
aws s3api put-public-access-block --bucket anihpj-static \
    --public-access-block-configuration \
        BlockPublicAcls=true,\
        IgnorePublicAcls=true,\
        BlockPublicPolicy=false,\
        RestrictPublicBuckets=false
Step 3: Create CloudFront Distribution
# Create distribution (simplified)
DIST_ID=$(aws cloudfront create-distribution \
    --distribution-config '{
  "CallerReference": "anihpj-s3-'$(date +%s)'",
  "Comment": "anihpj static files CDN",
  "Enabled": true,
  "Origins": {
    "Quantity": 1,
    "Items": [{
      "Id": "S3-anihpj-static",
      "DomainName": "anihpj-static.s3.amazonaws.com",
      "S3OriginConfig": {
        "OriginAccessIdentity": "origin-access-identity/cloudfront/'$OAI_ID'"
      }
    }]
  },
  "DefaultCacheBehavior": {
    "TargetOriginId": "S3-anihpj-static",
    "ViewerProtocolPolicy": "redirect-to-https",
    "AllowedMethods": {"Quantity": 2, "Items": ["GET", "HEAD"]},
    "CachedMethods": {"Quantity": 2, "Items": ["GET", "HEAD"]},
    "Compress": true,
    "MinTTL": 86400,
    "DefaultTTL": 2592000,
    "MaxTTL": 31536000
  },
  "PriceClass": "PriceClass_100",
  "ViewerCertificate": {
    "CloudFrontDefaultCertificate": true
  }
}' --query 'Distribution.Id' --output text)

echo "Distribution ID: $DIST_ID"

# Get CloudFront domain
DOMAIN=$(aws cloudfront get-distribution --id $DIST_ID \
    --query 'Distribution.DomainName' --output text)

echo "CloudFront Domain: $DOMAIN"
echo "Status: Deploying... (wait 15-20 minutes)"

# Wait for deployment
aws cloudfront wait distribution-deployed --id $DIST_ID
echo "✅ Distribution deployed!"

3.3 ALB/EC2 Distribution Setup (Dynamic Content)

Accelerate Django application responses through CloudFront.

Create Distribution with ALB Origin
# Get ALB DNS name
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers \
    --names anihpj-alb \
    --query 'LoadBalancers[0].DNSName' \
    --output text)

echo "ALB DNS: $ALB_DNS"

# Create CloudFront distribution with ALB origin
DIST_ID=$(aws cloudfront create-distribution \
    --distribution-config '{
  "CallerReference": "anihpj-alb-'$(date +%s)'",
  "Comment": "anihpj Django app CDN",
  "Enabled": true,
  "Origins": {
    "Quantity": 1,
    "Items": [{
      "Id": "ALB-anihpj",
      "DomainName": "'$ALB_DNS'",
      "CustomOriginConfig": {
        "HTTPPort": 80,
        "HTTPSPort": 443,
        "OriginProtocolPolicy": "https-only",
        "OriginSslProtocols": {"Quantity": 1, "Items": ["TLSv1.2"]},
        "OriginReadTimeout": 30,
        "OriginKeepaliveTimeout": 5
      },
      "CustomHeaders": {
        "Quantity": 1,
        "Items": [{
          "HeaderName": "X-Origin-Verify",
          "HeaderValue": "CloudFront-anihpj-'$(openssl rand -hex 16)'"
        }]
      }
    }]
  },
  "DefaultCacheBehavior": {
    "TargetOriginId": "ALB-anihpj",
    "ViewerProtocolPolicy": "redirect-to-https",
    "AllowedMethods": {
      "Quantity": 7,
      "Items": ["GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "PUT", "POST", "PATCH", "DELETE"]
    },
    "CachedMethods": {"Quantity": 2, "Items": ["GET", "HEAD"]},
    "Compress": true,
    "MinTTL": 0,
    "DefaultTTL": 300,
    "MaxTTL": 3600,
    "ForwardedValues": {
      "QueryString": true,
      "Cookies": {
        "Forward": "whitelist",
        "WhitelistedNames": {"Quantity": 2, "Items": ["sessionid", "csrftoken"]}
      },
      "Headers": {
        "Quantity": 3,
        "Items": ["Host", "CloudFront-Viewer-Country", "CloudFront-Is-Mobile-Viewer"]
      }
    }
  },
  "PriceClass": "PriceClass_All"
}' --query 'Distribution.Id' --output text)

echo "Distribution ID: $DIST_ID"
echo "⏳ Deploying... (15-20 minutes)"

3.4 Django Integration

Configure Django to serve static/media files via CloudFront.

anihpj/settings.py - CloudFront Configuration
# anihpj/settings.py

import os
from decouple import config

# CloudFront settings
CLOUDFRONT_DOMAIN = config('CLOUDFRONT_DOMAIN', default='d1234abcd.cloudfront.net')
USE_CLOUDFRONT = config('USE_CLOUDFRONT', default=True, cast=bool)

if USE_CLOUDFRONT:
    # Serve static files via CloudFront
    STATIC_URL = f'https://{CLOUDFRONT_DOMAIN}/static/'
    MEDIA_URL = f'https://{CLOUDFRONT_DOMAIN}/media/'
    
    # AWS S3 settings (django-storages)
    AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME = 'anihpj-static'
    AWS_S3_REGION_NAME = 'us-east-1'
    AWS_S3_CUSTOM_DOMAIN = CLOUDFRONT_DOMAIN
    AWS_S3_OBJECT_PARAMETERS = {
        'CacheControl': 'max-age=86400',  # 1 day
    }
    
    # Static files storage
    STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'storages.backends.s3boto3.S3Boto3Storage'
    
    # Media files storage (user uploads)
    DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = 'storages.backends.s3boto3.S3Boto3Storage'
    
    # CloudFront signed URLs for private media (optional)
    AWS_CLOUDFRONT_KEY_ID = config('AWS_CLOUDFRONT_KEY_ID', default='')
    AWS_CLOUDFRONT_KEY = config('AWS_CLOUDFRONT_KEY_PATH', default='')
    
else:
    # Local development
    STATIC_URL = '/static/'
    MEDIA_URL = '/media/'
    STATIC_ROOT = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'staticfiles')
    MEDIA_ROOT = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'media')

# Security headers for CloudFront
SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER = ('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO', 'https')
SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT = True

# Trusted origins (CloudFront custom domain)
CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS = [
    'https://anihpj.com',
    'https://www.anihpj.com',
    f'https://{CLOUDFRONT_DOMAIN}'
]
Install django-storages & boto3
# Install dependencies
pip install django-storages boto3

# Add to requirements.txt
echo "django-storages==1.14.2" >> requirements.txt
echo "boto3==1.34.10" >> requirements.txt

# Update settings
echo "INSTALLED_APPS += ['storages']" >> anihpj/settings.py

# Collect static files to S3 via CloudFront
python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

# Test in Django shell
python manage.py shell
>>> from django.templatetags.static import static
>>> print(static('css/style.css'))
'https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/static/css/style.css'

3.5 Testing & Validation

Verify CloudFront Setup
# 1. Test CloudFront domain
curl -I https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/static/css/style.css

# Check response headers:
# HTTP/2 200
# x-cache: Hit from cloudfront
# x-amz-cf-pop: IAD89-P3
# age: 3600

# 2. Test custom domain (if configured)
curl -I https://anihpj.com/static/css/style.css

# 3. Test cache behavior
for i in {1..5}; do
  curl -o /dev/null -s -w "Request $i: %{time_total}s\n" \
    https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/jobs/
done

# Output shows faster responses after first request (cache warming)

# 4. Check cache headers in Django view
# jobpost/views.py
from django.views.decorators.cache import cache_control

@cache_control(max_age=300, public=True)  # 5 minutes
def job_list(request):
    jobs = JobPost.objects.all()
    return render(request, 'jobs/list.html', {'jobs': jobs})

# 5. Verify CloudFront is working
python manage.py shell
>>> import requests
>>> response = requests.get('https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/')
>>> print(response.headers.get('X-Cache'))
'Hit from cloudfront'  # ✅ Cached response
>>> print(response.headers.get('X-Amz-Cf-Pop'))
'IAD89-P3'  # Edge location (Virginia)
✅ Post-Setup Checklist:
  • ✅ CloudFront domain resolves: d1234abcd.cloudfront.net
  • ✅ Static files load via CloudFront (check Network tab in browser)
  • ✅ Cache headers present: x-cache: Hit from cloudfront
  • ✅ HTTPS working (SSL certificate)
  • ✅ Django STATIC_URL points to CloudFront
  • ✅ Response time improved (check CloudWatch metrics)

🔑 4. Key Features ▼

Advanced CloudFront capabilities for enterprise content delivery.

🌟 CloudFront Key Features Overview
Lambda@Edge: Run code at edge locations, manipulate requests/responses
SSL/TLS: Free certificates via ACM, custom SSL, TLS 1.2/1.3
Signed URLs/Cookies: Private content access control, time-limited links
Compression: Automatic gzip/brotli, 70-90% size reduction
Geo Restriction: Whitelist/blacklist countries, compliance requirements
Origin Shield: Extra caching layer, reduces origin load by 50-70%

4.1 Edge Functions (Lambda@Edge & CloudFront Functions)

Run serverless code at CloudFront edge locations to customize content delivery.

⚡ Edge Functions Comparison
Feature CloudFront Functions Lambda@Edge
Runtime JavaScript (ECMAScript 5.1) Node.js, Python
Execution Time <1ms (sub-millisecond) 5-10 seconds
Triggers Viewer request, response Viewer + Origin request/response
Network Access No Yes (HTTP/S calls, DBs)
Max Size 10 KB 50 MB (packaged)
Cost $0.10 per 1M invocations $0.60 per 1M + duration
Use Case Simple transformations Complex logic, API calls
CloudFront Functions Example: Add Security Headers
// cloudfront-function-security-headers.js
function handler(event) {
    var response = event.response;
    var headers = response.headers;

    // Add security headers for anihpj
    headers['strict-transport-security'] = { 
        value: 'max-age=63072000; includeSubdomains; preload'
    };
    headers['x-content-type-options'] = { value: 'nosniff' };
    headers['x-frame-options'] = { value: 'DENY' };
    headers['x-xss-protection'] = { value: '1; mode=block' };
    headers['referrer-policy'] = { value: 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin' };
    
    // Content Security Policy
    headers['content-security-policy'] = { 
        value: "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://cdn.jsdelivr.net; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';"
    };

    return response;
}

4.2 SSL/TLS & HTTPS

Secure content delivery with free SSL certificates from AWS Certificate Manager.

Configure Custom Domain with ACM Certificate
# 1. Request ACM certificate in us-east-1 (MUST be us-east-1 for CloudFront)
aws acm request-certificate \
    --domain-name anihpj.com \
    --subject-alternative-names www.anihpj.com static.anihpj.com \
    --validation-method DNS \
    --region us-east-1

# 2. Get certificate ARN
CERT_ARN=$(aws acm list-certificates --region us-east-1 \
    --query "CertificateSummaryList[?DomainName=='anihpj.com'].CertificateArn" \
    --output text)

echo "Certificate ARN: $CERT_ARN"

# 3. Update CloudFront distribution with custom domain
aws cloudfront update-distribution \
    --id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --distribution-config '{
  "Aliases": {
    "Quantity": 2,
    "Items": ["anihpj.com", "www.anihpj.com"]
  },
  "ViewerCertificate": {
    "ACMCertificateArn": "'$CERT_ARN'",
    "SSLSupportMethod": "sni-only",
    "MinimumProtocolVersion": "TLSv1.2_2021",
    "Certificate": "'$CERT_ARN'",
    "CertificateSource": "acm"
  }
}'

# 4. Update Route 53 ALIAS record
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z1234567890ABC \
    --change-batch '{
  "Changes": [{
    "Action": "UPSERT",
    "ResourceRecordSet": {
      "Name": "anihpj.com",
      "Type": "A",
      "AliasTarget": {
        "HostedZoneId": "Z2FDTNDATAQYW2",
        "DNSName": "d1234abcd.cloudfront.net",
        "EvaluateTargetHealth": false
      }
    }
  }]
}'

# Now accessible at: https://anihpj.com (via CloudFront)

4.3 Signed URLs & Cookies (Private Content)

Control access to private content like job seeker resumes and company documents.

Generate Signed URL for Private Resume Download
# jobpost/utils/cloudfront_signer.py
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from cryptography.hazmat.backends import default_backend
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes, serialization
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import padding
from botocore.signers import CloudFrontSigner
import boto3

def rsa_signer(message):
    """RSA signer for CloudFront signed URLs"""
    with open('cloudfront-private-key.pem', 'rb') as key_file:
        private_key = serialization.load_pem_private_key(
            key_file.read(),
            password=None,
            backend=default_backend()
        )
    return private_key.sign(message, padding.PKCS1v15(), hashes.SHA1())

def generate_signed_url(cloudfront_domain, object_key, expire_minutes=60):
    """
    Generate signed URL for private S3 object via CloudFront
    
    Args:
        cloudfront_domain: d1234abcd.cloudfront.net
        object_key: media/resumes/john-doe-resume.pdf
        expire_minutes: URL validity in minutes
    
    Returns:
        Signed URL string
    """
    key_id = 'K1234567890ABC'  # CloudFront key pair ID
    url = f'https://{cloudfront_domain}/{object_key}'
    
    expire_date = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=expire_minutes)
    
    cloudfront_signer = CloudFrontSigner(key_id, rsa_signer)
    
    signed_url = cloudfront_signer.generate_presigned_url(
        url, 
        date_less_than=expire_date
    )
    
    return signed_url


# Usage in Django view
# jobpost/views.py
from django.contrib.auth.decorators import login_required
from django.http import HttpResponseRedirect
from .utils.cloudfront_signer import generate_signed_url

@login_required
def download_resume(request, application_id):
    """Generate signed URL for private resume download"""
    application = JobApplication.objects.get(id=application_id)
    
    # Verify user has permission to view resume
    if request.user != application.job.author and not request.user.is_staff:
        return HttpResponseForbidden("You don't have permission to view this resume")
    
    # Generate signed URL (valid for 10 minutes)
    resume_key = f'media/resumes/{application.resume_file}'
    signed_url = generate_signed_url(
        cloudfront_domain='d1234abcd.cloudfront.net',
        object_key=resume_key,
        expire_minutes=10
    )
    
    # Redirect to signed URL
    return HttpResponseRedirect(signed_url)


# Example signed URL:
# https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/media/resumes/john-doe.pdf?
#   Expires=1703001600&
#   Signature=ABC...XYZ&
#   Key-Pair-Id=K1234567890ABC

4.4 Automatic Compression

CloudFront automatically compresses text-based content to reduce transfer size.

📦 Compression Benefits for anihpj:
  • HTML: 70-80% size reduction (200 KB → 40 KB)
  • CSS: 75-85% reduction (100 KB → 15 KB)
  • JavaScript: 70-80% reduction (500 KB → 100 KB)
  • JSON API responses: 60-70% reduction
  • Compression formats: Gzip (default), Brotli (better compression)
  • Automatic: CloudFront detects Accept-Encoding header and compresses

4.5 Geographic Restrictions

Control content access based on user location for compliance or licensing.

Enable Geo-Blocking for Specific Countries
# Block access from specific countries (e.g., GDPR compliance before implementation)
aws cloudfront update-distribution \
    --id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --distribution-config '{
  "Restrictions": {
    "GeoRestriction": {
      "RestrictionType": "blacklist",
      "Quantity": 3,
      "Items": ["CN", "RU", "KP"]
    }
  }
}'

# Or whitelist only specific countries
aws cloudfront update-distribution \
    --id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --distribution-config '{
  "Restrictions": {
    "GeoRestriction": {
      "RestrictionType": "whitelist",
      "Quantity": 3,
      "Items": ["US", "CA", "GB"]
    }
  }
}'

# Users from blocked countries receive: HTTP 403 Forbidden
💡 Geo-Restriction Use Cases for anihpj:
  • Regional Job Portals: Block access from countries where you don't operate
  • Compliance: Restrict access until GDPR/local laws implemented
  • Licensing: Content licensing restrictions by geography
  • Cost Control: Avoid expensive data transfer to certain regions

💰 5. Pricing & Cost Optimization ▼

Transparent pricing based on data transfer and HTTP/S requests.

5.1 Pricing Model

💵 CloudFront Pricing Components
Component Cost Notes
Data Transfer Out (US/EU) $0.085/GB First 10 TB/month
Data Transfer Out (Asia) $0.140/GB Higher cost for Asia traffic
HTTP/S Requests $0.0075/10,000 GET, HEAD requests
POST/PUT Requests $0.010/10,000 Upload requests (forms, APIs)
Free Tier (12 months) FREE 1 TB out + 10M requests/month
Dedicated IP SSL $600/month Use SNI SSL instead (FREE)
Lambda@Edge $0.60/1M invocations + $0.00005001/GB-second
CloudFront Functions $0.10/1M invocations Much cheaper than Lambda@Edge
Invalidation Requests FREE (first 1,000/month) $0.005 per path after

5.2 Cost Examples

💵 Cost Scenarios for anihpj
Small: 100GB/month, 1M requests = $10-15/month
Medium: 500GB/month, 5M requests = $50-70/month
Large: 2TB/month, 20M requests = $180-250/month
Free Tier: 1TB out + 10M requests FREE (first year)
Origin Shield: $0.01/10,000 requests (optional)
Real-Time Logs: $0.01/1M log lines (optional)

5.3 Cost Optimization Strategies

💡 Cost Optimization Tips for anihpj
  1. Increase Cache Hit Ratio (85% → 95%)
    • Set longer TTL for static assets (1 day → 1 year)
    • Use versioned URLs: style.v2.css instead of style.css
    • Enable Origin Shield ($5/month → saves $20+ in origin costs)
  2. Use CloudFront Functions Instead of Lambda@Edge
    • Functions: $0.10/1M invocations
    • Lambda@Edge: $0.60/1M invocations (6x more expensive)
    • Use Functions for: headers, URL rewrites, redirects
    • Use Lambda@Edge for: API calls, database queries, complex logic
  3. Enable Compression (70% bandwidth savings)
    • CloudFront automatically compresses HTML, CSS, JS, JSON
    • 500 GB without compression = $42.50
    • 150 GB with compression = $12.75 (70% savings = $29.75)
  4. Choose Right Price Class
    • PriceClass_All: All edge locations (most expensive)
    • PriceClass_200: US, EU, Asia, South America (balanced)
    • PriceClass_100: US, EU only (cheapest, saves 30-40%)
    • For anihpj US/EU users: Use PriceClass_100 (saves $3-5/month per 100GB)
  5. Optimize Invalidations (FREE limit: 1,000/month)
    • Use wildcard invalidations: /static/* instead of 1,000 individual files
    • Use versioned URLs instead of invalidations (style.v2.css)
    • Schedule invalidations during low-traffic periods
  6. S3 Transfer Acceleration vs CloudFront
    • S3 direct: $0.09/GB (US), $0.15/GB (Asia)
    • CloudFront: $0.085/GB (US), $0.14/GB (Asia) + caching
    • CloudFront is cheaper AND faster (caching benefit)

5.4 Free Tier & New Customer Benefits

🎁 CloudFront Free Tier (First 12 Months):
  • Data Transfer Out: 1 TB/month to internet (all regions)
  • HTTP/S Requests: 10 million requests/month
  • Invalidations: 1,000 paths/month (always free)
  • Field-Level Encryption: First 10,000 requests/month
  • Perfect for anihpj: Covers ~100-200K monthly visitors for free
  • Expires: After 12 months, standard pricing applies
💰 Calculate Your CloudFront Costs
# Simple CloudFront cost calculator
def calculate_cloudfront_cost(
    data_transfer_gb_us_eu=0,
    data_transfer_gb_asia=0,
    get_requests=0,
    post_requests=0,
    use_functions=False,
    function_invocations=0,
    use_origin_shield=False
):
    """
    Calculate monthly CloudFront costs for anihpj
    
    Returns: dict with cost breakdown
    """
    # Pricing (per GB/request)
    PRICE_US_EU_PER_GB = 0.085  # First 10 TB
    PRICE_ASIA_PER_GB = 0.140
    PRICE_GET_PER_10K = 0.0075
    PRICE_POST_PER_10K = 0.010
    PRICE_FUNCTIONS_PER_1M = 0.10
    PRICE_ORIGIN_SHIELD_PER_10K = 0.01
    
    # Calculate costs
    cost_us_eu = data_transfer_gb_us_eu * PRICE_US_EU_PER_GB
    cost_asia = data_transfer_gb_asia * PRICE_ASIA_PER_GB
    cost_get = (get_requests / 10000) * PRICE_GET_PER_10K
    cost_post = (post_requests / 10000) * PRICE_POST_PER_10K
    
    cost_functions = 0
    if use_functions:
        cost_functions = (function_invocations / 1000000) * PRICE_FUNCTIONS_PER_1M
    
    cost_shield = 0
    if use_origin_shield:
        total_requests = get_requests + post_requests
        cost_shield = (total_requests / 10000) * PRICE_ORIGIN_SHIELD_PER_10K
    
    total_cost = sum([
        cost_us_eu, cost_asia, cost_get, cost_post, 
        cost_functions, cost_shield
    ])
    
    return {
        'data_transfer_us_eu': f'${cost_us_eu:.2f}',
        'data_transfer_asia': f'${cost_asia:.2f}',
        'get_requests': f'${cost_get:.2f}',
        'post_requests': f'${cost_post:.2f}',
        'functions': f'${cost_functions:.2f}' if use_functions else 'N/A',
        'origin_shield': f'${cost_shield:.2f}' if use_origin_shield else 'N/A',
        'total_monthly': f'${total_cost:.2f}',
        'total_annual': f'${total_cost * 12:.2f}'
    }


# Example: anihpj with 500GB traffic, 5M requests/month
costs = calculate_cloudfront_cost(
    data_transfer_gb_us_eu=350,
    data_transfer_gb_asia=150,
    get_requests=4_500_000,
    post_requests=500_000,
    use_functions=True,
    function_invocations=5_000_000,
    use_origin_shield=True
)

print("CloudFront Monthly Costs for anihpj:")
for key, value in costs.items():
    print(f"  {key}: {value}")

# Output:
# data_transfer_us_eu: $29.75
# data_transfer_asia: $21.00
# get_requests: $3.38
# post_requests: $0.50
# functions: $0.50
# origin_shield: $5.00
# total_monthly: $60.13
# total_annual: $721.56

📈 6. Performance & Scalability ▼

Optimize CloudFront for maximum performance and global scalability.

6.1 Cache Strategies

💾 Caching Strategies for anihpj
Content Type Strategy TTL Cache Key
Static Assets (CSS, JS) Aggressive caching + versioning 1 year (31536000s) URI only
Images/Media Long-term caching 30 days (2592000s) URI only
Job Listings (homepage) Short-term caching 5 minutes (300s) URI + query strings
API Responses Selective caching 30 seconds (30s) URI + query + headers
User Dashboard No caching 0 (pass-through) Forward all headers/cookies
Search Results Query-based caching 1 minute (60s) URI + all query params

6.2 TTL Configuration

Configure Cache Behavior TTL
# Boto3: Configure TTL for different content types
import boto3

cloudfront = boto3.client('cloudfront')

# Get current distribution config
response = cloudfront.get_distribution_config(Id='E1234ABCD5678')
config = response['DistributionConfig']
etag = response['ETag']

# Update cache behaviors with optimal TTLs
config['CacheBehaviors']['Items'] = [
    {
        'PathPattern': '/static/*',
        'TargetOriginId': 'S3-Static',
        'ViewerProtocolPolicy': 'redirect-to-https',
        'AllowedMethods': {'Quantity': 2, 'Items': ['GET', 'HEAD']},
        'Compress': True,
        'MinTTL': 86400,        # 1 day minimum
        'DefaultTTL': 2592000,  # 30 days default
        'MaxTTL': 31536000,     # 1 year maximum
        'ForwardedValues': {
            'QueryString': False,
            'Cookies': {'Forward': 'none'},
            'Headers': {'Quantity': 0}
        }
    },
    {
        'PathPattern': '/api/*',
        'TargetOriginId': 'ALB-Django',
        'ViewerProtocolPolicy': 'https-only',
        'AllowedMethods': {
            'Quantity': 7,
            'Items': ['GET', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS', 'PUT', 'POST', 'PATCH', 'DELETE']
        },
        'MinTTL': 0,      # No minimum (respect origin headers)
        'DefaultTTL': 0,  # No default caching
        'MaxTTL': 30,     # Max 30 seconds for GET requests
        'ForwardedValues': {
            'QueryString': True,
            'Cookies': {'Forward': 'all'},
            'Headers': {
                'Quantity': 4,
                'Items': ['Authorization', 'Accept', 'Content-Type', 'Origin']
            }
        }
    },
    {
        'PathPattern': '/jobs/*',
        'TargetOriginId': 'ALB-Django',
        'ViewerProtocolPolicy': 'redirect-to-https',
        'AllowedMethods': {'Quantity': 3, 'Items': ['GET', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS']},
        'Compress': True,
        'MinTTL': 0,
        'DefaultTTL': 300,   # 5 minutes for job listings
        'MaxTTL': 3600,      # 1 hour maximum
        'ForwardedValues': {
            'QueryString': True,  # Cache different query params separately
            'Cookies': {'Forward': 'none'},
            'Headers': {'Quantity': 1, 'Items': ['CloudFront-Viewer-Country']}
        }
    }
]

# Update distribution
cloudfront.update_distribution(
    Id='E1234ABCD5678',
    DistributionConfig=config,
    IfMatch=etag
)

print("✅ TTL configuration updated for anihpj")

6.3 Cache Invalidation

Clear cached content when updates are deployed.

Invalidate CloudFront Cache
# Method 1: Invalidate specific files
aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
    --distribution-id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --paths \
        /static/css/style.css \
        /static/js/app.js \
        /index.html

# Method 2: Invalidate entire directory (uses wildcard = counts as 1 path)
aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
    --distribution-id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --paths "/static/*" "/media/*"

# Method 3: Invalidate everything (emergency deployment)
aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
    --distribution-id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --paths "/*"

# Method 4: Python/Boto3 invalidation in Django deployment
import boto3
import time

def invalidate_cloudfront_cache(distribution_id, paths):
    """
    Invalidate CloudFront cache paths
    
    Args:
        distribution_id: CloudFront distribution ID
        paths: List of paths to invalidate
    
    Returns:
        Invalidation ID
    """
    cloudfront = boto3.client('cloudfront')
    
    invalidation = cloudfront.create_invalidation(
        DistributionId=distribution_id,
        InvalidationBatch={
            'Paths': {
                'Quantity': len(paths),
                'Items': paths
            },
            'CallerReference': f'anihpj-{int(time.time())}'
        }
    )
    
    invalidation_id = invalidation['Invalidation']['Id']
    print(f"✅ Invalidation created: {invalidation_id}")
    print(f"Status: {invalidation['Invalidation']['Status']}")
    
    return invalidation_id

# Usage after Django deployment
invalidation_id = invalidate_cloudfront_cache(
    distribution_id='E1234ABCD5678',
    paths=['/static/*', '/media/*', '/']
)

# Check invalidation status
cloudfront = boto3.client('cloudfront')
status = cloudfront.get_invalidation(
    DistributionId='E1234ABCD5678',
    Id=invalidation_id
)

print(f"Invalidation Status: {status['Invalidation']['Status']}")
# Status: InProgress → Completed (5-15 minutes)
💡 Invalidation Best Practices:
  • Free Tier: First 1,000 paths/month are FREE, then $0.005/path
  • Use Wildcards: /static/* counts as 1 path (invalidates all files in /static/)
  • Versioned URLs Better: style.v2.css instead of invalidating style.css
  • Timing: Invalidations take 5-15 minutes to complete globally
  • Automation: Add to CI/CD pipeline after successful deployment

6.4 Performance Metrics & Monitoring

Monitor CloudFront Performance with CloudWatch
# Get CloudFront metrics for anihpj
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
    --namespace AWS/CloudFront \
    --metric-name Requests \
    --dimensions Name=DistributionId,Value=E1234ABCD5678 \
    --start-time 2025-12-01T00:00:00Z \
    --end-time 2025-12-15T23:59:59Z \
    --period 86400 \
    --statistics Sum

# Key metrics to monitor:
# 1. Requests: Total number of requests
# 2. BytesDownloaded: Data transferred to viewers
# 3. BytesUploaded: Data uploaded from viewers (POST/PUT)
# 4. 4xxErrorRate: Client errors (400-499)
# 5. 5xxErrorRate: Server errors (500-599)
# 6. CacheHitRate: Percentage of requests served from cache
Python Script: Monitor Cache Hit Ratio
# monitor_cloudfront.py
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def get_cache_hit_ratio(distribution_id, days=7):
    """Get CloudFront cache hit ratio for last N days"""
    cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
    
    end_time = datetime.utcnow()
    start_time = end_time - timedelta(days=days)
    
    # Get cache hit rate metric
    response = cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
        Namespace='AWS/CloudFront',
        MetricName='CacheHitRate',
        Dimensions=[
            {'Name': 'DistributionId', 'Value': distribution_id}
        ],
        StartTime=start_time,
        EndTime=end_time,
        Period=86400,  # 1 day
        Statistics=['Average']
    )
    
    datapoints = sorted(response['Datapoints'], key=lambda x: x['Timestamp'])
    
    print(f"\n📊 Cache Hit Ratio for {distribution_id} (Last {days} days)")
    print("=" * 60)
    
    total_hit_rate = 0
    for point in datapoints:
        date = point['Timestamp'].strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
        hit_rate = point['Average']
        total_hit_rate += hit_rate
        
        # Visual bar
        bar = '█' * int(hit_rate / 2)
        print(f"{date}: {hit_rate:5.2f}% {bar}")
    
    avg_hit_rate = total_hit_rate / len(datapoints) if datapoints else 0
    print("=" * 60)
    print(f"Average Cache Hit Rate: {avg_hit_rate:.2f}%")
    
    # Performance assessment
    if avg_hit_rate >= 90:
        print("✅ EXCELLENT - Cache is highly optimized")
    elif avg_hit_rate >= 80:
        print("✅ GOOD - Cache performing well")
    elif avg_hit_rate >= 70:
        print("⚠️  FAIR - Consider increasing TTL or using Origin Shield")
    else:
        print("❌ POOR - Review cache behaviors and TTL settings")
    
    return avg_hit_rate

# Usage
hit_ratio = get_cache_hit_ratio('E1234ABCD5678', days=7)

# Output:
# 📊 Cache Hit Ratio for E1234ABCD5678 (Last 7 days)
# ============================================================
# 2025-12-09: 87.34% ███████████████████████████████████████████
# 2025-12-10: 89.12% ████████████████████████████████████████████
# 2025-12-11: 91.45% █████████████████████████████████████████████
# 2025-12-12: 88.76% ████████████████████████████████████████████
# 2025-12-13: 90.23% █████████████████████████████████████████████
# 2025-12-14: 92.11% ██████████████████████████████████████████████
# 2025-12-15: 89.87% ████████████████████████████████████████████
# ============================================================
# Average Cache Hit Rate: 89.84%
# ✅ GOOD - Cache performing well
🎯 Performance Targets for anihpj:
  • Cache Hit Ratio: Target 85-95% (higher is better)
  • Origin Response Time: <100ms (ALB → Django)
  • Edge Response Time: <10ms (cache hits)
  • 4xx Error Rate: <1% (client errors)
  • 5xx Error Rate: <0.1% (server errors)
  • Global P99 Latency: <50ms (99th percentile)

🚀 7. Advanced Topics ▼

Master advanced CloudFront features for enterprise-grade applications.

7.1 CloudFront Functions Deep Dive

Example 1: URL Rewrite for SEO-Friendly URLs
// CloudFront Function: Rewrite URLs for anihpj
// /jobs/python-developer-123 → /jobs/?slug=python-developer-123

function handler(event) {
    var request = event.request;
    var uri = request.uri;
    
    // Pattern: /jobs/[slug]
    var jobPattern = /^\/jobs\/([a-z0-9\-]+)$/;
    var match = uri.match(jobPattern);
    
    if (match) {
        // Rewrite to query string
        request.uri = '/jobs/';
        request.querystring = {
            slug: { value: match[1] }
        };
    }
    
    // Add default index.html for directory requests
    if (uri.endsWith('/')) {
        request.uri += 'index.html';
    }
    
    return request;
}
Example 2: A/B Testing at the Edge
// CloudFront Function: A/B test for anihpj homepage
function handler(event) {
    var request = event.request;
    var uri = request.uri;
    
    // Only A/B test homepage
    if (uri !== '/' && uri !== '/index.html') {
        return request;
    }
    
    // Check if user already has A/B cookie
    var cookies = request.cookies;
    var abVariant = cookies['ab_variant'] ? cookies['ab_variant'].value : null;
    
    if (!abVariant) {
        // Assign 50/50 split
        abVariant = Math.random() < 0.5 ? 'A' : 'B';
        
        // Set cookie (CloudFront Functions can't set cookies, do in origin)
        // We'll pass this as a header for origin to set
        request.headers['x-ab-variant'] = { value: abVariant };
    }
    
    // Route to different origin paths
    if (abVariant === 'B') {
        request.uri = '/index-variant-b.html';
    }
    
    return request;
}

7.2 Lambda@Edge Advanced Use Cases

Example 1: Dynamic Image Resizing
# Lambda@Edge (Origin Response): Resize images on-the-fly for anihpj
# Trigger: Origin Response
import json
import boto3
from PIL import Image
import io
import base64

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Resize images based on query parameters
    URL: /media/company-logo.jpg?w=300&h=200
    """
    request = event['Records'][0]['cf']['request']
    response = event['Records'][0]['cf']['response']
    
    # Only process images
    uri = request['uri']
    if not uri.endswith(('.jpg', '.jpeg', '.png', '.webp')):
        return response
    
    # Get query parameters
    querystring = request.get('querystring', '')
    params = dict(qc.split('=') for qc in querystring.split('&') if '=' in qc)
    
    width = int(params.get('w', 0))
    height = int(params.get('h', 0))
    
    if not width and not height:
        return response  # No resize needed
    
    # Get original image from response body
    if response['status'] != '200':
        return response
    
    body = response['body']
    if response.get('bodyEncoding') == 'base64':
        body = base64.b64decode(body)
    else:
        body = body.encode('utf-8')
    
    # Resize image
    img = Image.open(io.BytesIO(body))
    
    # Calculate dimensions maintaining aspect ratio
    if width and height:
        img = img.resize((width, height), Image.LANCZOS)
    elif width:
        ratio = width / img.width
        height = int(img.height * ratio)
        img = img.resize((width, height), Image.LANCZOS)
    elif height:
        ratio = height / img.height
        width = int(img.width * ratio)
        img = img.resize((width, height), Image.LANCZOS)
    
    # Convert back to bytes
    buffer = io.BytesIO()
    img_format = img.format or 'JPEG'
    img.save(buffer, format=img_format, quality=85, optimize=True)
    buffer.seek(0)
    
    # Update response
    response['body'] = base64.b64encode(buffer.read()).decode('utf-8')
    response['bodyEncoding'] = 'base64'
    response['headers']['content-length'] = [{'value': str(len(buffer.getvalue()))}]
    response['headers']['cache-control'] = [{'value': 'public, max-age=86400'}]
    
    return response
Example 2: JWT Authentication at the Edge
# Lambda@Edge (Viewer Request): Verify JWT tokens for anihpj API
# Trigger: Viewer Request
import json
import jwt
import os
from datetime import datetime

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Verify JWT token before forwarding to origin
    Protects /api/* endpoints
    """
    request = event['Records'][0]['cf']['request']
    uri = request['uri']
    
    # Only protect /api/* routes
    if not uri.startswith('/api/'):
        return request
    
    # Get Authorization header
    headers = request.get('headers', {})
    auth_header = headers.get('authorization', [{}])[0].get('value', '')
    
    if not auth_header.startswith('Bearer '):
        return generate_401_response('Missing or invalid Authorization header')
    
    token = auth_header.replace('Bearer ', '')
    
    # Verify JWT
    try:
        secret = os.environ['JWT_SECRET']  # Set in Lambda environment
        payload = jwt.decode(token, secret, algorithms=['HS256'])
        
        # Check expiration
        exp = payload.get('exp', 0)
        if datetime.utcnow().timestamp() > exp:
            return generate_401_response('Token expired')
        
        # Add user info to custom header for Django
        request['headers']['x-user-id'] = [{'value': str(payload.get('user_id'))}]
        request['headers']['x-user-email'] = [{'value': payload.get('email', '')}]
        
        return request
        
    except jwt.InvalidTokenError as e:
        return generate_401_response(f'Invalid token: {str(e)}')

def generate_401_response(message):
    """Generate 401 Unauthorized response"""
    return {
        'status': '401',
        'statusDescription': 'Unauthorized',
        'headers': {
            'content-type': [{'value': 'application/json'}],
            'cache-control': [{'value': 'no-store'}]
        },
        'body': json.dumps({'error': message})
    }

7.3 Field-Level Encryption

Encrypt sensitive data (credit cards, SSN, etc.) at the edge before reaching origin.

Setup Field-Level Encryption for anihpj
# Step 1: Generate public/private key pair
openssl genrsa -out private_key.pem 2048
openssl rsa -pubout -in private_key.pem -out public_key.pem

# Step 2: Upload public key to CloudFront
aws cloudfront create-public-key \
    --public-key-config \
        Name=anihpj-payment-encryption,\
        EncodedKey=$(cat public_key.pem | grep -v "PUBLIC KEY" | tr -d '\n'),\
        CallerReference=anihpj-$(date +%s),\
        Comment="Encrypt payment info for anihpj"

# Step 3: Create field-level encryption profile
aws cloudfront create-field-level-encryption-profile \
    --field-level-encryption-profile-config '{
        "Name": "anihpj-payment-profile",
        "CallerReference": "anihpj-'$(date +%s)'",
        "Comment": "Encrypt credit card and CVV",
        "EncryptionEntities": {
            "Quantity": 2,
            "Items": [
                {
                    "PublicKeyId": "K2ABCDEFG12345",
                    "ProviderId": "anihpj-payment",
                    "FieldPatterns": {
                        "Quantity": 2,
                        "Items": ["credit_card_number", "cvv"]
                    }
                }
            ]
        }
    }'

# Step 4: Create field-level encryption configuration
aws cloudfront create-field-level-encryption-config \
    --field-level-encryption-config '{
        "CallerReference": "anihpj-'$(date +%s)'",
        "Comment": "Encrypt payment forms",
        "QueryArgProfileConfig": {
            "ForwardWhenQueryArgProfileIsUnknown": false,
            "QueryArgProfiles": {
                "Quantity": 1,
                "Items": [{
                    "QueryArg": "payment",
                    "ProfileId": "P2BCDEFGH23456"
                }]
            }
        },
        "ContentTypeProfileConfig": {
            "ForwardWhenContentTypeIsUnknown": false,
            "ContentTypeProfiles": {
                "Quantity": 1,
                "Items": [{
                    "ContentType": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
                    "ProfileId": "P2BCDEFGH23456"
                }]
            }
        }
    }'
🔒 Field-Level Encryption Use Cases:
  • Payment Processing: Encrypt credit card numbers, CVV codes
  • Personal Data: SSN, passport numbers, medical records
  • Compliance: PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR requirements
  • Zero-Knowledge: CloudFront/ALB never see plaintext
  • Decryption: Only application with private key can decrypt

7.4 Real-Time Logs

Enable Real-Time Logs for anihpj
# Step 1: Create Kinesis Data Stream
aws kinesis create-stream \
    --stream-name anihpj-cloudfront-logs \
    --shard-count 2

# Step 2: Create real-time log configuration
aws cloudfront create-realtime-log-config \
    --name anihpj-realtime-logs \
    --end-points \
        StreamType=Kinesis,\
        StreamARN=arn:aws:kinesis:us-east-1:123456789012:stream/anihpj-cloudfront-logs,\
        KinesisStreamConfig={RoleARN=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CloudFrontRealtimeLogRole} \
    --fields \
        timestamp \
        c-ip \
        cs-method \
        cs-uri-stem \
        cs-uri-query \
        sc-status \
        sc-bytes \
        time-taken \
        cs-referer \
        cs-user-agent \
        x-edge-location \
        x-edge-request-id \
        x-host-header \
        cs-protocol \
        cs-bytes \
        time-to-first-byte \
