    --sampling-rate 100  # 100% of requests (adjust as needed)
Process Real-Time Logs with Lambda
# Lambda function: Process CloudFront real-time logs
import json
import base64
import boto3

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Process CloudFront real-time logs from Kinesis
    Analyze traffic patterns, detect anomalies
    """
    cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
    
    metrics = {
        'requests': 0,
        '4xx_errors': 0,
        '5xx_errors': 0,
        'slow_requests': 0,
        'total_bytes': 0
    }
    
    for record in event['Records']:
        # Decode Kinesis record
        payload = base64.b64decode(record['kinesis']['data']).decode('utf-8')
        log_entry = json.loads(payload)
        
        metrics['requests'] += 1
        
        # Count errors
        status = int(log_entry.get('sc-status', 0))
        if 400 <= status < 500:
            metrics['4xx_errors'] += 1
        elif status >= 500:
            metrics['5xx_errors'] += 1
        
        # Track slow requests (>1 second)
        time_taken = float(log_entry.get('time-taken', 0))
        if time_taken > 1.0:
            metrics['slow_requests'] += 1
        
        # Track bandwidth
        metrics['total_bytes'] += int(log_entry.get('sc-bytes', 0))
    
    # Send metrics to CloudWatch
    cloudwatch.put_metric_data(
        Namespace='anihpj/CloudFront',
        MetricData=[
            {
                'MetricName': '4xxErrorRate',
                'Value': (metrics['4xx_errors'] / metrics['requests']) * 100,
                'Unit': 'Percent'
            },
            {
                'MetricName': '5xxErrorRate',
                'Value': (metrics['5xx_errors'] / metrics['requests']) * 100,
                'Unit': 'Percent'
            },
            {
                'MetricName': 'SlowRequestRate',
                'Value': (metrics['slow_requests'] / metrics['requests']) * 100,
                'Unit': 'Percent'
            },
            {
                'MetricName': 'BytesDownloaded',
                'Value': metrics['total_bytes'],
                'Unit': 'Bytes'
            }
        ]
    )
    
    print(f"✅ Processed {metrics['requests']} log entries")
    return {'statusCode': 200}

7.5 Origin Shield Deep Dive

🛡️ Origin Shield Benefits:
  • Reduces Origin Load: 50-70% fewer requests to ALB/Django
  • Improves Cache Hit: Additional caching layer between edge and origin
  • Cost Savings: Less ALB data transfer, fewer Django computations
  • Better for Popular Content: High-traffic items cached longer
  • Cost: $0.010/10,000 requests (~$10/month for 10M requests)
Enable Origin Shield for anihpj
# Boto3: Enable Origin Shield
import boto3

cloudfront = boto3.client('cloudfront')

# Get current distribution
response = cloudfront.get_distribution_config(Id='E1234ABCD5678')
config = response['DistributionConfig']
etag = response['ETag']

# Enable Origin Shield for ALB origin
for origin in config['Origins']['Items']:
    if origin['Id'] == 'ALB-Django':
        origin['OriginShield'] = {
            'Enabled': True,
            'OriginShieldRegion': 'us-east-1'  # Same region as ALB
        }

# Update distribution
cloudfront.update_distribution(
    Id='E1234ABCD5678',
    DistributionConfig=config,
    IfMatch=etag
)

print("✅ Origin Shield enabled for anihpj ALB origin")

✅ 8. Best Practices ▼

Production-ready best practices for secure, performant CloudFront distributions.

8.1 Security Best Practices

🔒 Security Checklist for anihpj:
  • ✅ HTTPS Only: Redirect HTTP to HTTPS (ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https)
  • ✅ TLS 1.2+: Disable TLS 1.0/1.1 (SecurityPolicyProtocol: TLSv1.2_2021)
  • ✅ OAI for S3: Block direct S3 access, use Origin Access Identity
  • ✅ Custom Headers: Add X-Origin-Verify header to validate requests from CloudFront
  • ✅ WAF Integration: Attach AWS WAF for SQL injection, XSS protection
  • ✅ Geo Restriction: Block countries if needed for compliance
  • ✅ Signed URLs: Protect premium content (resumes, documents)
  • ✅ Security Headers: Add HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options via CloudFront Functions
  • ✅ Shield Standard: FREE DDoS protection (automatically enabled)
  • ✅ Access Logs: Enable logging for security audits
Django: Verify Requests from CloudFront
# anihpj/middleware.py
from django.http import HttpResponseForbidden
from django.conf import settings

class CloudFrontVerificationMiddleware:
    """
    Verify requests come from CloudFront, not direct to ALB
    """
    def __init__(self, get_response):
        self.get_response = get_response
    
    def __call__(self, request):
        # Allow health checks
        if request.path == '/health/':
            return self.get_response(request)
        
        # Verify CloudFront custom header
        origin_verify = request.META.get('HTTP_X_ORIGIN_VERIFY', '')
        expected_value = settings.CLOUDFRONT_ORIGIN_VERIFY_SECRET
        
        if origin_verify != expected_value:
            return HttpResponseForbidden("Direct access not allowed")
        
        return self.get_response(request)

# settings.py
MIDDLEWARE = [
    'django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware',
    'anihpj.middleware.CloudFrontVerificationMiddleware',  # Add this
    # ... other middleware
]

CLOUDFRONT_ORIGIN_VERIFY_SECRET = 'anihpj-secure-random-string-12345'

8.2 Performance Tuning Best Practices

⚡ Cache Optimization
  • Increase TTL for static assets (1 year)
  • Use versioned URLs (style.v2.css)
  • Target 90%+ cache hit ratio
  • Enable Origin Shield ($10/month saves $40+)
📦 Compression
  • Enable automatic compression
  • Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG
  • Save 70-85% bandwidth
  • Faster page loads globally
🌍 Edge Locations
  • Use all edge locations (PriceClass_All)
  • Or limit to US/EU (PriceClass_100)
  • 450+ global PoPs
  • <10ms response for 85% requests
🎯 Origin Optimization
  • Keep-alive connections (300s)
  • Connection timeout: 10s
  • Response timeout: 30s
  • Minimize origin round-trips
📊 Monitoring
  • CloudWatch cache hit ratio
  • 4xx/5xx error rates
  • Origin latency metrics
  • Real-time logs for debugging
🔄 Invalidation Strategy
  • Use versioned URLs (preferred)
  • Wildcard invalidations (1 path cost)
  • Schedule during low traffic
  • First 1,000 paths FREE/month

8.3 Monitoring & Alerting

CloudWatch Alarms for anihpj
# Alarm 1: High 4xx error rate
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name anihpj-cloudfront-4xx-errors \
    --alarm-description "Alert when 4xx errors exceed 5%" \
    --metric-name 4xxErrorRate \
    --namespace AWS/CloudFront \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 300 \
    --evaluation-periods 2 \
    --threshold 5 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --dimensions Name=DistributionId,Value=E1234ABCD5678 \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts

# Alarm 2: High 5xx error rate
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name anihpj-cloudfront-5xx-errors \
    --alarm-description "Alert when 5xx errors exceed 1%" \
    --metric-name 5xxErrorRate \
    --namespace AWS/CloudFront \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 300 \
    --evaluation-periods 2 \
    --threshold 1 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --dimensions Name=DistributionId,Value=E1234ABCD5678 \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts

# Alarm 3: Low cache hit ratio
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name anihpj-cloudfront-low-cache-hit \
    --alarm-description "Alert when cache hit ratio below 80%" \
    --metric-name CacheHitRate \
    --namespace AWS/CloudFront \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 3600 \
    --evaluation-periods 2 \
    --threshold 80 \
    --comparison-operator LessThanThreshold \
    --dimensions Name=DistributionId,Value=E1234ABCD5678 \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts

8.4 Disaster Recovery & High Availability

Multi-Region Failover with Origin Groups
# Boto3: Configure origin failover for anihpj
import boto3

cloudfront = boto3.client('cloudfront')

# Get distribution config
response = cloudfront.get_distribution_config(Id='E1234ABCD5678')
config = response['DistributionConfig']
etag = response['ETag']

# Add secondary origin (eu-west-1)
config['Origins']['Quantity'] = 3
config['Origins']['Items'].append({
    'Id': 'ALB-Django-Secondary',
    'DomainName': 'anihpj-secondary.eu-west-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
    'CustomOriginConfig': {
        'HTTPPort': 80,
        'HTTPSPort': 443,
        'OriginProtocolPolicy': 'https-only',
        'OriginSslProtocols': {
            'Quantity': 1,
            'Items': ['TLSv1.2']
        }
    },
    'ConnectionAttempts': 3,
    'ConnectionTimeout': 10
})

# Create origin group for failover
config['OriginGroups'] = {
    'Quantity': 1,
    'Items': [{
        'Id': 'ALB-Django-OriginGroup',
        'FailoverCriteria': {
            'StatusCodes': {
                'Quantity': 4,
                'Items': [500, 502, 503, 504]  # Failover on these status codes
            }
        },
        'Members': {
            'Quantity': 2,
            'Items': [
                {'OriginId': 'ALB-Django'},          # Primary (us-east-1)
                {'OriginId': 'ALB-Django-Secondary'}  # Secondary (eu-west-1)
            ]
        }
    }]
}

# Update cache behaviors to use origin group
for behavior in config['CacheBehaviors']['Items']:
    if behavior['TargetOriginId'] == 'ALB-Django':
        behavior['TargetOriginId'] = 'ALB-Django-OriginGroup'

config['DefaultCacheBehavior']['TargetOriginId'] = 'ALB-Django-OriginGroup'

# Update distribution
cloudfront.update_distribution(
    Id='E1234ABCD5678',
    DistributionConfig=config,
    IfMatch=etag
)

print("✅ Multi-region failover configured")
print("Primary: us-east-1 | Secondary: eu-west-1")
🔄 DR Best Practices:
  • Origin Groups: Automatic failover to secondary region
  • Health Checks: Monitor origin health every 30 seconds
  • Multi-Region: Deploy to us-east-1 (primary) + eu-west-1 (secondary)
  • Database Replication: RDS read replicas in secondary region
  • S3 Replication: Cross-region replication for static assets
  • Failover Time: Typically <1 minute automatic failover

💼 9. Use Cases ▼

Real-world CloudFront implementation scenarios for anihpj.

9.1 Static Website Hosting

Complete Static Website Stack (S3 + CloudFront + Route 53)
# Step 1: Create S3 bucket for static website
aws s3api create-bucket \
    --bucket anihpj-static-site \
    --region us-east-1

aws s3 website s3://anihpj-static-site/ \
    --index-document index.html \
    --error-document 404.html

# Step 2: Upload static files
aws s3 sync ./static/ s3://anihpj-static-site/ \
    --cache-control "max-age=31536000, public" \
    --exclude "*.html" \
    --exclude "*.json"

# HTML files with shorter cache
aws s3 sync ./static/ s3://anihpj-static-site/ \
    --cache-control "max-age=3600, public" \
    --exclude "*" \
    --include "*.html"

# Step 3: Create OAI
OAI_ID=$(aws cloudfront create-cloud-front-origin-access-identity \
    --cloud-front-origin-access-identity-config \
        CallerReference=anihpj-$(date +%s),\
        Comment="OAI for anihpj static site" \
    --query 'CloudFrontOriginAccessIdentity.Id' \
    --output text)

# Step 4: Update S3 bucket policy
aws s3api put-bucket-policy \
    --bucket anihpj-static-site \
    --policy '{
        "Version": "2012-10-17",
        "Statement": [{
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Principal": {
                "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::cloudfront:user/CloudFront Origin Access Identity '$OAI_ID'"
            },
            "Action": "s3:GetObject",
            "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-static-site/*"
        }]
    }'

# Step 5: Create CloudFront distribution
DISTRIBUTION_ID=$(aws cloudfront create-distribution \
    --distribution-config '{
        "CallerReference": "anihpj-static-'$(date +%s)'",
        "Comment": "anihpj static website",
        "Enabled": true,
        "Origins": {
            "Quantity": 1,
            "Items": [{
                "Id": "S3-Static-Site",
                "DomainName": "anihpj-static-site.s3.amazonaws.com",
                "S3OriginConfig": {
                    "OriginAccessIdentity": "origin-access-identity/cloudfront/'$OAI_ID'"
                }
            }]
        },
        "DefaultCacheBehavior": {
            "TargetOriginId": "S3-Static-Site",
            "ViewerProtocolPolicy": "redirect-to-https",
            "AllowedMethods": {
                "Quantity": 2,
                "Items": ["GET", "HEAD"]
            },
            "Compress": true,
            "MinTTL": 0,
            "DefaultTTL": 86400,
            "MaxTTL": 31536000,
            "ForwardedValues": {
                "QueryString": false,
                "Cookies": {"Forward": "none"}
            }
        },
        "CustomErrorResponses": {
            "Quantity": 1,
            "Items": [{
                "ErrorCode": 404,
                "ResponsePagePath": "/404.html",
                "ResponseCode": "404",
                "ErrorCachingMinTTL": 300
            }]
        },
        "Aliases": {
            "Quantity": 1,
            "Items": ["www.anihpj.com"]
        },
        "ViewerCertificate": {
            "ACMCertificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123",
            "SSLSupportMethod": "sni-only",
            "MinimumProtocolVersion": "TLSv1.2_2021"
        }
    }' --query 'Distribution.Id' --output text)

# Step 6: Create Route 53 record
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z3ABCDEFG12345 \
    --change-batch '{
        "Changes": [{
            "Action": "CREATE",
            "ResourceRecordSet": {
                "Name": "www.anihpj.com",
                "Type": "A",
                "AliasTarget": {
                    "HostedZoneId": "Z2FDTNDATAQYW2",
                    "DNSName": "d1234abcd.cloudfront.net",
                    "EvaluateTargetHealth": false
                }
            }
        }]
    }'

echo "✅ Static website deployed: https://www.anihpj.com"

9.2 Video Streaming & Media Delivery

HLS Video Streaming for anihpj Company Videos
# Generate HLS manifest and serve via CloudFront
from django.http import HttpResponse
from botocore.signers import CloudFrontSigner
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import serialization
from cryptography.hazmat.backends import default_backend
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import rsa

def rsa_signer(message):
    with open('cloudfront_private_key.pem', 'rb') as key_file:
        private_key = serialization.load_pem_private_key(
            key_file.read(),
            password=None,
            backend=default_backend()
        )
    return private_key.sign(message, padding.PKCS1v15(), hashes.SHA1())

def generate_signed_video_url(video_key, expire_minutes=60):
    """Generate signed URL for HLS video streaming"""
    cloudfront_domain = 'd1234abcd.cloudfront.net'
    key_id = 'K2ABCDEFG12345'
    
    url = f'https://{cloudfront_domain}/{video_key}'
    
    expire_date = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=expire_minutes)
    
    cloudfront_signer = CloudFrontSigner(key_id, rsa_signer)
    signed_url = cloudfront_signer.generate_presigned_url(
        url,
        date_less_than=expire_date
    )
    
    return signed_url

# Django view: Serve company introduction video
def company_video(request, company_id):
    company = get_object_or_404(Company, id=company_id)
    
    # HLS manifest path
    video_key = f'videos/companies/{company_id}/master.m3u8'
    
    # Generate signed URL (60-minute expiry)
    signed_url = generate_signed_video_url(video_key, expire_minutes=60)
    
    return render(request, 'video_player.html', {
        'company': company,
        'video_url': signed_url,
        'poster': f'https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/videos/companies/{company_id}/poster.jpg'
    })

# video_player.html template
"""




"""

9.3 API Acceleration

Cache API Responses for anihpj Job Search
# Django views.py: API with CloudFront caching
from django.http import JsonResponse
from django.views.decorators.cache import cache_control
from django.views.decorators.vary import vary_on_headers

@cache_control(public=True, max_age=60, s_maxage=300)
@vary_on_headers('Accept-Language', 'CloudFront-Viewer-Country')
def api_job_search(request):
    """
    API endpoint: /api/jobs/search/
    CloudFront caches for 5 minutes (s-maxage=300)
    Browser caches for 1 minute (max-age=60)
    """
    query = request.GET.get('q', '')
    location = request.GET.get('location', '')
    category = request.GET.get('category', '')
    
    # Get user's country from CloudFront header
    viewer_country = request.META.get('HTTP_CLOUDFRONT_VIEWER_COUNTRY', 'US')
    
    jobs = Job.objects.filter(
        title__icontains=query,
        location__icontains=location
    )
    
    if category:
        jobs = jobs.filter(category=category)
    
    # Localize results based on viewer country
    jobs = jobs.filter(countries__contains=[viewer_country])[:20]
    
    results = [{
        'id': job.id,
        'title': job.title,
        'company': job.company.name,
        'location': job.location,
        'salary': job.salary,
        'posted': job.created_at.isoformat()
    } for job in jobs]
    
    response = JsonResponse({
        'query': query,
        'location': location,
        'country': viewer_country,
        'count': len(results),
        'results': results
    })
    
    # Add cache headers for CloudFront
    response['Cache-Control'] = 'public, max-age=60, s-maxage=300'
    response['Vary'] = 'Accept-Language, CloudFront-Viewer-Country'
    
    return response

# Cache behavior for API (in CloudFront)
"""
Path Pattern: /api/jobs/search/*
Cache Key: URI + Query Strings (q, location, category)
Headers: CloudFront-Viewer-Country, Accept-Language
TTL: 5 minutes
Result: 90% cache hit ratio for popular searches
"""

9.4 Dynamic Content Acceleration

⚡ Accelerate anihpj Dynamic Pages:
  • Job Listings Page: Cache 5 minutes, vary by query params
  • Company Profiles: Cache 1 hour, invalidate on updates
  • Search Results: Cache 1 minute per query, 10,000+ unique caches
  • User Dashboard: No caching (TTL=0), but faster with CloudFront's optimized network
  • Static Assets: Cache 1 year, versioned URLs (style.v2.css)
Django Middleware: Smart Caching for Dynamic Content
# anihpj/middleware.py
class SmartCloudFrontCacheMiddleware:
    """
    Automatically set Cache-Control headers based on content type
    """
    def __init__(self, get_response):
        self.get_response = get_response
    
    def __call__(self, request):
        response = self.get_response(request)
        
        # Don't cache authenticated requests
        if request.user.is_authenticated:
            response['Cache-Control'] = 'private, no-cache, no-store'
            return response
        
        # Smart caching based on path
        path = request.path
        
        if path.startswith('/jobs/'):
            # Job listings: 5 minutes
            response['Cache-Control'] = 'public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300'
            response['Vary'] = 'Cookie'
        
        elif path.startswith('/company/'):
            # Company profiles: 1 hour
            response['Cache-Control'] = 'public, max-age=3600, s-maxage=3600'
        
        elif path.startswith('/search/'):
            # Search results: 1 minute
            response['Cache-Control'] = 'public, max-age=60, s-maxage=60'
            response['Vary'] = 'Cookie, Accept-Language'
        
        elif path == '/':
            # Homepage: 5 minutes
            response['Cache-Control'] = 'public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300'
        
        else:
            # Default: no caching for dynamic pages
            response['Cache-Control'] = 'private, no-cache'
        
        return response

# settings.py
MIDDLEWARE = [
    # ... other middleware
    'anihpj.middleware.SmartCloudFrontCacheMiddleware',
]

🔧 10. Troubleshooting ▼

Debug and resolve common CloudFront issues for anihpj.

🔍 Troubleshooting Flowchart
START: CloudFront Issue Detected
│
├─ ISSUE: 403 Forbidden Error
│  ├─ Origin Type?
│  │  ├─ S3 Bucket
│  │  │  └─ Check OAI configuration
│  │  │     ├─ OAI not configured? → FIX: Create OAI, update bucket policy
│  │  │     └─ OAI exists? → Verify bucket policy includes OAI principal
│  │  │        └─ aws s3api get-bucket-policy --bucket anihpj-assets
│  │  │
│  │  └─ ALB/EC2 Origin
│  │     └─ Check security group
│  │        └─ FIX: Allow HTTPS from CloudFront prefix list
│  │           └─ Source: com.amazonaws.global.cloudfront.origin-facing
│  │
├─ ISSUE: 504 Gateway Timeout
│  ├─ Check origin response time
│  │  └─ curl -w "@curl-format.txt" https://origin.anihpj.com
│  │     ├─ >30s? → Origin too slow
│  │     │  └─ FIX: Increase CloudFront timeout to 60s
│  │     │     └─ Optimize Django (DB queries, caching)
│  │     │
│  │     └─ <5s? → CloudFront issue
│  │        └─ Check CloudWatch metrics (OriginLatency)
│  │           └─ Contact AWS Support
│  │
├─ ISSUE: Stale Content (Not Updating)
│  ├─ Content Type?
│  │  ├─ Static Assets (CSS/JS/Images)
│  │  │  └─ BEST FIX: Use versioned URLs
│  │  │     ├─ style.css → style.v2.css (no invalidation needed)
│  │  │     └─ OR: style.css?v=20240115
│  │  │        └─ FREE & INSTANT (new URL = cache miss)
│  │  │
│  │  └─ Dynamic Content (API/HTML)
│  │     └─ FIX: Create invalidation
│  │        └─ aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
│  │           --distribution-id E1234ABCD \
│  │           --paths "/api/*" "/index.html"
│  │           └─ Wait 5-15 minutes for propagation
│  │
├─ ISSUE: Low Cache Hit Ratio (<70%)
│  ├─ Check CloudWatch: CacheHitRate metric
│  │  └─ Identify patterns: Which paths have low hits?
│  │     └─ FIX OPTIONS:
│  │        ├─ 1. Increase TTL (if content rarely changes)
│  │        ├─ 2. Normalize query strings (case, order)
│  │        ├─ 3. Reduce forwarded headers (only forward needed ones)
│  │        ├─ 4. Enable Origin Shield (centralizes cache)
│  │        └─ 5. Use cache policies (not legacy settings)
│  │
├─ ISSUE: High Costs (Unexpected Bill)
│  ├─ Check cost drivers in CloudWatch
│  │  ├─ Data Transfer High?
│  │  │  └─ Enable compression (Gzip/Brotli)
│  │  │     └─ FIX: Reduces transfer by 70% for text
│  │  │        └─ Distribution Settings → Compress Objects Automatically
│  │  │
│  │  ├─ Too Many Invalidations?
│  │  │  └─ FIX: Use versioned URLs (FREE)
│  │  │     └─ vs $0.005/path for invalidations
│  │  │
│  │  └─ Using All Edge Locations?
│  │     └─ FIX: Use Price Class 100 (US/EU only)
│  │        └─ Saves 30-40% if you don't need global coverage
│  │
├─ ISSUE: SSL/TLS Certificate Errors
│  ├─ Certificate region? → MUST be us-east-1
│  │  └─ Wrong region? → Request new cert in us-east-1
│  │     └─ aws acm request-certificate --region us-east-1
│  │
│  ├─ Domain validation? → Check DNS
│  │  └─ Add CNAME records for ACM validation
│  │
│  └─ SNI vs Dedicated IP?
│     └─ Use SNI (FREE) unless you need IE on Windows XP
│        └─ Dedicated IP = $600/month (not worth it)
│
├─ ISSUE: CORS Errors in Browser
│  └─ Console shows: "No Access-Control-Allow-Origin header"
│     └─ FIX: Forward CORS headers from origin
│        ├─ CloudFront: Forward Access-Control-* headers
│        ├─ Cache based on: Origin header
│        └─ Django: Install django-cors-headers
│           └─ CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS = ['https://cdn.anihpj.com']
│
└─ ISSUE: 502 Bad Gateway
   ├─ Origin down?
   │  └─ Test: curl https://origin.anihpj.com/health
   │     ├─ Not responding? → Check ALB/EC2 health
   │     └─ SSL error? → Verify certificate chain
   │
   └─ CloudFront can't reach origin?
      └─ Check security group allows CloudFront IPs
         └─ Use managed prefix list: com.amazonaws.global.cloudfront.origin-facing

🔧 DIAGNOSTIC COMMANDS:
# Check distribution status
aws cloudfront get-distribution --id E1234ABCD --query 'Distribution.Status'

# View cache statistics
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
  --namespace AWS/CloudFront \
  --metric-name CacheHitRate \
  --dimensions Name=DistributionId,Value=E1234ABCD \
  --start-time 2024-01-15T00:00:00Z \
  --end-time 2024-01-15T23:59:59Z \
  --period 3600 \
  --statistics Average

# Test edge location serving you
curl -I https://d1234.cloudfront.net/test.jpg | grep -i "x-cache"
# Hit from cloudfront = cached
# Miss from cloudfront = fetched from origin

# Check origin latency
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
  --namespace AWS/CloudFront \
  --metric-name OriginLatency \
  --dimensions Name=DistributionId,Value=E1234ABCD \
  --start-time 2024-01-15T00:00:00Z \
  --end-time 2024-01-15T23:59:59Z \
  --period 300 \
  --statistics Average,Maximum

10.1 Common Issues & Solutions

⚠️ Common CloudFront Problems
Issue Symptoms Solution
403 Forbidden S3 objects return 403 • Check OAI configuration
• Verify S3 bucket policy
• Ensure objects are not encrypted with wrong KMS key
504 Gateway Timeout Slow origin responses • Increase origin response timeout (default 30s)
• Optimize Django queries
• Add database indices
Low Cache Hit Ratio <60% cache hits • Increase TTL values
• Reduce unique query strings
• Enable Origin Shield
• Use versioned URLs instead of invalidations
SSL Certificate Error HTTPS not working • Certificate MUST be in us-east-1
• Add domain to certificate SANs
• Wait for certificate validation
• Use SNI (not dedicated IP)
Stale Content Old cached versions • Create invalidation
• Use versioned URLs (better)
• Check Cache-Control headers
• Wait 5-15 min for invalidation
CORS Errors Browser blocks requests • Forward Access-Control headers
• Add OPTIONS to allowed methods
• Configure CORS in Django
• Whitelist CloudFront headers
High Costs Unexpected bills • Enable compression (70% savings)
• Use PriceClass_100 (US/EU only)
• Increase cache hit ratio
• Review access logs for abuse

10.2 Debugging Tools & Techniques

Debug with curl and Response Headers
# Test CloudFront cache behavior
curl -I https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/static/css/style.css

# Key headers to check:
# HTTP/2 200
# x-cache: Hit from cloudfront               ← Cache hit!
# x-amz-cf-pop: IAD89-P3                      ← Edge location (Virginia)
# x-amz-cf-id: abc123...                      ← Request ID for support
# age: 3456                                   ← Cached for 3456 seconds
# cache-control: public, max-age=31536000     ← TTL from origin
# vary: Accept-Encoding                       ← Vary on encoding

# Test cache miss
curl -I -H "Cache-Control: no-cache" https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/static/css/style.css
# x-cache: Miss from cloudfront               ← Cache miss!
# x-cache: RefreshHit from cloudfront         ← Revalidated with origin

# Test from specific location (using proxy or VPN)
curl -I https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/jobs/
# x-amz-cf-pop: BOM52-C1                      ← Mumbai edge location

# Test compression
curl -I -H "Accept-Encoding: gzip, br" https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/static/js/app.js
# content-encoding: br                         ← Brotli compression
# vary: Accept-Encoding                        ← Cached separately per encoding

# Debug with verbose output
curl -v https://d1234abcd.cloudfront.net/api/jobs/search/?q=python
# Shows full request/response cycle, TLS handshake, headers
Python Script: Comprehensive CloudFront Diagnostics
# cloudfront_diagnostics.py
import requests
import json
from collections import Counter

def diagnose_cloudfront_distribution(domain, paths):
    """
    Test multiple paths and analyze CloudFront behavior
    
    Args:
        domain: CloudFront domain (d1234abcd.cloudfront.net)
        paths: List of paths to test
    """
    results = {
        'cache_hits': 0,
        'cache_misses': 0,
        'errors': [],
        'edge_locations': [],
        'response_times': []
    }
    
    print(f"\n🔍 Diagnosing CloudFront Distribution: {domain}")
    print("=" * 70)
    
    for path in paths:
        url = f'https://{domain}{path}'
        
        try:
            # Test 1: First request (likely cache miss)
            response1 = requests.get(url, headers={'Cache-Control': 'no-cache'})
            time1 = response1.elapsed.total_seconds()
            
            # Test 2: Second request (should be cache hit)
            response2 = requests.get(url)
            time2 = response2.elapsed.total_seconds()
            
            # Analyze headers
            x_cache = response2.headers.get('X-Cache', 'Unknown')
            pop = response2.headers.get('X-Amz-Cf-Pop', 'Unknown')
            age = response2.headers.get('Age', '0')
            
            # Track metrics
            if 'Hit' in x_cache:
                results['cache_hits'] += 1
            else:
                results['cache_misses'] += 1
            
            results['edge_locations'].append(pop)
            results['response_times'].append((path, time1, time2))
            
            # Display results
            cache_icon = "✅" if "Hit" in x_cache else "❌"
            print(f"\n{cache_icon} {path}")
            print(f"   Status: {response2.status_code}")
            print(f"   Cache: {x_cache}")
            print(f"   Edge: {pop}")
            print(f"   Age: {age}s")
            print(f"   Time: {time1:.3f}s (miss) → {time2:.3f}s (hit)")
            print(f"   Speedup: {(time1/time2):.1f}x faster")
            
        except Exception as e:
            results['errors'].append((path, str(e)))
            print(f"\n❌ {path}")
            print(f"   Error: {e}")
    
    # Summary
    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
    print("📊 SUMMARY")
    print("=" * 70)
    
    total = results['cache_hits'] + results['cache_misses']
    hit_ratio = (results['cache_hits'] / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0
    
    print(f"Cache Hit Ratio: {hit_ratio:.1f}% ({results['cache_hits']}/{total})")
    
    if hit_ratio >= 80:
        print("✅ GOOD - Cache performing well")
    elif hit_ratio >= 60:
        print("⚠️  FAIR - Consider increasing TTL")
    else:
        print("❌ POOR - Review cache configuration")
    
    # Edge location distribution
    pop_counts = Counter(results['edge_locations'])
    print(f"\nEdge Locations: {dict(pop_counts)}")
    
    # Performance summary
    avg_miss = sum(t[1] for t in results['response_times']) / len(results['response_times'])
    avg_hit = sum(t[2] for t in results['response_times']) / len(results['response_times'])
    print(f"\nAverage Response Time:")
    print(f"  Cache Miss: {avg_miss:.3f}s")
    print(f"  Cache Hit:  {avg_hit:.3f}s")
    print(f"  Speedup:    {(avg_miss/avg_hit):.1f}x")
    
    if results['errors']:
        print(f"\n❌ Errors: {len(results['errors'])}")
        for path, error in results['errors']:
            print(f"   {path}: {error}")

# Usage
diagnose_cloudfront_distribution(
    domain='d1234abcd.cloudfront.net',
    paths=[
        '/static/css/style.css',
        '/static/js/app.js',
        '/media/logos/company-123.jpg',
        '/jobs/',
        '/api/jobs/search/?q=python',
        '/'
    ]
)

# Output:
# 🔍 Diagnosing CloudFront Distribution: d1234abcd.cloudfront.net
# ======================================================================
# 
# ✅ /static/css/style.css
#    Status: 200
#    Cache: Hit from cloudfront
#    Edge: IAD89-P3
#    Age: 3456s
#    Time: 0.245s (miss) → 0.008s (hit)
#    Speedup: 30.6x faster
# 
# ✅ /static/js/app.js
#    Status: 200
#    Cache: Hit from cloudfront
#    Edge: IAD89-P3
#    Age: 2341s
#    Time: 0.198s (miss) → 0.006s (hit)
#    Speedup: 33.0x faster
# ...
# ======================================================================
# 📊 SUMMARY
# ======================================================================
# Cache Hit Ratio: 83.3% (5/6)
# ✅ GOOD - Cache performing well
# 
# Edge Locations: {'IAD89-P3': 6}
# 
# Average Response Time:
#   Cache Miss: 0.234s
#   Cache Hit:  0.009s
#   Speedup:    26.0x

10.3 Cache Debugging

🔍 Understanding X-Cache Header:
  • Hit from cloudfront: Served from edge cache (fastest, <10ms)
  • RefreshHit from cloudfront: Revalidated with origin (304 Not Modified)
  • Miss from cloudfront: Fetched from origin (first request or cache expired)
  • Error from cloudfront: Origin error, serving stale content if available

10.4 Frequently Asked Questions

❓ CloudFront FAQs for anihpj

Q: Why are my Django static files returning 403 Forbidden?
A: Check OAI configuration. Ensure S3 bucket policy allows CloudFront OAI to read objects. Also verify objects are not encrypted with KMS keys that CloudFront can't access.

Q: How long does it take for CloudFront changes to propagate?
A: Distribution changes take 5-15 minutes to deploy globally. Invalidations also take 5-15 minutes. For faster updates, use versioned URLs.

Q: Why is my cache hit ratio low (<70%)?
A: Common causes: too many unique query strings, short TTL, cookies forwarding too broadly, unique headers per request. Enable Origin Shield and increase TTL.

Q: Can I use CloudFront with Django's CSRF protection?
A: Yes! Forward the csrftoken cookie in cache behaviors for POST requests. For GET requests (read-only), you can cache without CSRF concerns.

Q: How do I invalidate cache after Django deployment?
A: Use aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths "/*". Better: use versioned URLs like style.v2.css to avoid invalidation costs.

Q: Why is my ACM certificate not working with CloudFront?
A: ACM certificates for CloudFront MUST be in us-east-1 region. If certificate is in another region, create a new one in us-east-1.

Q: What's the difference between TTL in CloudFront vs origin Cache-Control?
A: CloudFront respects origin Cache-Control: max-age headers unless you override with MinTTL/MaxTTL. CloudFront's DefaultTTL is used when origin doesn't send Cache-Control.

Q: How do I handle dynamic content with user-specific data?
A: Don't cache authenticated pages (TTL=0). For personalized content, use Lambda@Edge to inject user data after caching base HTML.

Q: Can CloudFront compress my Django responses?
A: Yes! Enable Compress: true in cache behaviors. CloudFront automatically compresses HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, XML with gzip/brotli (saves 70-85% bandwidth).

Q: How much does CloudFront cost for anihpj?
A: For 100-500GB/month: $10-60. Includes 1TB FREE tier for 12 months. Use Origin Shield and compression to reduce costs 30-50%.

⌨️ 11. API & CLI Reference ▼

Complete API and CLI reference for CloudFront automation.

11.1 AWS CLI Commands

Essential CloudFront CLI Commands
# List all distributions
aws cloudfront list-distributions \
    --query 'DistributionList.Items[*].[Id,DomainName,Comment,Status]' \
    --output table

# Get distribution details
aws cloudfront get-distribution --id E1234ABCD5678

# Get distribution configuration (for updates)
aws cloudfront get-distribution-config --id E1234ABCD5678

# Create invalidation
aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
    --distribution-id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --paths "/*" "/static/*" "/media/*"

# List invalidations
aws cloudfront list-invalidations --distribution-id E1234ABCD5678

# Get invalidation status
aws cloudfront get-invalidation \
    --distribution-id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --id I2ABCDEFG12345

# Update distribution (enable logging)
aws cloudfront get-distribution-config --id E1234ABCD5678 > config.json
# Edit config.json
aws cloudfront update-distribution \
    --id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --if-match  \
    --distribution-config file://config.json

# Disable distribution (before deletion)
aws cloudfront get-distribution-config --id E1234ABCD5678 > config.json
# Set "Enabled": false in config.json
aws cloudfront update-distribution \
    --id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --if-match  \
    --distribution-config file://config.json

# Delete distribution (must be disabled first)
aws cloudfront delete-distribution \
    --id E1234ABCD5678 \
    --if-match 

# List CloudFront Functions
aws cloudfront list-functions

# Create CloudFront Function
aws cloudfront create-function \
    --name anihpj-security-headers \
    --function-config Comment="Add security headers",Runtime=cloudfront-js-1.0 \
    --function-code fileb://function.js

# Test CloudFront Function
aws cloudfront test-function \
    --name anihpj-security-headers \
    --if-match  \
    --event-object file://test-event.json

11.2 Python/Boto3 SDK

Complete CloudFront Management with Boto3
# cloudfront_manager.py - Complete Boto3 wrapper for anihpj
import boto3
import time
import json

class CloudFrontManager:
    """Manage CloudFront distributions for anihpj"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.client = boto3.client('cloudfront')
    
    def create_distribution(self, origin_domain, aliases=None, certificate_arn=None):
        """
        Create a new CloudFront distribution
        
        Args:
            origin_domain: ALB or S3 domain name
            aliases: List of custom domains (e.g., ['www.anihpj.com'])
            certificate_arn: ACM certificate ARN (us-east-1)
        
        Returns:
            Distribution ID
        """
        config = {
            'CallerReference': f'anihpj-{int(time.time())}',
            'Comment': f'anihpj distribution for {origin_domain}',
            'Enabled': True,
            'Origins': {
                'Quantity': 1,
                'Items': [{
                    'Id': 'Origin-1',
                    'DomainName': origin_domain,
                    'CustomOriginConfig': {
                        'HTTPPort': 80,
                        'HTTPSPort': 443,
                        'OriginProtocolPolicy': 'https-only',
                        'OriginSslProtocols': {
                            'Quantity': 1,
                            'Items': ['TLSv1.2']
                        },
                        'OriginReadTimeout': 30,
                        'OriginKeepaliveTimeout': 5
                    },
                    'CustomHeaders': {
                        'Quantity': 1,
                        'Items': [{
                            'HeaderName': 'X-Origin-Verify',
                            'HeaderValue': 'anihpj-secure-token-12345'
                        }]
                    }
                }]
            },
            'DefaultCacheBehavior': {
                'TargetOriginId': 'Origin-1',
                'ViewerProtocolPolicy': 'redirect-to-https',
                'AllowedMethods': {
                    'Quantity': 7,
                    'Items': ['GET', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS', 'PUT', 'POST', 'PATCH', 'DELETE'],
                    'CachedMethods': {'Quantity': 2, 'Items': ['GET', 'HEAD']}
                },
                'Compress': True,
                'MinTTL': 0,
                'DefaultTTL': 300,
                'MaxTTL': 3600,
                'ForwardedValues': {
                    'QueryString': True,
                    'Cookies': {'Forward': 'whitelist', 'WhitelistedNames': {
                        'Quantity': 2,
                        'Items': ['sessionid', 'csrftoken']
                    }},
                    'Headers': {'Quantity': 2, 'Items': ['Host', 'CloudFront-Viewer-Country']}
                },
                'TrustedSigners': {'Enabled': False, 'Quantity': 0}
            }
        }
        
        # Add custom domain and SSL certificate
        if aliases and certificate_arn:
            config['Aliases'] = {'Quantity': len(aliases), 'Items': aliases}
            config['ViewerCertificate'] = {
                'ACMCertificateArn': certificate_arn,
                'SSLSupportMethod': 'sni-only',
                'MinimumProtocolVersion': 'TLSv1.2_2021'
            }
        else:
            config['ViewerCertificate'] = {
                'CloudFrontDefaultCertificate': True
            }
        
        response = self.client.create_distribution(DistributionConfig=config)
        distribution_id = response['Distribution']['Id']
        domain_name = response['Distribution']['DomainName']
        
        print(f"✅ Distribution created: {distribution_id}")
        print(f"   Domain: {domain_name}")
        print(f"   Status: {response['Distribution']['Status']}")
        
        return distribution_id
    
    def create_invalidation(self, distribution_id, paths):
        """
        Invalidate CloudFront cache
        
        Args:
            distribution_id: CloudFront distribution ID
            paths: List of paths to invalidate (e.g., ['/*', '/static/*'])
        
        Returns:
            Invalidation ID
        """
        response = self.client.create_invalidation(
            DistributionId=distribution_id,
            InvalidationBatch={
                'Paths': {'Quantity': len(paths), 'Items': paths},
                'CallerReference': f'anihpj-{int(time.time())}'
            }
        )
        
        invalidation_id = response['Invalidation']['Id']
        status = response['Invalidation']['Status']
        
        print(f"✅ Invalidation created: {invalidation_id}")
        print(f"   Status: {status}")
        print(f"   Paths: {paths}")
        
        return invalidation_id
    
    def wait_for_invalidation(self, distribution_id, invalidation_id, timeout=900):
        """Wait for invalidation to complete (max 15 minutes)"""
        start_time = time.time()
        
        while time.time() - start_time < timeout:
            response = self.client.get_invalidation(
                DistributionId=distribution_id,
                Id=invalidation_id
            )
            
            status = response['Invalidation']['Status']
            print(f"Invalidation status: {status}")
            
            if status == 'Completed':
                print("✅ Invalidation completed!")
                return True
            
            time.sleep(10)  # Check every 10 seconds
        
        print("❌ Invalidation timeout")
        return False
    
    def get_distribution_metrics(self, distribution_id, days=7):
        """Get CloudWatch metrics for distribution"""
        cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch')
        
        end_time = datetime.utcnow()
        start_time = end_time - timedelta(days=days)
        
        metrics = {}
        
        for metric_name in ['Requests', 'BytesDownloaded', 'CacheHitRate', '4xxErrorRate', '5xxErrorRate']:
            response = cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
                Namespace='AWS/CloudFront',
                MetricName=metric_name,
                Dimensions=[{'Name': 'DistributionId', 'Value': distribution_id}],
                StartTime=start_time,
                EndTime=end_time,
                Period=86400,
                Statistics=['Sum', 'Average']
            )
            
            metrics[metric_name] = response['Datapoints']
        
        return metrics
    
    def enable_logging(self, distribution_id, bucket_name, prefix='cloudfront-logs/'):
        """Enable access logging for distribution"""
        # Get current config
        response = self.client.get_distribution_config(Id=distribution_id)
        config = response['DistributionConfig']
        etag = response['ETag']
        
        # Enable logging
        config['Logging'] = {
            'Enabled': True,
            'IncludeCookies': False,
            'Bucket': f'{bucket_name}.s3.amazonaws.com',
            'Prefix': prefix
        }
        
        # Update distribution
        self.client.update_distribution(
            Id=distribution_id,
            DistributionConfig=config,
            IfMatch=etag
        )
        
        print(f"✅ Logging enabled: s3://{bucket_name}/{prefix}")

# Usage
manager = CloudFrontManager()

# Create distribution
dist_id = manager.create_distribution(
    origin_domain='anihpj.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',
    aliases=['www.anihpj.com', 'anihpj.com'],
    certificate_arn='arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123'
)

# Invalidate cache after deployment
inv_id = manager.create_invalidation(dist_id, ['/static/*', '/media/*', '/'])
manager.wait_for_invalidation(dist_id, inv_id)

# Enable logging
manager.enable_logging(dist_id, 'anihpj-logs', prefix='cloudfront/')

# Get metrics
metrics = manager.get_distribution_metrics(dist_id, days=7)
print(f"📊 Metrics: {metrics}")

11.3 Infrastructure as Code (CloudFormation)

CloudFormation Template for anihpj
# cloudfront-stack.yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'CloudFront distribution for anihpj Django app'

Parameters:
  OriginDomainName:
    Type: String
    Description: 'ALB domain name'
    Default: 'anihpj.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com'
  
  CustomDomain:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Custom domain (e.g., www.anihpj.com)'
    Default: 'www.anihpj.com'
  
  ACMCertificateArn:
    Type: String
    Description: 'ACM certificate ARN (us-east-1)'

Resources:
  CloudFrontDistribution:
    Type: AWS::CloudFront::Distribution
    Properties:
      DistributionConfig:
        Comment: 'anihpj Django application CDN'
        Enabled: true
        HttpVersion: http2
        PriceClass: PriceClass_All
        
        Origins:
          - Id: ALB-Django
            DomainName: !Ref OriginDomainName
            CustomOriginConfig:
              HTTPPort: 80
              HTTPSPort: 443
              OriginProtocolPolicy: https-only
              OriginSSLProtocols:
                - TLSv1.2
              OriginReadTimeout: 30
              OriginKeepaliveTimeout: 5
            OriginCustomHeaders:
              - HeaderName: X-Origin-Verify
                HeaderValue: !Sub '{{resolve:secretsmanager:anihpj/cloudfront:SecretString:verify_token}}'
            OriginShield:
              Enabled: true
              OriginShieldRegion: us-east-1
        
        DefaultCacheBehavior:
          TargetOriginId: ALB-Django
          ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https
          AllowedMethods:
            - GET
            - HEAD
            - OPTIONS
            - PUT
            - POST
            - PATCH
            - DELETE
          CachedMethods:
            - GET
            - HEAD
          Compress: true
          MinTTL: 0
          DefaultTTL: 300
          MaxTTL: 3600
          ForwardedValues:
            QueryString: true
            Cookies:
              Forward: whitelist
              WhitelistedNames:
                - sessionid
                - csrftoken
            Headers:
              - Host
              - CloudFront-Viewer-Country
              - Accept-Language
        
        CacheBehaviors:
          - PathPattern: '/static/*'
            TargetOriginId: ALB-Django
            ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https
            AllowedMethods:
              - GET
              - HEAD
            Compress: true
            MinTTL: 86400
            DefaultTTL: 2592000
            MaxTTL: 31536000
            ForwardedValues:
              QueryString: false
              Cookies:
                Forward: none
          
          - PathPattern: '/media/*'
            TargetOriginId: ALB-Django
            ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https
            AllowedMethods:
              - GET
              - HEAD
            Compress: false
            MinTTL: 3600
            DefaultTTL: 86400
            MaxTTL: 2592000
            ForwardedValues:
              QueryString: false
              Cookies:
                Forward: none
          
          - PathPattern: '/api/*'
            TargetOriginId: ALB-Django
            ViewerProtocolPolicy: https-only
            AllowedMethods:
              - GET
              - HEAD
              - OPTIONS
              - PUT
              - POST
              - PATCH
              - DELETE
            Compress: true
            MinTTL: 0
            DefaultTTL: 0
            MaxTTL: 30
            ForwardedValues:
              QueryString: true
              Cookies:
                Forward: all
              Headers:
                - '*'
        
        Aliases:
          - !Ref CustomDomain
        
        ViewerCertificate:
          AcmCertificateArn: !Ref ACMCertificateArn
          SslSupportMethod: sni-only
          MinimumProtocolVersion: TLSv1.2_2021
        
        Logging:
          Bucket: !GetAtt LogsBucket.DomainName
          IncludeCookies: false
          Prefix: 'cloudfront/'
        
        CustomErrorResponses:
          - ErrorCode: 404
            ResponseCode: 404
            ResponsePagePath: /404.html
            ErrorCachingMinTTL: 300
          - ErrorCode: 500
            ResponseCode: 500
            ResponsePagePath: /500.html
            ErrorCachingMinTTL: 0

  LogsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub 'anihpj-cloudfront-logs-${AWS::AccountId}'
      AccessControl: Private
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      LifecycleConfiguration:
        Rules:
          - Id: DeleteOldLogs
            Status: Enabled
            ExpirationInDays: 90

Outputs:
  DistributionId:
    Description: 'CloudFront Distribution ID'
    Value: !Ref CloudFrontDistribution
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-DistributionId'
  
  DistributionDomain:
    Description: 'CloudFront Domain Name'
    Value: !GetAtt CloudFrontDistribution.DomainName
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-DomainName'
  
  LogsBucket:
    Description: 'S3 bucket for CloudFront logs'
    Value: !Ref LogsBucket
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-LogsBucket'
Deploy CloudFormation Stack
# Deploy stack
aws cloudformation create-stack \
    --stack-name anihpj-cloudfront \
    --template-body file://cloudfront-stack.yaml \
    --parameters \
        ParameterKey=OriginDomainName,ParameterValue=anihpj.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com \
        ParameterKey=CustomDomain,ParameterValue=www.anihpj.com \
        ParameterKey=ACMCertificateArn,ParameterValue=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123 \
    --capabilities CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM

# Wait for stack creation
aws cloudformation wait stack-create-complete \
    --stack-name anihpj-cloudfront

# Get outputs
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name anihpj-cloudfront \
    --query 'Stacks[0].Outputs' \
    --output table

# Update stack
aws cloudformation update-stack \
    --stack-name anihpj-cloudfront \
    --template-body file://cloudfront-stack.yaml \
    --parameters \
        ParameterKey=OriginDomainName,UsePreviousValue=true \
        ParameterKey=CustomDomain,UsePreviousValue=true \
        ParameterKey=ACMCertificateArn,UsePreviousValue=true

# Delete stack
aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name anihpj-cloudfront

11.4 Terraform Configuration

Terraform: Complete CloudFront Setup
# main.tf - anihpj CloudFront with Terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
  }
}

provider "aws" {
  region = "us-east-1"
}

# CloudFront distribution
resource "aws_cloudfront_distribution" "anihpj" {
  comment             = "anihpj Django application CDN"
  enabled             = true
  is_ipv6_enabled     = true
  http_version        = "http2and3"
  price_class         = "PriceClass_All"
  aliases             = ["www.anihpj.com", "anihpj.com"]
  
  origin {
    domain_name = var.alb_domain_name
    origin_id   = "ALB-Django"
    
    custom_origin_config {
      http_port              = 80
      https_port             = 443
      origin_protocol_policy = "https-only"
      origin_ssl_protocols   = ["TLSv1.2"]
      origin_read_timeout    = 30
      origin_keepalive_timeout = 5
    }
    
    custom_header {
      name  = "X-Origin-Verify"
      value = var.origin_verify_secret
    }
    
    origin_shield {
      enabled              = true
      origin_shield_region = "us-east-1"
    }
  }
  
  default_cache_behavior {
    target_origin_id       = "ALB-Django"
    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
    allowed_methods        = ["DELETE", "GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "PATCH", "POST", "PUT"]
    cached_methods         = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    compress               = true
    min_ttl                = 0
    default_ttl            = 300
    max_ttl                = 3600
    
    forwarded_values {
      query_string = true
      
      cookies {
        forward           = "whitelist"
        whitelisted_names = ["sessionid", "csrftoken"]
      }
      
      headers = ["Host", "CloudFront-Viewer-Country", "Accept-Language"]
    }
  }
  
  # Static assets
  ordered_cache_behavior {
    path_pattern           = "/static/*"
    target_origin_id       = "ALB-Django"
    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
    allowed_methods        = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    cached_methods         = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    compress               = true
    min_ttl                = 86400
    default_ttl            = 2592000
    max_ttl                = 31536000
    
    forwarded_values {
      query_string = false
      
      cookies {
        forward = "none"
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Media files
  ordered_cache_behavior {
    path_pattern           = "/media/*"
    target_origin_id       = "ALB-Django"
    viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
    allowed_methods        = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    cached_methods         = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    compress               = false
    min_ttl                = 3600
    default_ttl            = 86400
    max_ttl                = 2592000
    
    forwarded_values {
      query_string = false
      
      cookies {
        forward = "none"
      }
    }
  }
  
  # API endpoints
  ordered_cache_behavior {
    path_pattern           = "/api/*"
    target_origin_id       = "ALB-Django"
    viewer_protocol_policy = "https-only"
    allowed_methods        = ["DELETE", "GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "PATCH", "POST", "PUT"]
    cached_methods         = ["GET", "HEAD"]
    compress               = true
    min_ttl                = 0
    default_ttl            = 0
    max_ttl                = 30
    
    forwarded_values {
      query_string = true
      
      cookies {
        forward = "all"
      }
      
      headers = ["*"]
    }
  }
  
  viewer_certificate {
    acm_certificate_arn      = var.acm_certificate_arn
    ssl_support_method       = "sni-only"
    minimum_protocol_version = "TLSv1.2_2021"
  }
  
  custom_error_response {
    error_code         = 404
    response_code      = 404
    response_page_path = "/404.html"
    error_caching_min_ttl = 300
  }
  
  custom_error_response {
    error_code         = 500
    response_code      = 500
    response_page_path = "/500.html"
    error_caching_min_ttl = 0
  }
  
  logging_config {
    include_cookies = false
    bucket          = aws_s3_bucket.logs.bucket_domain_name
    prefix          = "cloudfront/"
  }
  
  restrictions {
    geo_restriction {
      restriction_type = "none"
    }
  }
  
  tags = {
    Name        = "anihpj-cloudfront"
    Environment = "production"
    Project     = "anihpj"
  }
}

# S3 bucket for logs
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "logs" {
  bucket = "anihpj-cloudfront-logs-${data.aws_caller_identity.current.account_id}"
  
  tags = {
    Name    = "anihpj-cloudfront-logs"
    Project = "anihpj"
  }
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration" "logs" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.logs.id
  
  rule {
    id     = "delete-old-logs"
    status = "Enabled"
    
    expiration {
      days = 90
    }
  }
}

# Route 53 record
resource "aws_route53_record" "anihpj" {
  zone_id = var.route53_zone_id
  name    = "www.anihpj.com"
  type    = "A"
  
  alias {
    name                   = aws_cloudfront_distribution.anihpj.domain_name
    zone_id                = aws_cloudfront_distribution.anihpj.hosted_zone_id
    evaluate_target_health = false
  }
}

# Outputs
output "distribution_id" {
  description = "CloudFront distribution ID"
  value       = aws_cloudfront_distribution.anihpj.id
}

output "distribution_domain" {
  description = "CloudFront domain name"
  value       = aws_cloudfront_distribution.anihpj.domain_name
}

output "distribution_arn" {
  description = "CloudFront distribution ARN"
  value       = aws_cloudfront_distribution.anihpj.arn
}

# Variables
variable "alb_domain_name" {
  description = "ALB domain name"
  type        = string
}

variable "acm_certificate_arn" {
  description = "ACM certificate ARN (us-east-1)"
  type        = string
}

variable "origin_verify_secret" {
  description = "Secret for X-Origin-Verify header"
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true
}

variable "route53_zone_id" {
  description = "Route 53 hosted zone ID"
  type        = string
}

data "aws_caller_identity" "current" {}

🔗 12. AWS Service Integration ▼

Integrate CloudFront with other AWS services for anihpj.

🪣 Amazon S3

Use Case: Static assets, media files

  • Origin Access Identity (OAI)
  • Block public S3 access
  • S3 Transfer Acceleration
  • Cross-region replication
⚖️ ALB/ELB

Use Case: Django application origin

  • Custom origin headers
  • Health checks integration
  • Origin failover groups
  • Sticky sessions via cookies
🌐 Route 53

Use Case: DNS management

  • ALIAS records (no cost)
  • Health-based routing
  • Geolocation routing
  • Weighted routing policies
🔒 AWS WAF

Use Case: Security layer

  • SQL injection protection
  • XSS attack prevention
  • Rate limiting (DDoS)
  • IP blocking/whitelisting
📊 CloudWatch

Use Case: Monitoring & alerts

  • Cache hit ratio metrics
  • Error rate alarms
  • Request count tracking
  • Custom dashboard
λ Lambda@Edge

Use Case: Edge computing

  • Request/response manipulation
  • A/B testing logic
  • Authentication at edge
  • Dynamic content generation

12.1 S3 Integration

Complete S3 + CloudFront Setup
# Step 1: Create S3 bucket
aws s3api create-bucket \
    --bucket anihpj-static-assets \
    --region us-east-1

# Step 2: Block all public access
aws s3api put-public-access-block \
    --bucket anihpj-static-assets \
    --public-access-block-configuration \
        BlockPublicAcls=true,\
        IgnorePublicAcls=true,\
        BlockPublicPolicy=true,\
        RestrictPublicBuckets=true

# Step 3: Upload static files with Cache-Control
aws s3 sync ./static/ s3://anihpj-static-assets/static/ \
    --cache-control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" \
    --content-type "text/css" \
    --exclude "*" --include "*.css"

aws s3 sync ./static/ s3://anihpj-static-assets/static/ \
    --cache-control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" \
    --content-type "application/javascript" \
    --exclude "*" --include "*.js"

# Step 4: Enable S3 Transfer Acceleration (optional)
aws s3api put-bucket-accelerate-configuration \
    --bucket anihpj-static-assets \
    --accelerate-configuration Status=Enabled

12.2 ALB/ELB Integration

CloudFront with Application Load Balancer (ALB)
# Step 1: Configure ALB as CloudFront origin
aws cloudfront create-distribution \
    --origin-domain-name anihpj-alb-123456.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com \
    --distribution-config '{
        "CallerReference": "anihpj-alb-distribution-'$(date +%s)'",
        "Comment": "CloudFront distribution for anihpj Django app via ALB",
        "Enabled": true,
        "Origins": {
            "Quantity": 1,
            "Items": [
                {
                    "Id": "anihpj-alb-origin",
                    "DomainName": "anihpj-alb-123456.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
                    "CustomOriginConfig": {
                        "HTTPPort": 80,
                        "HTTPSPort": 443,
                        "OriginProtocolPolicy": "https-only",
                        "OriginSslProtocols": {
                            "Quantity": 3,
                            "Items": ["TLSv1.2", "TLSv1.3"]
                        },
                        "OriginReadTimeout": 30,
                        "OriginKeepaliveTimeout": 5
                    },
                    "CustomHeaders": {
                        "Quantity": 1,
                        "Items": [
                            {
                                "HeaderName": "X-Custom-Origin-Header",
                                "HeaderValue": "CloudFrontRequest"
                            }
                        ]
                    }
                }
            ]
        },
        "DefaultCacheBehavior": {
            "TargetOriginId": "anihpj-alb-origin",
            "ViewerProtocolPolicy": "redirect-to-https",
            "AllowedMethods": {
                "Quantity": 7,
                "Items": ["GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "PUT", "POST", "PATCH", "DELETE"],
                "CachedMethods": {
                    "Quantity": 2,
                    "Items": ["GET", "HEAD"]
                }
            },
            "ForwardedValues": {
                "QueryString": true,
                "Cookies": {
                    "Forward": "whitelist",
                    "WhitelistedNames": {
                        "Quantity": 2,
                        "Items": ["sessionid", "csrftoken"]
                    }
                },
                "Headers": {
                    "Quantity": 3,
                    "Items": ["Host", "User-Agent", "CloudFront-Viewer-Country"]
                }
            },
            "MinTTL": 0,
            "DefaultTTL": 0,
            "MaxTTL": 31536000,
            "Compress": true
        }
    }'
🎯 Key Configuration Points for ALB/ELB Origins
Setting Recommendation Reason
Origin Protocol Policy https-only Secure connection between CloudFront and ALB
Origin Read Timeout 30-60 seconds Allow time for dynamic content generation
Forward Cookies Whitelist only required Improve cache hit ratio by limiting cookie forwarding
Forward Headers Host, User-Agent, Custom Required for application routing and behavior
Query String Forwarding true (forward all) Django apps often rely on query parameters
Allowed HTTP Methods GET, HEAD, OPTIONS, PUT, POST, PATCH, DELETE Support full REST API operations
Origin Custom Headers Add custom validation header Verify requests come from CloudFront, not direct ALB access
TTL for Dynamic Content MinTTL=0, DefaultTTL=0 Let origin control caching with Cache-Control headers
Django Settings for CloudFront + ALB
# anihpj/settings.py - Configure for CloudFront + ALB

# Allow CloudFront domain and custom domain
ALLOWED_HOSTS = [
    'd1234abcd.cloudfront.net',
    'www.anihpj.com',
    'anihpj.com',
    'anihpj-alb-123456.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com',  # Direct ALB access
]

# Trust CloudFront headers
SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER = ('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO', 'https')

# Get real client IP from CloudFront
USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST = True
USE_X_FORWARDED_PORT = True

# Custom middleware to validate CloudFront origin header
MIDDLEWARE = [
    'django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware',
    'anihpj.middleware.CloudFrontOriginMiddleware',  # Custom middleware
    'django.contrib.sessions.middleware.SessionMiddleware',
    # ... other middleware
]

# Cache configuration for dynamic content
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': 'redis://anihpj-redis:6379/1',
    }
}
Custom Middleware to Validate CloudFront Requests
# anihpj/middleware.py
import hashlib
import hmac
from django.conf import settings
from django.http import HttpResponseForbidden

class CloudFrontOriginMiddleware:
    """
    Validate that requests come through CloudFront, not directly to ALB.
    This prevents bypassing CloudFront's WAF and DDoS protection.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, get_response):
        self.get_response = get_response
        # Secret shared between CloudFront and Django
        self.origin_secret = settings.CLOUDFRONT_ORIGIN_SECRET
    
    def __call__(self, request):
        # Skip validation for health checks
        if request.path == '/health/':
            return self.get_response(request)
        
        # Check for custom CloudFront origin header
        origin_header = request.META.get('HTTP_X_CUSTOM_ORIGIN_HEADER')
        
        if origin_header != 'CloudFrontRequest':
            # Request didn't come through CloudFront
            return HttpResponseForbidden(
                "Direct access not allowed. Please use the proper domain."
            )
        
        # Add CloudFront metadata to request
        request.cloudfront_viewer_country = request.META.get(
            'HTTP_CLOUDFRONT_VIEWER_COUNTRY', 'Unknown'
        )
        request.cloudfront_is_mobile = request.META.get(
            'HTTP_CLOUDFRONT_IS_MOBILE_VIEWER', 'false'
        ) == 'true'
        
        response = self.get_response(request)
        
        # Set Cache-Control headers based on view
        if not response.has_header('Cache-Control'):
            # Default: no cache for dynamic content
            response['Cache-Control'] = 'private, no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate'
        
        return response

💡 Best Practice: Use Origin Shield between CloudFront and ALB to reduce load on your origin and improve cache hit ratio. Enable Origin Shield in the CloudFront region closest to your ALB.

12.3 AWS WAF Integration

Attach WAF to CloudFront
# Step 1: Create WAF Web ACL
aws wafv2 create-web-acl \
    --name anihpj-waf \
    --scope CLOUDFRONT \
    --region us-east-1 \
    --default-action Allow={} \
    --rules '[
        {
            "Name": "RateLimitRule",
            "Priority": 1,
            "Statement": {
                "RateBasedStatement": {
                    "Limit": 2000,
                    "AggregateKeyType": "IP"
                }
            },
            "Action": {"Block": {}},
            "VisibilityConfig": {
                "SampledRequestsEnabled": true,
                "CloudWatchMetricsEnabled": true,
                "MetricName": "RateLimitRule"
            }
        },
        {
            "Name": "SQLInjectionRule",
            "Priority": 2,
            "Statement": {
                "SqliMatchStatement": {
                    "FieldToMatch": {"QueryString": {}},
                    "TextTransformations": [{"Priority": 0, "Type": "URL_DECODE"}]
                }
            },
            "Action": {"Block": {}},
            "VisibilityConfig": {
                "SampledRequestsEnabled": true,
                "CloudWatchMetricsEnabled": true,
                "MetricName": "SQLInjectionRule"
            }
        }
    ]' \
    --visibility-config \
        SampledRequestsEnabled=true,\
        CloudWatchMetricsEnabled=true,\
        MetricName=anihpj-waf

# Step 2: Associate WAF with CloudFront
aws wafv2 associate-web-acl \
    --web-acl-arn arn:aws:wafv2:us-east-1:123456789012:global/webacl/anihpj-waf/a1b2c3d4 \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:cloudfront::123456789012:distribution/E1234ABCD5678

12.4 Route 53 Integration

Advanced Route 53 Setup
# Weighted routing: 90% CloudFront, 10% direct ALB (for testing)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z3ABCDEFG12345 \
    --change-batch '{
        "Changes": [
            {
                "Action": "CREATE",
                "ResourceRecordSet": {
                    "Name": "www.anihpj.com",
                    "Type": "A",
                    "SetIdentifier": "CloudFront-Primary",
                    "Weight": 90,
                    "AliasTarget": {
                        "HostedZoneId": "Z2FDTNDATAQYW2",
                        "DNSName": "d1234abcd.cloudfront.net",
                        "EvaluateTargetHealth": false
                    }
                }
            },
            {
                "Action": "CREATE",
                "ResourceRecordSet": {
                    "Name": "www.anihpj.com",
                    "Type": "A",
                    "SetIdentifier": "ALB-Direct",
                    "Weight": 10,
                    "AliasTarget": {
                        "HostedZoneId": "Z35SXDOTRQ7X7K",
                        "DNSName": "anihpj.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com",
                        "EvaluateTargetHealth": true
                    }
                }
            }
        ]
    }'

🔄 13. Migration Strategies ▼

Migrate to CloudFront from other CDNs or on-premises infrastructure.

13.1 Migrating from Other CDNs

📋 Migration Checklist
Step Action Status
1. Audit Current CDN Document cache rules, TTLs, custom headers, SSL certs □
2. Create CloudFront Distribution Match existing cache behaviors and settings □
3. Test CloudFront Use CloudFront domain (d1234.cloudfront.net) for testing □
4. SSL Certificate Request ACM certificate in us-east-1, validate DNS □
5. Gradual Cutover Route 10% traffic to CloudFront, monitor metrics □
6. Full Cutover Update DNS to point to CloudFront (A/CNAME record) □
7. Monitor Watch CloudWatch metrics for 48 hours, validate cache hit ratio □
8. Decommission Old CDN Cancel old CDN service after 30 days □
Gradual Migration Script
# Phase 1: Route 10% traffic to CloudFront (90% to Cloudflare)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z3ABCDEFG12345 \
    --change-batch '{
        "Changes": [{
            "Action": "UPSERT",
            "ResourceRecordSet": {
                "Name": "www.anihpj.com",
                "Type": "A",
                "SetIdentifier": "CloudFront-Testing",
                "Weight": 10,
                "AliasTarget": {
                    "HostedZoneId": "Z2FDTNDATAQYW2",
                    "DNSName": "d1234abcd.cloudfront.net",
                    "EvaluateTargetHealth": false
                }
            }
        }]
    }'

# Monitor for 24 hours, check metrics
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
    --namespace AWS/CloudFront \
    --metric-name CacheHitRate \
    --dimensions Name=DistributionId,Value=E1234ABCD5678 \
    --start-time $(date -u -d '24 hours ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S) \
    --end-time $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S) \
    --period 3600 \
    --statistics Average

# Phase 2: Increase to 50%
# Update Weight to 50 for CloudFront, 50 for Cloudflare

# Phase 3: Full cutover (100%)
aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
    --hosted-zone-id Z3ABCDEFG12345 \
    --change-batch '{
        "Changes": [{
            "Action": "UPSERT",
            "ResourceRecordSet": {
                "Name": "www.anihpj.com",
                "Type": "A",
                "AliasTarget": {
                    "HostedZoneId": "Z2FDTNDATAQYW2",
                    "DNSName": "d1234abcd.cloudfront.net",
                    "EvaluateTargetHealth": false
                }
            }
        }]
    }'

13.2 Migrating from On-Premises

🏢 On-Premises to CloudFront Migration:
  • Step 1: Deploy Django app to ALB (or keep on-prem as origin)
  • Step 2: Create CloudFront distribution pointing to on-prem IP/domain
  • Step 3: Test with CloudFront domain before DNS change
  • Step 4: Update DNS gradually (A/B test with Route 53 weighted routing)
  • Step 5: Monitor origin load reduction (50-70% with CloudFront)
  • Step 6: Consider migrating origin to AWS (EC2, ECS, Fargate)

🛡️ 14. Security & Compliance ▼

CloudFront compliance certifications and security features for anihpj.

14.1 Compliance Certifications

✅ PCI DSS Level 1

Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

  • Safe for payment processing
  • Field-level encryption
  • Secure credit card handling
✅ HIPAA Eligible

Health Insurance Portability Act

  • BAA available
  • PHI data protection
  • Medical records security
✅ SOC 1, 2, 3

Service Organization Control Reports

  • Third-party audited
  • Security controls verified
  • Operational excellence
✅ ISO 27001

Information Security Management

  • Global standard
  • Risk management
  • Data protection certified
✅ GDPR Compliant

EU Data Protection Regulation

  • EU data residency options
  • Data processing agreements
  • Right to be forgotten support
✅ FedRAMP

Federal Risk Authorization

  • U.S. government workloads
  • High security baseline
  • Continuous monitoring

14.2 Security Features Summary

🔒 CloudFront Security for anihpj:
  • ✅ DDoS Protection: AWS Shield Standard (FREE, automatic)
  • ✅ TLS 1.2+: Modern encryption, disable old protocols
  • ✅ Field-Level Encryption: Encrypt sensitive form data at edge
  • ✅ Geo Restriction: Block/allow countries for compliance
  • ✅ Signed URLs/Cookies: Time-limited access to premium content
  • ✅ WAF Integration: SQL injection, XSS, rate limiting protection
  • ✅ OAI for S3: Private S3 buckets, no public access
  • ✅ Custom Headers: Verify requests from CloudFront only
  • ✅ Access Logs: Full audit trail for security reviews
  • ✅ Real-Time Monitoring: CloudWatch metrics and alarms
💡 Recommended Security Setup for anihpj:
  1. Enable HTTPS-only (ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https)
  2. Use ACM certificates (FREE, auto-renewal)
  3. Attach AWS WAF (SQL injection, XSS, rate limiting)
  4. Enable Origin Shield ($10/month, reduces origin load 50-70%)
  5. Add X-Origin-Verify header to validate CloudFront requests in Django
  6. Enable access logging to S3 (security audits, compliance)
  7. Use signed URLs for premium content (resumes, documents)
  8. Add CloudFront Functions for security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options)
  9. Set up CloudWatch alarms (4xx/5xx error rates, cache hit ratio)
  10. Review access logs weekly for suspicious patterns

14.3 Data Privacy & Residency

CloudFront Edge Locations by Region:

  • North America: 100+ edge locations (US, Canada)
  • Europe: 90+ edge locations (EU, UK, Eastern Europe)
  • Asia Pacific: 110+ edge locations (Japan, Singapore, India, Australia)
  • South America: 15+ edge locations (Brazil, Argentina)
  • Middle East & Africa: 20+ edge locations (UAE, South Africa)

GDPR Compliance:

  • Use PriceClass_100 to limit to US/EU edge locations only
  • Data Processing Agreement (DPA) available from AWS
  • EU data residency options (origin in eu-west-1, eu-central-1)
  • Right to deletion: Implement cache invalidation API for user data
  • Access logs stored in S3 with encryption and lifecycle policies
